Вы можете отправить запрос в отношении ваших персональных данных по адресу: job@playrix.com.

Title: Data Modeler
Description:
Senior Data Modeler – Non-Financial Risk

Industry: Banking & Financial Services

Location: Brussels, Belgium (Hybrid)




About the Role

We are seeking an experienced Senior Data Modeler to help design and govern the data foundations supporting a large-scale Non-Financial Risk (NFR) environment. You will work closely with Risk, Compliance, Data Governance, Architecture, and Technology teams to translate complex business and risk concepts into scalable conceptual, logical, and physical data models.

This role focuses on building sustainable data structures that support reporting, governance, analytics, regulatory requirements, and future AI-driven initiatives.




Key Responsibilities

Data Modelling

Design and maintain conceptual, logical, and physical data models.
Develop and evolve canonical models for key Non-Financial Risk concepts, including:
Risks
Controls
Incidents
Issues
Action Plans
Key Risk Indicators (KRIs)
Business Activities
Ensure consistency across operational systems, data lakes, reporting platforms, and governance tools.
Maintain coherent data architectures and data assets over time.




Business & Risk Analysis

Collaborate with business stakeholders, risk experts, and IT teams.
Facilitate workshops to gather requirements and define data solutions.
Translate business terminology and risk management concepts into reusable data structures.
Build and maintain a common business vocabulary and data glossary.
Act as the bridge between business users and technical teams.




Data Governance

Support data governance initiatives including metadata, lineage, ownership, and traceability.
Ensure compliance with governance standards, quality requirements, and regulatory expectations.
Contribute to the management of critical data elements and risk indicators.
Create and maintain functional documentation and modelling standards.




Project & Delivery

Plan and monitor project activities.
Assess new tools, technologies, and methodologies.
Define modelling best practices and design patterns.
Support testing strategies and validate new developments and enhancements.




Required Skills & Experience

5+ years of experience in conceptual, logical, and physical data modelling.
Strong expertise in data management, metadata, master data, governance, and data security.
Solid understanding of data modelling methodologies and best practices.
Experience working with Agile delivery environments.
Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills.
Ability to bridge business and technical teams.




Preferred Experience

Experience within one or more Non-Financial Risk domains:
Operational Risk
Compliance Risk
IT Risk
Information Security Risk
Model Risk
Third-Party Risk
Fraud Risk
Experience with data governance platforms such as Collibra or similar.
Knowledge of risk and control frameworks, KRIs, incidents, and controls management.
Experience with data warehouses, data lakes, and cloud-based analytics environments.




Soft Skills

Analytical Thinking
Conceptual Thinking
Collaboration
Customer Orientation
Data-Driven Mindset
Strong Communication Skills
Proactive Problem Solving




Nice to Have

Background in Banking, Insurance, Risk Management, Compliance, or Regulatory Reporting.
Exposure to AI, advanced analytics, and enterprise data architecture initiatives.

Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
Job Title: Data Analytics Intern (Remote)

Company: Stealth (Confidential)




About the Role

We are hiring on behalf of a fast-growing Stealth Startup looking for a curious and detail-oriented Data Analytics Intern. This is an exciting opportunity to work closely with founding teams and gain hands-on experience in extracting insights, building dashboards, and driving data-backed decision-making in a high-impact environment.




Key Responsibilities

Collect, clean, and analyze structured and unstructured data
Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) to identify trends and patterns
Build and maintain dashboards and reports for business insights
Write efficient SQL queries to extract and manipulate data
Assist in defining KPIs and metrics for business performance
Collaborate with product, marketing, and business teams
Present insights in a clear and actionable manner




Required Qualifications

Currently pursuing or recently completed a degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or related field
Strong knowledge of Excel/Google Sheets
Basic proficiency in SQL
Familiarity with Python (Pandas, NumPy) or similar tools
Understanding of data visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, etc.)
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills




Preferred Qualifications

Experience with real-world datasets or analytics projects
Knowledge of business metrics and performance tracking
Exposure to A/B testing or statistical analysis
Familiarity with startup or fast-paced environments




Key Skills

Data Analysis & Interpretation
SQL & Data Querying
Dashboarding & Visualization
Critical Thinking
Communication & Storytelling with Data




What We’re Looking For

A data-driven thinker who can translate numbers into meaningful insights
Someone who thrives in a fast-paced, ambiguous startup environment
High ownership and willingness to learn quickly
Strong attention to detail




Location

Remote




Duration

Internship (3–6 months, with potential extension or full-time opportunity)




Compensation

Competitive stipend (based on experience and client budget)




Why Join?

Work directly with a stealth-mode startup solving real-world problems
Gain hands-on experience with live data and business decision-making
Opportunity to convert into a full-time role based on performance

Title: Data-analist
Description:
At Darwind, we believe data only matters when it leads to better decisions and real impact. We're looking for a Data Consultant who enjoys bringing clarity to complexity, someone who can turn raw information into insights that organisations can actually act on.

At Darwind, we don't fit consultants into projects, we look for the project that fits you. You'll join a community of Darwinders who value autonomy, ownership, and genuine professional growth, and who believe the best work experience should always be the next one.




What you will do

In this role, you support end-to-end data initiatives across business and technical teams. You will:

Collect, clean and transform data from various sources.
Build dashboards and visualisations using Power BI, Tableau or Excel.
Write and optimise SQL queries for reporting and analysis.
Support or develop data pipelines (ETL/ELT) and contribute to cloud-based data environments.
Model datasets and design efficient, scalable data structures.
Identify patterns, trends and insights that support business decisions.
Collaborate with analysts, IT teams and business stakeholders.
Improve data quality, documentation and data governance practices.
Present findings clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.




Who you are

We look beyond the CV. For us, motivation and curiosity matter just as much as the technical box-ticking, but to thrive in this role, you'll likely recognise yourself in most of the following:

A Master's degree.
At least 1 to 2 years of experience in data management or a similar field.
Strong knowledge of SQL and at least one analytics or visualisation tool (Power BI, Tableau or Excel).
Understanding of data modelling, data structures and relational databases.
The ability to translate complex data into clear business insights.
A proactive, accurate and curious mindset.
Experience in cloud environments is a plus.
Fluency in Dutch, with proficiency in French as an advantage.




What we offer

Competitive benefits package

Hospitalization insurance
Group insurance
Meal allowance
Your choice between a company car with EU fuel card or the federal mobility budget

Flexibility and work-life balance

Hybrid work possibilities
Flexible working hours
Projects that match your skills and ambitions, not the other way around

Learning and growth

Continuous coaching
Technical and soft-skills training
Room to grow across different domains and technologies

Culture

A human, transparent and collaborative environment
Real ownership and influence on the projects you contribute to
A team that lives its values, initiative, authenticity and open communication, every day




Ready for your next step?

If the traditional consultancy model never quite resonated with you, and you're looking for a place where people, impact and growth genuinely matter, let's talk.

Title: Data & Business Analyst
Description:
Who we are




Phinest is a Belgian Management Consulting firm providing tailor-made services for Bel20 and Bel20-like companies.

We help these leading companies deliver their key strategic projects across various sectors (Utilities, Pharmaceutical, Banking & Insurance, Retail, Transport, Public, Telecom).




We combine a human-sized organisation with large-scale impact. You are not just a consultant: you grow with us while making an impact for our clients.

At Phinest, you’ll find the right balance between professional growth, meaningful projects, and a collaborative environment.




Position: Business & Data Analyst




Your Mission:

Join a team at the forefront of the energy transition! As an Energy Business & Data Analyst, you will be the key link between business and IT teams, driving the design, development, and optimization.




Your Responsibilities:

Analyze business needs related to energy management and optimization.
Translate these needs into clear functional specifications for technical teams.
Collect, structure, and analyze data to identify performance drivers.
Collaborate with stakeholders (business, IT, engineering, data) to ensure project success.
Monitor performance indicators and propose continuous improvements.




Your Profile:

A unique mix of business insight and data/IT mindset.
Comfortable with data analysis, modeling, and reporting tools.
Strong ability to bridge technical and non-technical discussions.




What we offer:




Our focus is to develop a long-term collaboration with our talented consultants. Therefore we invest in our people as of day one to support them into a blossoming career within Phinest.

Our Talent Management Services department (a.k.a TMS) is designed and fully dedicated to the development and well-being of our employees.




We also offer:




A company culture focused on well-being
A wide range of trainings and career development opportunities (certification, practical training)
A collaborative, dynamic and fulfilling working atmosphere with a supportive and collaborative team
The opportunity to work with a fast-growing firm and talented people
A competitive remuneration package with fringe benefits
Hybrid workplace: combination of home office and on-site




Join us to make an impact together! Apply now!

Brussels, Belgium (Hybrid)

Long-Term Freelance Contract

Competitive Market Rate




We are currently looking for an experienced Data Engineer with strong Databricks expertise to join a long-term transformation programme for a leading organisation based in Brussels.

Fabric, data omzetten in inzichten voor de business.




Als Data Analyst werk je mee aan het centrale datamodel in de gold layer met DBT en gebruik je Power BI als semantische laag voor analyses en self-service inzichten. Zo draag je bij aan consistente datamodellen en semantische lagen waarop dashboards en rapporten gebouwd worden.
Je rapporteert aan de Teamlead Resource Pool Data en krijgt volop ruimteom je verder te ontwikkelen als data professional.




Jouw verantwoordelijkheden




Als Data Analyst werk je voornamelijk op de gold layer, waar je samen met data engineers data modelleert en beschikbaar maakt voor analysedoeleinden. Je gebruikt DBT om mee te werken aan het centrale datamodel en de semantische lagen, en zet Power BI in als semantische en consumptielaag voor rapportering en self‑service analytics.
Je beheert datamodellen en datasets die de basis vormen voor consistente rapporten en analyses binnen de organisatie, en helpt stakeholders om zelfstandig met betrouwbare data te werken.
Je werkt mee aan het integreren van data uit verschillende bronnen in een centraal datamodel.
Je draagt, onder begeleiding, bij aan datamodellering in de gold layer met behulp van DBT (zoals dimensionele modellen en businesslogica).
Je helpt bij het beheren en verder uitbouwen van semantische modellen bovenop de gold data.
Je staat in voor het aanbieden en onderhouden van herbruikbare Power BI-datasets voor verschillende stakeholders.
Je ondersteunt self-service analytics zodat businessgebruikers zelfstandig inzichten kunnen opbouwen op basis van gevalideerde data.
Je vertaalt businessvragen naar duidelijke KPI’s, measures en datadefinities binnen het Power BI-model.
Je werkt samen met data engineers om consistente definities en één versie van de waarheid te garanderen.
Je werkt nauw samen met data engineers om ervoor te zorgen dat brondata kwalitatief en tijdig beschikbaar is voor verdere modellering.







Wat wij verwachten

Must haves

Je beschikt over een bachelor- of masterdenkniveau in een data-, IT- of analytische richting (of bent laatstejaarsstudent).
Je hebt een sterke interesse in data, analytics en het werken met datamodellen.
Je hebt basiskennis van SQL.
Je hebt een eerste ervaring met of sterke interesse in Power BI, met focus op datasets en semantische modellen.
Je hebt interesse in datamodellering en bent gemotiveerd om te leren werken met DBT binnen de gold layer.
Je bent analytisch, gestructureerd en leergierig.
Je werkt graag samen en durft vragen te stellen.




Nice to haves

Je hebt stage- of eerste werkervaring in data of analytics.
Je hebt basisinzicht in dimensionele modellen (facts& dimensions).
Je hebt interesse in data governance, metadata en dataplatformen.
Je hebt ervaring met Agile/Scrum-methodieken.
Je hebt interesse in cloudomgevingen.

Title: 📊 Data Engineer
Description:
SFEIR BELGIUM - 📊 Data Engineer - Become an Augmented Expert

Brussels | Startup Spirit | AI-Native Group 🤖

Sharp Tech. Real Impact.




🔎 Who are we? 🔍

SFEIR is a technology innovation catalyst at the forefront of the AI revolution. We transform organizations by creating tangible, lasting impact — not by following trends, but by industrializing them alongside our clients.




Our belief: well-orchestrated agentic AI enables massive acceleration across delivery cycles. The organizations that will pull ahead won't be those that adopt AI the earliest, but those that industrialize it with method. That's exactly what we do through our Context Engineering approach, our partnership with Anthropic, and our GenAI platform RAISE.







Key figures:

950 consultants passionate about tech and augmented by AI
8 offices across France, Luxembourg, and Brussels
Anthropic Select Partner within the Services track of the Claude Partner Network since June 1st!
15 years of partnership with Google Cloud, Partner of the Year multiple times, and a premium partner ecosystem (AWS, Microsoft, Scaleway, S3NS, Databricks, Snowflake, Confluent, dbt…)
90+ active clients and 5,000+ people trained per year through SFEIR Institute







💫 What drives us? 💫

What all Sfeirians have in common is a passion for Tech.

Our culture is firmly oriented toward technological excellence, knowledge sharing, and our core values: kindness, diversity, excellence, freedom, and responsibility.




We are AI-Powered: we use RAISE (our internal tool) and Claude Code on a daily basis. Our Anthropic partnership gives you access to tools and certifications before anyone else.




Community spirit is in our DNA: speakers at local meetups, monthly internal presentations, a community blog, and IC career paths to grow in expertise without leaving the code.







💻 Your future role: Data & Innovation

In practice, you'll bring meaning to data and create real value — because without solid data, there's no performant AI.

✔ Data Engineering: Design and maintain modern, scalable pipelines (Spark, Kafka, etc.).

✔ AI-Ready: Build architectures enabling AI and GenAI value creation.

✔ Transformations: Work across ingestion, real-time processing, modeling, and governance.

✔ Build: Use Python or Scala to manipulate and enrich datasets on build projects.

✔ Data Platform: AWS, Azure, GCP

✔ French & English speaking







🇧🇪 The "Brussels Factor"

SFEIR Belgium offers a startup vibe backed by a large group's resources:

A team of 10+ devs where everyone knows each other
An Engineering Director (Renaud Hoyoux) who still codes alongside you
Stylish offices on Avenue Louise
A vibrant tech scene: internal talks, meetups, afterworks, and knowledge sharing
The AI Champions program to level up with AI







🎁 What we offer

Salary: Based on your real skills, validated at the PlayOffs — our technical recruitment process
Company car or mobility budget
Premium gear: MacBook Pro or Dell, your choice
Pluxee meal vouchers and eco-vouchers
European phone plan
Health insurance: DKV Hospi







🎯 Practical details

Location: at our clients' sites across the Brussels metropolitan area







🎮 What happens next

HR interview with Vanessa
Technical pair-programming tests (algorithm, language, platform)

Our process is a mutual discovery — you also get to decide whether we're the right fit for you. We assess your reasoning, your way of collaborating, and how you unblock situations — well beyond what a CV can tell.







🤩 Ready to build the future of data with us?

Want to architect next-level data platforms, with a team that gets you, in a company that respects your pace and your curiosity?




Let's go ✌🏻! Send us your CV or reach out via DM.







SFEIR collects your personal data in order to process your application. To learn more about how your personal data is managed and to exercise your rights, please refer to our Privacy Policy.

Title: Customer Data Analyst
Description:
Ben jij iemand die energie krijgt van klantendata en marketingimpact?

Werk je graag op het snijvlak van data, business en marketing en wil je écht verschil maken in campagnes?

Dan is deze tijdelijke opdracht als Customer Data Analyst bij onze retailklant iets voor jou.




Jouw uitdaging

Als Customer Data Analyst speel je een sleutelrol in het vertalen van klantendata naar concrete marketinginzichten. Je ondersteunt het marketingteam door data om te zetten in gerichte acties die campagnes sterker en efficiënter maken.

Je analyseert klant- en campagnedata en vertaalt deze naar heldere aanbevelingen
Je ondersteunt marketingcollega’s met zowel ad-hoc analyses als diepgaande inzichten
Je identificeert trends, opportuniteiten en optimalisaties in campagnes en doelgroepen
Je bouwt en verbetert dashboards en rapporteringen
Je zorgt voor betrouwbare en consistente data als basis voor beslissingen
Je werkt nauw samen met marketing, data- en IT-teams




Jouw profiel

Je combineert analytische skills met businessinzichten en een hands-on mindset:

Masterdiploma (economie, business, IT, …)
Min. 2 jaar ervaring met B2C klantendata, bij voorkeur in (food)retailomgeving. Kennis van GDPR-guidelines is een plus
Sterke ervaring met data (-analyse) en bijhorende progammeertalen zoals Python
Je kan complexe data vertalen naar duidelijke, actiegerichte inzichten
Je begrijpt hoe marketing werkt en denkt mee met de business
Je communiceert vlot met zowel technische als niet-technische stakeholders
Je werkt nauwkeurig en gestructureerd
Je hebt een vlotte kennis van het Nederlands, Frans en Engels




Wat kan je verwachten?

Tijdelijke opdracht: 01/06/2026 – 30/09/2026
Competitief loonpakket
Snelle opstart en impactvolle rol
Werkplaats: Zellik




Interesse?

Solliciteer vandaag nog en ontdek hoe jij data kan omzetten in echte businessimpact.

Solliciteer online of via mail naar katelyne.dai.pre@springprofessional.be

Title: HR Data Analyst
Description:
Wat ga je doen

As HR Data Analyst, you increase insight into how our associates experience HR and our key HR KPI’s. You will help lead our HR community to drive data-driven decision making across our organization. You will design and create (where needed together with our BI or IT teams) operational/tactical metrics and dashboards that will assist in making decisions on different HR domains. You will be partnering across teams to ensure that our HR metrics are consistent and aligned to our strategic goals. You will be responsible for digging into the details to improve data quality, show new insights and make new recommendations that will impact decision making.


Profound expertise on HR data, HR reporting’s (operational & strategic) and HR dashboard(s) : With your profound expertise on HR data, reporting and dashboards you easily identify possible impacts and are able to create ad-hoc reports.
Drive data-driven decision making : You help lead our HR community to drive data-driven decision making across our organization. You provide HR related insights to our HR Community and organization.
HR Data analytics roadmap : Take ownership of our HR Data analytics roadmap by identifying, designing and deploying HR metrics and dashboards.
Stakeholder management : Identify and support stakeholders to become data-minded. Take initiatives to increase data awareness.
Predictive analytics : Co-deliver, together with the BI team, predictive analytics for HR.


Wie ben jij


You have a master degree in Econometrics, Mathemathics, Statistics, Computer Sciences or other relevant fields of study around Data, Math/Statistics.
You have a minimum of 3 years of relevant experience.
You have experience with Power BI, and ideally other data analytics tools.
Ideally, you have experience with SAP HR and SuccessFactors.
You have proven to be convincing and to be able to build trust and alignment on all levels in and outside the organization.
You should be passionate about and have experience with data and reportings and have a passion to learn and master new data analysis techniques and data science methodologies. You follow pro-actively trends in the business.
You are skilled at providing clear insights within a demanding environment.
You bring strong interpersonal communication skills.
You have the ability to work cross-functionally.
You have proven to be creative in finding solutions for complex issues with detail-oriented and simplicity mindset.
You have a strong analytical profile and ability to document requirements, functional specifications, decisions made, etc.
You have project management skills.
You manage change and drive process improvement.
You are fluent in Dutch or French, with a good knowledge of English.


Wat we bieden

Een job én een rijkelijk gevuld privéleven? Bij Delhaize kan dat. Je krijgt een aantrekkelijk loon aangepast aan jouw ervaring. Bovendien geniet je van diverse extralegale voordelen: bedrijfswagen met tank- of laadkaart binnen het federaal mobiliteitsbudget, gsm plus abonnement, netto vergoeding, jaarlijkse bonus, maaltijdcheques, uitgebreide groepsverzekering en facultatieve hospitalisatieverzekering, en uiteraard een personeelskorting in de winkel! Thuiswerk spreekt voor zich en wordt voor elke job anders ingevuld. En groeien? Dat gaat hard bij Delhaize. Door de opleidingen, op de werkvloer én via onze opleidingscatalogus, krijg je daar de ruimte voor. Doorgroeien? Ook dat kan. Vraag maar aan één van je toekomstige collega’s. Spreekt bovenstaande functie jou aan? Solliciteer dan onmiddellijk en ontdek wat jouw mogelijkheden zijn bij Delhaize!

Over Delhaize

Beter leven begint bij beter eten. Dat geloven wij bij Delhaize met heel ons hart. Daarom zetten wij ons in voor het eten op het bord van 5 miljoen klanten, elke week opnieuw. In meer dan 810 winkels kunnen Belgische en Luxemburgse klanten terecht voor een uitgebreid assortiment van meer dan 20.000 kwaliteitsproducten. Van een snelle stop in een Shop&Go, over dagelijkse boodschappen in een lokale Proxy tot een ruim aanbod binnen onze AD Delhaize's; en vergeet zeker onze e-commerce diensten niet.

Die hele keten van voeding, die start binnen onze Logistiek. De 2200 collega's in onze distributiecentra van Zellik en Ninove verwerken dagelijks alle producten van onze leveranciers, vaak van Belgische bodem, en brengen deze via meer dan 3000 leveringen per dag tot bij onze winkels.

Maar het stopt niet in onze winkels of logistiek. Want ook onze meer dan 1000 collega's binnen Finance, HR, IT, Commerce of Legal staan staan om elkaar en onze klanten te helpen goed te eten.

Title: Freelance PowerBI Developer
Description:
We are looking for a Freelance Commercial Business Analyst / Power BI Developer who is passionate about transforming data into actionable business insights.

In this role, you will combine business analysis, data modeling, and Power BI development to support commercial teams in making data-driven decisions. You will work closely with stakeholders across the business, translating their needs into scalable reporting solutions and analytical insights that drive performance and growth.

This is a freelance assignment for a hands-on professional who enjoys operating at the intersection of business, data, and technology while delivering tangible value through data-driven decision-making.




Key Responsibilities

Business Intelligence & Reporting

Design, develop, and maintain Power BI dashboards, reports, and analytical solutions.
Translate business requirements into meaningful KPIs, metrics, and reporting frameworks.
Deliver actionable insights that support both strategic and operational decision-making.

Data Management & Governance

Manage and optimize data flows across multiple systems and data sources.
Ensure data quality, consistency, and compliance with governance standards.
Develop and maintain robust data models that enable scalable and reliable reporting.

Business Analysis & Commercial Insights

Collect, structure, and analyze commercial data from various sources.
Identify trends, opportunities, and performance drivers to support business growth.
Act as a trusted partner for commercial stakeholders by translating data into business value.

Automation & Process Improvement

Drive automation initiatives to improve reporting efficiency and reduce manual effort.
Identify opportunities to streamline existing processes and workflows.
Contribute to a more data-driven culture by improving accessibility and usability of business insights.




Your Profile

Bachelor's or Master's degree in Business, Data Analytics, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
Proven experience as a Business Analyst, BI Analyst, Power BI Developer, or similar role.
Strong expertise in Power BI, data modeling, and reporting solutions.
Solid understanding of data governance, data quality, and data flow management.
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to translate data into business recommendations.
Commercially minded with a clear understanding of how data can drive business performance.
Experience with automation tools and process optimization initiatives.

In this role, you will combine business analysis, data modeling, and Power BI development to support commercial teams in making data-driven decisions. You will work closely with stakeholders across the business, translating their needs into scalable reporting solutions and analytical insights that drive performance and growth.

This is a freelance assignment for a hands-on professional who enjoys operating at the intersection of business, data, and technology while delivering tangible value through data-driven decision-making.


Key Responsibilities

Business Intelligence & Reporting

  • Design, develop, and maintain Power BI dashboards, reports, and analytical solutions.
  • Translate business requirements into meaningful KPIs, metrics, and reporting frameworks.
  • Deliver actionable insights that support both strategic and operational decision-making.

Data Management & Governance

  • Manage and optimize data flows across multiple systems and data sources.
  • Ensure data quality, consistency, and compliance with governance standards.
  • Develop and maintain robust data models that enable scalable and reliable reporting.

Business Analysis & Commercial Insights

  • Collect, structure, and analyze commercial data from various sources.
  • Identify trends, opportunities, and performance drivers to support business growth.
  • Act as a trusted partner for commercial stakeholders by translating data into business value.

Automation & Process Improvement

  • Drive automation initiatives to improve reporting efficiency and reduce manual effort.
  • Identify opportunities to streamline existing processes and workflows.
  • Contribute to a more data-driven culture by improving accessibility and usability of business insights.


Your Profile

  • Bachelor's or Master's degree in Business, Data Analytics, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
  • Proven experience as a Business Analyst, BI Analyst, Power BI Developer, or similar role.
  • Strong expertise in Power BI, data modeling, and reporting solutions.
  • Solid understanding of data governance, data quality, and data flow management.
  • Strong analytical mindset with the ability to translate data into business recommendations.
  • Commercially minded with a clear understanding of how data can drive business performance.
  • Experience with automation tools and process optimization initiatives.
  • Knowledge of AI, machine learning, or advanced analytics is considered a strong plus.
  • Excellent communication skills and the ability to collaborate with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
  • Comfortable operating independently in a freelance consulting environment.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Do you want to be a part of the world’s leading jewelry company while putting your experience in technology into use? If yes, then we have an exciting opportunity for you! You will be part of Pandora Marketing Analytics teams and work in an international organisation of can-do spirited, passionate, and performance-driven people. We value flexibility and informal working, where you are trusted to deliver and to focus on outcomes and business value.




Responsibilities




· Formulate and define scope and objectives based on a thorough understanding of business needs and business processes.

· Understand the business implications of advanced analytics solutions and assist in defining the solutions to support any future business requirements.

· Execute advanced analytics projects and provide ad hoc analyses to the business as needed.

· Design, drive, and implement analytics tools and ad hoc analyses. Engage with users to understand & meet their analytics requirements.

· Ensure timely delivery of reports/analysis and proactively communicate delays/issues.

· Ensure data accuracy in each deliverable.

· Provide support for business continuity of deliverables/analyses, and drive the usage and adoption of our new self-service analytical tool.




Skills Required

· 7+ years of relevant experience

· Proficiency in data visualizations and dashboards.

· Effective SQL skills.

· Strong analytical skills.

· Proficiency in querying and manipulating complex raw datasets for analysis.

· Experience explaining technical concepts to varied audiences and experience translating business objectives into actionable analyses, with a focus on design thinking and user centric design

· Sound knowledge & experience in business communication/ presentation skills and reporting

· Proven stakeholder management skills

· BS Degree in Business, Economics, Statistics, Mathematics, Applied Mathematics, Information Technology, Finance or similar.

· Passion for data science & AI, & having Python or Pyspark skills is a plus

· Fluent English both spoken and written

Engagement Model: Direct Independent Contractor (Please read carefully)

This is an independent contractor opportunity based on a direct contractual relationship between Zoolatech and the individual service provider.

To facilitate this direct partnership, we engage with professionals who are registered and operate as a sole proprietorship, private entrepreneur, or an equivalent self-employment status in your country.

Please note, our model does not accommodate contracts through third-party intermediaries such as agencies, incubators, or umbrella companies. The essential requirement is your ability to enter into a service agreement and invoice Zoolatech directly. This is not an offer of direct employment

Please note that only candidates whose profiles closely match our requirements will be contacted.

Title: Data and Control Analyst
Description:
Job description:



 

 

The vacancy is in the Business Analytics Department.

 

Function

 

You will be involved in SAP roll-outs at our daughter companies. You will propose & implement changes to improve the controlling function within our group.
You will be promoting and supporting process improvement and standardization initiatives in the area of controlling and profitability analysis across Daikin Europe HQ and Entities.
You will discuss changes requested by the business and translate this, together with the IT Center, into SAP/PowerBI solutions.
You will be involved in the roll-out of Sales &Sales & Service software package at our daughter companies.  During these projects, you will mainly focus on the interface with SAP for controlling functionalities, as well as the reporting track in PowerBI. 
You work together with a wide range of stakeholders that all have different requirements, you are able to set priorities,  manage expectations and communicate accordingly. Your ideas how to further improve our setup will created added value.

 

 



Profile description:





Your profile

 

You have a Master's degree in business economics, finance or controlling.
You already have extended experience in a similar role as controller or SAP key-user, or extended experience in an analytical position.
You have user experience of SAP modules SD, FI and CO. 
You are prepared to travel abroad (15% of your time) and/or to the Oostkamp office (Accounting & IT) if projects or meetings require this.
You work accurate, have the ability to dig into the details without losing the helicopter view.
You possess excellent analytical skills and demonstrate good communication skills.
You are a team player but you’re also able to work independently.
You are result-driven with a strong focus on improvements.    


 



We offer:




What we offer

At Daikin, you can count on a varied job with an international market leader that stimulates its people to grow and to work together. We offer you an open ended contract. You will be given extensive training and will be well supported from the start. The position carries a competitive salary and a package of extra-legal benefits.  This function offers an excellent stepping stone to take on more responsibilities in a planning-oriented function or a detachment abroad at one of our EMEA affiliates.





 

Interested in joining our team?



Daikin Europe is a subsidiary of Daikin Industries Ltd. and the leading provider of heating, cooling, ventilation, air purification, and refrigeration technology. The company designs, manufactures and brings to market a broad portfolio of equipment, as well as tailor-made solutions for residential, commercial, and industrial purposes in Europe, Middle East, and Africa (EMEA). To date, Daikin EMEA has over 12 000 employees across more than 59 consolidated subsidiaries. It has 12 major manufacturing facilities based in Belgium, the Czech Republic, Germany, Italy, Spain, Turkey, Austria, and the UK. Our aim is to drive sustainable and effective solutions, sparking a good climate for the generations to come. And all this can only be achieved because of our People. 



Good climate starts with people 
Our employees are the beating heart of our company. At Daikin, you get to work in a positive environment backed by trust, creativity and continuous improvement. Together, we create our top-quality solutions by empowering talented teams. You will get plenty of opportunities to learn and grow. We are deeply committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace culture that reflects the communities we serve. Good climate starts with people.

Title: Master Data Analyst
Description:
Data Operations Analyst (Master Data & Testing)

Location: Poland, Czech Republic, Slovakia, Slovenia, Croatia, or Hungary (Remote/Hybrid)

Contract: 12-Month Fixed-Term Contract (with strong possibility of extension)

Employment Model: Outsourced/FSP Model via Kelly Services

Industry: Healthcare / Life Sciences




About the Opportunity:

We are supporting a global healthcare organization undergoing a large-scale business transformation and system separation programme across Europe.




As part of an established EMEA Data Operations team, the Data Operations Analyst will play a key role in ensuring the accuracy, quality, and integrity of critical business data during a major ERP and workflow transformation initiative. This position offers exposure to master data management, testing, user access governance, documentation control, and cross-functional stakeholder collaboration within a highly regulated environment.




This is an excellent opportunity for candidates with experience in data operations, master data, ERP systems, testing, or supply chain processes who enjoy working in a structured, data-driven environment.




Key Responsibilities:




Testing & Validation

Execute functional, user acceptance (UAT), and business process testing activities.
Validate workflow processes, system configurations, and business requirements.
Identify, document, and track defects through to resolution.
Ensure full traceability between requirements, test scenarios, and outcomes.

Master Data & Process Validation

Support testing and validation of material and pricing data processes.
Analyze data issues and perform root cause investigations.
Work with technical and business teams to resolve data-related defects.

User Access & Data Governance

Support user access reviews, validation exercises, and access clean-up activities.
Analyze user access data to identify inconsistencies, duplication, or compliance risks.
Maintain audit-ready records and progress trackers.

Documentation Management

Create, update, and maintain SOPs, work instructions, process documentation, and governance records.
Ensure documentation remains compliant, accurate, and aligned with business requirements.
Support documentation updates required during system and process transitions.

Reporting & Coordination

Maintain trackers and reporting dashboards using Excel and Power BI.
Monitor progress, risks, dependencies, and action items.
Support project governance activities, meetings, and stakeholder communications.




Must-Have Requirements

Bachelor's degree in Business, Supply Chain, Logistics, Data Analytics, Information Systems, or a related discipline.
1–4 years of experience in Data Operations, Master Data, Supply Chain, Business Operations, or related functions.
Experience working with ERP systems such as SAP, Oracle, JDE, or similar platforms.
Understanding of data quality, master data management, or business process governance.
Experience supporting testing activities (UAT, functional testing, validation, defect management).
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills including pivot tables, lookups, filtering, and data analysis.
Strong analytical mindset with exceptional attention to detail.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.
Fluent English (written and spoken).




Nice-to-Have Skills

Experience with SAP S/4HANA, SAP ECC, or JD Edwards.
Exposure to master data domains such as materials, pricing, customers, vendors, or product data.
Knowledge of user access management, controls, or governance processes.
Experience with Power BI, Power Query, or dashboard reporting.
Familiarity with SQL, Databricks, or other data analysis tools.
Experience working within healthcare, life sciences, manufacturing, supply chain, or regulated industries.
Understanding of documentation standards, SOPs, and audit readiness requirements.




Ideal Candidate Profile

Highly organized with strong execution and follow-through.
Comfortable working with large datasets and detailed processes.
Able to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced project environment.
Proactive, solution-oriented, and able to identify risks early.
Collaborative team player who enjoys working across international teams and stakeholders.




What’s on Offer

Opportunity to join a globally recognized healthcare organization during a major transformation programme.
Exposure to large-scale ERP, master data, and process improvement initiatives.
Work within an established international Data Operations team.
12-month contract with strong potential for extension.
Flexible working environment across multiple European locations.
Career development within a highly regulated and data-driven industry.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Don’t settle for just any consulting company, choose the one that’s right for you!




At Data Wizards, we value perseverance, curiosity, creativity, integrity and excellence. In that sense, we are looking for passionate AI & data professionals who want to help companies navigate the transition the world is facing today, and who want to be part of the Wizard adventure we are building.




On our side, we do our best to find exciting, challenging and meaningful projects for you. Today, we work with companies of all sizes, across various sectors, where we believe our work brings real value and purpose.




We are continuously on the lookout for bright data engineers, data scientists, machine learning engineers, AI engineers, and data analysts. So this job description is for anyone in data out there feeling the magic in them and ready to spread it 🪄




We’re on the lookout for Data Analysts to join our team of Wizards!




As a Data Analyst, your project would typically include:




Collecting, cleaning, and transforming data from various sources to ensure quality and usability
Writing and optimizing SQL queries and/or using Python to analyze and process data
Building and maintaining dashboards and reports (e.g. Power BI) to support business decision-making
Translating business needs into data requirements and actionable insights
Performing exploratory data analysis to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities
Collaborating with data engineers and other teams to ensure data availability and reliability
Contributing to the improvement of data models, datasets, and reporting processes
Ensuring data accuracy, consistency, and documentation of your work
Supporting stakeholders by explaining insights in a clear and structured way







You’re a step closer to joining our team if you bring:




Background:




Masters degree related to computer science, data or AI
1-4 years of experience in a data role
Previous experience building or maintaining data pipelines and ETL/ELT processes.
Exposure to working with large datasets from multiple sources (e.g., CRM, web analytics, call logs, transactional systems).




Technical Skills:




Solid hands-on experience with Python, and SQL.
Experience with SAS is a plus.
Experience with Databricks is a plus.
Familiarity with data warehousing concepts and best practices.
Experience integrating reporting tools such as Power BI.
Basic understanding of version control (e.g., Git) and automated workflows/scheduling.




Soft Skills:




Strong problem-solving and analytical thinking skills.
Ability to collaborate effectively with technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Good communication skills and attention to detail.
Proactive mindset with a willingness to learn and grow in a fast-moving environment.




Practical Info:




Languages: Native in French or Dutch and fluent in English




We know that finding the right job isn’t just about the role, it’s about what’s in it for you, too. While we’re a start-up, we’re big on taking care of our people right from day one.




Here’s a sneak peek at what we offer:




💰 A compensation package that keeps you motivated

Competitive salary
Mobility budget or company car with a fuel card
30 days of paid time off
A top-tier laptop & smartphone with a subscription
Representation allowances
Hospitalization & dental insurance
Meal vouchers
Opportunity to earn company stock over time




🎯 A place to be your best self, grow, and thrive

Work with the most innovative technologies in Data & AI
Work in an agile, collaborative environment
Hybrid work model
Monthly team-building events (plus one epic annual celebration)
Tailored training programs to fuel your career growth
A cool, ergonomic office with creative meeting spaces with a 24/7 open gym fully equiped
A Nintendo Switch for spontaneous Mario Kart battles 🎮
A massage chair for those deep-thinking moments 🧘
Fresh coffee & drinks to keep you energized ☕




✨ And most importantly, a growing company where opportunities are limitless!




Our Recruitment Process:

Quick intro call with our HR team
HR Interview
Technical Interview
Partner Meet-up
Potential Interview with a client




About us

At Data Wizards, we value perseverance, curiosity, creativity, integrity and excellence. In that sense, we are looking for passionate AI & data professionals who want to help companies navigate the transition the world is facing today, and who want to be part of the Wizard adventure we are building.

On our side, we do our best to find exciting, challenging and meaningful projects for you. Today, we work with companies of all sizes, across various sectors, where we believe our work brings real value and purpose.




If you’re passionate about what we do but aren’t sure you meet every single requirement—don’t hesitate to apply. We value potential, mindset, and growth as much as experience. At Data Wizards, we believe diversity is our strength. We welcome candidates from all backgrounds and experiences because different viewpoints help us grow and evolve.




Let’s build the future of AI & Data, together🤝




Please note: As a consulting company, our recruitment process can be either project-driven or based on strong profiles we believe in. The consultancy world moves fast, and while we always aim to match great talent with the right opportunity, there might not always be an immediate fit. With your permission, we’ll gladly keep your profile in mind for future missions, and hope to collaborate with you soon on an exciting project at Data Wizards!

Title: Marketing Data Analyst
Description:
SPREAD ENTHUSIASM. SHAPE THE FUTURE.

SHARE YOUR PASSION.

Knowing the destination isn't enough: you need to know how to get there. Build up your own networks, exploit new ideas, consistently take the initiative. Inspire other people with your enthusiasm. That's the only way to give ideas the power that allows them to become true innovations.

Are you a driven Marketing Data Analyst who knows how to turn marketing activity into measurable sales impact? Do you want to play a key role in strengthening a truly data‑driven organisation within BMW Financial Services and Alphabet? If so, we would love to hear from you.

In this role, you act as the bridge between marketing, sales and data. You transform complex datasets into clear insights, empower teams with meaningful dashboards and help shape smarter, data‑led decisions across the business.

What awaits you?


Design, maintain and continuously improve Marketing & Sales dashboards using tools such as Tableau, Adobe Analytics, Salesforce and related platforms, clearly linking marketing efforts to sales performance.
Take ownership of marketing data structure and data quality, including the setup and further development of the data warehouse to unlock actionable insights.
Analyse and visualise campaign performance, including tracking and tagging, working closely with digital marketers, campaign managers and external agencies.
Support the B2C Sales team by building, optimising and expanding reports and dashboards in Tableau, Adobe Analytics and Salesforce.
Translate data into insights by placing results in the right context and advising marketing, communication and sales teams to support strategic and data‑driven decision‑making.


What should you bring along?


You hold a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a relevant field, or equivalent experience.
You have at least 3 years’ experience in a data‑driven role such as Data Analyst or Marketing Intelligence Analyst.
You are highly proficient in Excel and have hands‑on experience with Adobe Analytics, Tableau, Salesforce or similar tools.
You combine strategic thinking with a hands‑on approach and strong analytical skills.
You are able to turn data into clear conclusions and communicate insights convincingly, both in writing and verbally.
Experience with AI applications and a strong interest in innovation within marketing and data analytics is a distinct advantage.
You are fluent in Dutch, English and French, both spoken and written.
You are enthusiastic, proactive and a true team player who enjoys collaborating and sharing knowledge across teams.


Why choose BMW Group Belux?

At BMW Financial Services you will not only work for a strong brand, but also in an environment that breathes premium - from our products to our people.

We Offer


An international working environment with a clear vision of innovation and sustainability;
A warm corporate culture where initiative and personal growth are encouraged;
Extensive training and development opportunities;
An attractive salary package with mobility solutions and a flexible cafeteria plan.


Apply today and discover how you can make a difference at BMW Group Belux!

Looking for purpose at work? One of BMW Group Belux’s entities (Alphabet Belgium) is recognized with EcoVadis Platinum for its sustainability!”

At the BMW Group, we place great importance on equal treatment and equal opportunities. Our recruiting decisions are based on the personality, experience, and skills of the applicants. Learn more here: Experience our culture | BMW Group Careers .

Title: Data Quality & Reporting Specialist (m/f/d)
Company: Halian | Managed Services, Recruitment Agency & Contract Staffing
Description:
A leading belgian financial services organization undergoing a significant data transformation journey. The company is strengthening its data quality capabilities and reporting maturity to ensure trusted, high-quality data supports decision-making, reporting, and operational processes across the business. As part of this initiative, they are seeking a Data Quality & Reporting Specialist to help expand and enhance established data quality frameworks and reporting solutions.




Responsibilities

Manage and coordinate a complex stakeholder landscape across both business and IT teams.
Develop and implement data quality plans for new projects and transformation initiatives in collaboration with project management teams.
Analyze and profile datasets to define critical data elements and establish effective data quality rules with data stewards, custodians, and business domain owners.
Build, enhance, and maintain Power BI data quality dashboards and reports in partnership with data engineers and functional analysts.
Translate data quality metrics and findings into actionable recommendations and support remediation activities.
Ensure consistency and scalability of existing data quality frameworks across new business domains and project phases.




Qualifications And Skills

Minimum 6 years of experience in data analysis, data quality management, or a related field, preferably within complex or evolving environments.
Strong proficiency in Power BI and SQL, with the ability to create meaningful reporting and insights.
Excellent stakeholder management skills and the ability to collaborate effectively across business and technical teams.
Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities, with the ability to translate technical findings into business value.
Self-driven, structured, and comfortable working independently while managing changing priorities.
Fluency in English and either Dutch or French; familiarity with data modeling concepts and experience within the insurance sector are considered advantageous.




Halian Group:

With over 28 years of experience, we have come to understand that innovation is the only way to provide agile, practical solutions that transform businesses and careers.

Our resourcing and smart services help you to realize tomorrow’s potential. Discover the amazing things possible when you bring the right people and the right technologies together.

At Halian, we recognize that diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) are essential to building high-performing teams for our clients. We are committed to connecting organizations with top talent from all backgrounds, ensuring that every individual feels valued, respected, and empowered to contribute their unique perspectives. We encourage applications from all qualified candidates, regardless of race, gender, disability, or any other characteristic that makes them unique. By fostering diverse and inclusive workplaces, we help our clients drive innovation, enhance collaboration, and better reflect the communities they serve.

Data Quality & Reporting Specialist in Brussels, Belgium

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Diegem – Business Intelligence Analyst – Nederlands, Engels en een basiskennis Frans – automotive sector – vast contract




Over de organisatie:

Onze klant is een internationaal bedrijf uit Diegem en een bekende speler in de automotive sector. Als Business Intelligence Analyst kom je terecht in een moderne werkomgeving waar je 40 uren per week werkt en recht hebt op 32 verlofdagen op jaarbasis. De werkuren zijn heel flexibel en je hebt recht op 2 dagen thuiswerk per week.




Je nieuwe functie:

Als Business Intelligence Analyst werk je dagelijks met Excel en vaak met Power BI omtrend analyses van bedrijfsdata. Je analyseert prestaties van verkoop, marketing, finance, operations, enzovoort en helpt management bij het nemen van strategische en operationele beslissingen. Hiervoor gebruik je dashboards, rapportages, forecasting en trendanalyses.




Jouw profiel:

Voor deze job als Business Intelligence Analyst is Hays op zoek naar kandidaten die beantwoorden aan onderstaande criteria:

Vlotte communicatie in het Nederlands en Engels. Daarnaast heb je ook een goede basiskennis Frans
Naast een Bachelor- of Masterdiploma beschik je ook over een eerste relevante werkervaring in een analytische job als Business Intelligence Analyst of gelijkaardige rollen.
Je bent analytisch ingesteld waardoor je kritisch met grotere volumes data kan omgaan.
Je hebt een zeer goede kennis Excel (pivot tables, v-lookups, … vormen geen probleem) en ook een goede kennis Powerpoint.
Kennis van Power BI is een groot pluspunt
Tenslotte ben je ook matuur en zelfstandig waardoor je administratief en nauwkeurig met data kan omgaan.




De arbeidsvoorwaarden:

Hays biedt je de kans om als Business Intelligence Analyst bij een bekend internationaal bedrijf uit Diegem terecht te komen. Vanaf de start krijg je

Een vast contract
een aantrekkelijk salarispakket die overeenkomt met jouw ervaring €3500 - €4300 euro bruto / maand
Maaltijdcheques van 10 euro per dag.
Groepsverzekering
Hospitalisatieverzekering
Invaliditeitsverzekering
Netto-onkostenvergoeding van 170 euro netto per maand
Korting op producten
Sport- en cultuurcheques




Solliciteer direct op deze vacature:

Heb je interesse in deze analytische job als Business Intelligence Analyst voor een bedrijf uit Diegem actief binnen de automotive sector. Solliciteer dan via onderstaande knop en wij nemen zo snel mogelijk contact met jou op. Voor andere jobs binnen office support kan je contact opnemen met Stijn Alens van Hays Diegem.

Title: Data Analyst (Insurance Sector)
Description:
For an innovative organization active within the insurance and financial services sector, we are looking for a Data Analyst. In this position, you will play a key role in transforming complex data into actionable insights that support strategic and operational decision-making across the business.

You will work in a data-driven environment where analytics, reporting, and continuous improvement are central. The role combines technical expertise with strong stakeholder interaction and offers the opportunity to contribute to both business performance and process optimization.



Your Responsibilities



Data Management & Preparation


Collect and consolidate data from multiple internal and external sources
Clean, validate, and structure datasets to ensure data quality and consistency
Support the maintenance and optimization of reporting datasets and analytical environments


Data Analysis & Insights


Analyze large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and business opportunities
Perform statistical and exploratory data analyses
Translate complex data into clear business insights and recommendations
Support business teams with ad hoc analyses and reporting requests


Reporting & Visualization


Develop dashboards, reports, and visualizations using modern BI tools
Present data in a clear and business-friendly way for both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Monitor KPIs and business performance metrics


Stakeholder Collaboration


Work closely with business, operational, and IT teams to understand reporting and analytical needs
Support decision-making through data-driven recommendations
Participate in initiatives focused on process improvement and data quality enhancement


Continuous Improvement


Stay informed about new technologies, tools, and best practices in analytics and BI
Contribute to the evolution of the organization's data capabilities and reporting standards
Share knowledge and collaborate within the broader data community


Your Profile


Experience in Data Analysis, Reporting, BI, or a similar analytical role
Strong knowledge of SQL for querying and manipulating data
Experience with reporting and visualization tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or similar
Familiarity with Python or R is considered a strong asset
Understanding of data modeling and data warehousing concepts
Strong analytical mindset with excellent problem-solving skills
Able to communicate complex insights in a clear and structured way
Autonomous, detail-oriented, and proactive
Bachelor's or Master's degree in a relevant field such as Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or similar
Professional level of English and fluent in either Dutch or French


What's Offered


The opportunity to join a modern and data-driven organization
A collaborative environment where innovation and analytics are highly valued
Exposure to large-scale data and impactful business projects
Flexibility, autonomy, and opportunities for professional growth
Competitive salary package with additional benefits

Title: Senior PowerBI data analyst
Description:
Freelance Senior Data Analyst Power BI

To start ASAP until end 2026, renewable

Brussels, Hybrid

Context

Group Controlling is rolling out Performance Indicators across the Group's local entities, with KPIs cascaded from strategic to operational level and visualised through automated Power BI dashboards.

As our Power BI Expert you will be the go-to person to design, automate, optimise and maintain the Power BI reporting landscape that supports these initiatives — making sure stakeholders, from executives to operational teams, get the right insights at the right time, in a way they can trust and act upon.

Role

1. KPI reporting & automation

a. Build and automate end-to-end KPI reporting for Group Controlling stakeholders

b. Translate business questions into clear, decision-ready dashboards aligned with our performance framework

2. Maintenance & continuous improvement of existing dashboards

a. Support the maintenance of the existing Group Controlling Power BI portfolio

b. Monitor adoption and usage (e.g. via Power BI Usage Metrics) and proactively propose improvements based on real user behaviour.

3. Semantic models, visualisation & data quality

a. Rationalise our Power BI semantic models — consolidate overlapping datasets, clarify ownership, document relationships and improve refresh performance.

b. Optimise visualisations: simplify, standardise and align them with our design and accessibility guidelines so reports are intuitive and consistent across the portfolio. c. Lead data cleaning and data quality activities upstream of the reports, in close coordination with data owners.

4. Stakeholder & bridge role between Business and IT

a. Act as a trusted advisor to business stakeholders (Controlling, IT, other departments), turning sometimes fuzzy needs into concrete reporting solutions.

b. Engage with IT and Data teams on databases, dataflows, gateways and Power BI governance (workspaces, roles, access).

Profile:

1. Solid hands-on experience with Power BI Desktop (semantic models, DAX, Power Query, workspaces, apps, dataset refreshes, gateways)

2. Proven experience designing, automating and maintaining KPI dashboards in a corporate or group-wide context, ideally in Controlling, Finance or IT governance

3. Strong skills in data modelling, semantic model optimisation and data cleaning (performance tuning, naming conventions, documentation).

4. Ability to work autonomously: identify needs, propose solutions for both data structure and visualisation, and drive them to delivery.

5. Excellent communication skills, comfortable talking to both business stakeholders (controllers, managers, executives) and IT/Data engineers;

6. Fluent in English; a good working knowledge of French and/or Dutch is a strong asset, German is a plus.

Title: Power BI
Description:
Role Overview

We are looking for a Medior Power BI / Analytics Consultant to support a long-term forecasting initiative by transforming fragmented Excel-based data into a scalable data model and interactive Power BI dashboards.

The consultant will work directly with business stakeholders, designing robust reporting solutions while improving the underlying data architecture.




Responsibilities

Develop Power BI dashboards and semantic models.
Structure and consolidate data from multiple sources, including Excel files.
Build and optimize data models and ETL processes using Databricks.
Translate complex business requirements into scalable reporting solutions.
Collaborate directly with business users and functional stakeholders.




Required Skills

Minimum 3 years of experience as a BI Consultant/Power BI Developer.
Strong expertise in Power BI, dashboard development and data visualization.
Experience with Databricks, ETL processes, and data modelling.
Experience translating complex business logic into efficient data models.
Ability to work autonomously with business stakeholders.
Native-level Dutch (C2) is mandatory.




Nice to Have

Experience with Azure, SQL, DAX, Python, Agile methodologies, Data Vault modelling, and reporting tools.

• Knowledge of the utilities/energy sector is a plus.

Title: Excel Analyst (Remote)
Description:
Role: Excel Analyst (Remote)
Location: Remote (Work from Anywhere)
Job Type: Contract
Payout: $40 - $55/hour




Role Overview:

We are hiring for one of our clients, seeking a Data Analyst (Excel) to work on a contract basis. This role involves analyzing data using Excel to support business decisions. The analyst will clean, transform, and visualize data to identify trends and generate insights.




Key Responsibilities:

• Extract and organize data from multiple sources into structured Excel reports.

• Perform data cleaning and validation to ensure accuracy and consistency.

• Create pivot tables, charts, and dashboards to present key metrics.

• Analyze data trends to assist in business decision-making processes.

• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather requirements and deliver actionable insights.




Required Skills & Qualifications:

• Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including advanced functions and formulas.

• Experience with data analysis and reporting in Excel.

• Ability to clean, transform, and validate large datasets.

• Strong attention to detail and accuracy in data processing.

• Familiarity with basic statistical analysis techniques.




More About the Opportunity:

This role offers a unique opportunity to work with a global leader in the Information Services industry, contributing to data-driven decision-making processes. The position supports strategic initiatives through accurate and timely data analysis.




Equal Opportunity Employer:

We hire based on skills and expertise. All qualified candidates are welcome regardless of background, experience, or prior employment history. Applications are reviewed solely on demonstrated technical ability and qualifications.

Title: Excel Expert - Remote
Description:
Job Description

Job Title: Excel Expert

Job Type: Contract

Location: Remote

Job Summary: In this role, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input. No prior experience in AI is required — your domain knowledge is what matters.

Key Responsibilities


Review, analyze, and enhance complex Excel files relevant to business operations, strategy, and data analysis.
Provide detailed feedback and examples of best practices in Excel for operational and consulting use cases (excluding finance).
Design, document, and share templates or solutions that reflect expert knowledge for AI learning purposes.
Collaborate with the customer’s team to identify key workflows and operational challenges represented in Excel documents.
Evaluate and simulate real-world business problems using advanced Excel features and formulas.
Communicate clearly and effectively in written and verbal formats, ensuring precise and comprehensive documentation.
Contribute to the creation of data analysis case studies and operational scenarios leveraging Excel expertise.


Required Skills And Qualifications


Expert-level proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including advanced functions, pivot tables, data validation, and automation tools.
Proven experience developing Excel solutions for business operations, strategy, or consulting environments.
Strong analytical skills and the ability to solve complex, non-financial business problems using Excel.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a keen eye for detail.
Ability to document processes, create instructional materials, and provide clear, actionable feedback.
Experience with data analysis, scenario modeling, and workflow optimization in Excel.
Self-motivated, proactive, and comfortable working independently in a remote setting.


Preferred Qualifications


Background in business operations, strategy, or management consulting.
Familiarity with AI training or contributing to machine learning datasets in a domain capacity.
Experience developing Excel-based tools or dashboards for operational excellence initiatives.

Title: Data Engineer (Fabric)
Description:
Our client seeking is seeking a skilled Data Engineer with hands-on experience in Microsoft Fabric and DBT to design, build, and maintain scalable, analytics-ready data platforms. The ideal candidate is a collaborative team player who can also work independently to solve complex business use cases end to end.

Experience in the telecommunications industry, particularly exposure to RAN sharing environments, is a strong advantage.




This is a contract/freelance role for 6 months initially with extensions.




Your Mission:

Design, build, document and maintain end-to-end data pipelines using Microsoft Fabric (Data Engineering, Data Factory, OneLake, Lakehouse, and Warehouses).
Ingest data from a variety of sources including APIs, relational databases, files, SaaS platforms, and telecommunications network systems.
Develop and manage ELT transformations using dbt, including reusable models, tests,
documentation, and data lineage.
Implement medallion architecture (Bronze, Silver, Gold) using Fabric and dbt best practices.
Build robust, analytics-ready data models (facts, dimensions, marts) to support BI, operational reporting, and advanced analytics.
Support telecommunications use cases, including network performance analysis, capacity
tracking, cost allocation, and compliance reporting.
Work with datasets related to RAN sharing, such as multi-operator performance metrics, shared asset utilization, and operator-level data segregation.
Collaborate effectively within cross-functional teams while also owning and independently driving data solutions from problem definition through delivery.
Implement CI/CD pipelines and Git-based version control for data solutions.
Ensure strong data governance, security, and access control, particularly in shared or multi-tenant data environments.
Document architecture, pipelines, and business logic clearly for long-term maintainability.




Your specific expertise:

Strong hands-on experience with Microsoft Fabric, including:

Fabric Data Engineering
Lakehouse & Warehouse
OneLake
Data Pipelines / Data Factory
Proven experience using dbt for analytics engineering and transformation-layer development.
Advanced SQL skills for data transformation, modeling, and optimization.
Experience with Spark (PySpark preferred).
Solid understanding of ELT patterns, dimensional modeling, and data warehouse best practices.
Experience with Git and CI/CD in data projects.




Your profile:

You have an bachelor or master degree in IT/data/computer science (or equivalent experience)
Minimum experience: 3 years in Data engineering in a modern IT environment
Good proficiency of English is a must, knowledge of Dutch or French is a plus
You are Analytical, proactive, and delivery-focused
You are a team player
You have excellent communication skills, and the ability to translate complex technical concepts to end user language.
Comfortable working in agile, fast-paced environment
Experience in a Telecom environment is certainly a big plus
Experience with advanced analytics and/or AI is a plus as well

Title: Data Analyst - Finance (Remote)
Description:
Role: Data Analyst - Finance (Remote)
Location: Remote (Work from Anywhere)
Job Type: Contract
Payout: $40 - $65/hour




Role Overview:

We are hiring for one of our clients, seeking a Financial Data Analyst to work on a contract basis. This role involves analyzing financial data to support decision-making and reporting. The position requires expertise in financial modeling, data visualization, and regulatory compliance.




Key Responsibilities:

• Collect, process, and analyze financial data from multiple sources to identify trends and anomalies.

• Develop and maintain financial models to forecast revenue, expenses, and cash flow.

• Prepare detailed reports and dashboards for stakeholders to support strategic planning.

• Ensure data accuracy and compliance with accounting standards and regulatory requirements.

• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to integrate financial insights into business operations.




Required Skills & Qualifications:

• Proficiency in financial data analysis tools such as Excel, SQL, or Python.

• Experience with financial modeling and forecasting techniques.

• Strong understanding of accounting principles and financial reporting standards.

• Ability to create visualizations using tools like Tableau or Power BI.

• Familiarity with data cleaning and validation processes.




More About the Opportunity:

This role offers a unique opportunity to work with a global leader in the financial services industry, contributing to accurate financial reporting and strategic decision-making.




Equal Opportunity Employer:

We hire based on skills and expertise. All qualified candidates are welcome regardless of background, experience, or prior employment history. Applications are reviewed solely on demonstrated technical ability and qualifications.

Title: BI Data Analyst
Description:
Coinspaid Solutions is a fintech company building blockchain payment infrastructure for the global economy.




We design, implement, and scale payment systems that connect digital assets with real-world financial operations. Our solutions help businesses and financial institutions operate efficiently in the evolving digital asset landscape.




For over 11 years, we’ve been developing products that bring crypto payments closer to mainstream adoption. Today, our team includes 350+ professionals working remotely across multiple countries.




We combine the pace of fintech with a long-term approach to building products, teams, and careers. As a remote-first company, we give people flexibility in how they work while maintaining strong collaboration and shared ownership across teams.




🏆 In 2026, Coinspaid was recognized as Best Corporate Culture in the Blockchain Industry in Europe, reflecting our focus on ownership, trust, and collaboration.




Responsibilities

Analyzing and interpreting complex data sets from various sources (e.g., blockchain transactions, financial markets, and customer data), using statistical techniques and data visualization tools to identify patterns, trends, and insights
Developing, maintaining, and optimizing SQL queries (Columnar databases) to ensure data accuracy, efficiency, and accessibility, perform statistical data analysis
Leveraging your expertise in Python to design and implement advanced data models, predictive analytics, and machine learning algorithms to support business decision-making and strategic initiatives
Collaborating with cross-functional teams, including product managers, engineers, and business stakeholders, to translate data insights into actionable recommendations and to develop data-driven strategies for our products and services
Creating and maintaining comprehensive documentation of data analysis processes, methodologies, and results, ensuring that key findings are effectively communicated to relevant stakeholders
Working with key stakeholders to identify needs and report outputs to answer questions and drive decisions
Staying current with industry trends and best practices in data analysis, blockchain, and finance, and identifying opportunities for improving our data infrastructure and analytical capabilities




Requirements

3+ years of BI/Analytics experience
English B2+ and experience of verbal communication in international teams
Strong knowledge of SQL
Strong experience with Tableau data visualization
Internally-motivated, self-starter mindset with an analytical thinking and curiousity to help find insights and show the value of data-driven decision making
Ability to work cross-functionally and communicate with technical and non-technical teams
Strong organization and presentation skills
Detail-oriented approach with a passion for analytics and problem-solving




Nice to have

Experience with Vertica or similar columnar database
Demonstrable experience in the blockchain or finance domain
Experience or familiarity with forecasting data (Python, R)
Experience building and maintaining data pipelines, ETL processes in Apache Airflow
Experience or familiarity with Machine Learning algorithms and their real-world advantages/drawbacks
Experience of ML models development




Why should you choose Coinspaid?

You’ll be joining a company that is actively shaping its space – with enough scale to matter and enough room to make an impact.

At Coinspaid, people are expected to think, contribute, and take ownership – and are supported in doing so.

We focus on flexibility, wellbeing, and long-term growth – without overcomplicating how benefits work.




Flexible Benefits

Benefit Bar – up to €230/month
A flexible monthly budget you can use for what matters most to you – from sports and mental health to coworking, home office, or medical-related expenses.




Work & Flexibility

Fully remote work from almost anywhere
Optional offices and relocation support
Flexible, async-friendly environment




Growth & Learning

Budget for courses, certifications, and professional development
Language learning support
Cross-team learning and knowledge sharing




Wellbeing & Support

Medical insurance or reimbursement depending on location
Access to mental health support
Financial support for important life events




Extras

Merch shop with rewards system
Team offsites and company events




Sounds good? Well then, we can’t wait to see your resume!

To learn more please visit: https://coinspaid.com/about-us/ & https://coinspaid.com/careers/
