Title: Marketing Data Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Marketing Data Analyst

Posting Start Date: 5/19/26

Job Location (Long):

Aartselaar, Belgium

Job Description

Are you a driven Marketing Data Analyst who knows how to turn marketing activity into measurable sales impact? Do you want to play a key role in strengthening a truly data‑driven organisation within BMW Financial Services and Alphabet? If so, we would love to hear from you.

In this role, you act as the bridge between marketing, sales and data. You transform complex datasets into clear insights, empower teams with meaningful dashboards and help shape smarter, data‑led decisions across the business.

What awaits you?


Design, maintain and continuously improve Marketing & Sales dashboards using tools such as Tableau, Adobe Analytics, Salesforce and related platforms, clearly linking marketing efforts to sales performance.
Take ownership of marketing data structure and data quality, including the setup and further development of the data warehouse to unlock actionable insights.
Analyse and visualise campaign performance, including tracking and tagging, working closely with digital marketers, campaign managers and external agencies.
Support the B2C Sales team by building, optimising and expanding reports and dashboards in Tableau, Adobe Analytics and Salesforce.
Translate data into insights by placing results in the right context and advising marketing, communication and sales teams to support strategic and data‑driven decision‑making.


What should you bring along?


You hold a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a relevant field, or equivalent experience.
You have at least 3 years’ experience in a data‑driven role such as Data Analyst or Marketing Intelligence Analyst.
You are highly proficient in Excel and have hands‑on experience with Adobe Analytics, Tableau, Salesforce or similar tools.
You combine strategic thinking with a hands‑on approach and strong analytical skills.
You are able to turn data into clear conclusions and communicate insights convincingly, both in writing and verbally.
Experience with AI applications and a strong interest in innovation within marketing and data analytics is a distinct advantage.
You are fluent in Dutch, English and French, both spoken and written.
You are enthusiastic, proactive and a true team player who enjoys collaborating and sharing knowledge across teams.


Why choose BMW Group Belux?

At BMW Financial Services you will not only work for a strong brand, but also in an environment that breathes premium - from our products to our people.

We Offer


An international working environment with a clear vision of innovation and sustainability;
A warm corporate culture where initiative and personal growth are encouraged;
Extensive training and development opportunities;
An attractive salary package with mobility solutions and a flexible cafeteria plan.


Apply today and discover how you can make a difference at BMW Group Belux!

Looking for purpose at work? One of BMW Group Belux’s entities (Alphabet Belgium) is recognized with EcoVadis Platinum for its sustainability!”

At the BMW Group, we place great importance on equal treatment and equal opportunities. Our recruiting decisions are based on the personality, experience, and skills of the applicants. Learn more here: Experience our culture | BMW Group Careers.

Title: Data engineer / Data analyst Banking
Description:
Talencia is looking for an experienced Data engineer and a Data analyst for one of his clients.







Location: Brussels

Start Date: ASAP

Duration: 6 months + (till end of 2027)

Language: English + FR or NL

Homeworking: Partially remote




As a Data Engineer, you will be responsible for designing, building, and maintaining the bank’s data architecture, including ETL/ELT pipelines and data warehouses. You will work closely with the Senior Data Analyst to ensure the availability of reliable, secure, and highly optimized data.




Mission:




Data Pipelines: Develop, test, and productionize robust ETL/ELT pipelines to integrate multi-source banking data streams.




Architecture & Storage: Assist in setting up and optimizing relational and dimensional database structures.




Performance Tuning: Ensure data quality, traceability, and high performance of SQL queries and production databases.




Security & Compliance: Maintain strict compliance with banking data security standards and regulations.




Profile:




Technical Skills: Flawless mastery of SQL
Solid knowledge of relational and dimensional data modeling.
Proficiency with modern data engineering tools (Python, ETL tools, Cloud or On-premises banking infrastructure).
Soft Skills: Team player with strong technical rigor and excellent interpersonal skills to collaborate effectively with the Data Analyst and infrastructure teams.
Languages: Fluent in English, combined with either French or Dutch.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

As a Senior Data Analyst, you will join our data department within a dynamic banking environment. You will serve as the crucial bridge between complex data and strategic business decisions. This role demands not only technical excellence but also a proactive mindset and outstanding communication skills to effectively collaborate with various business units.




Responsibilities:

Data Modeling & Analysis: Design, optimize, and maintain relational and dimensional data models tailored to banking requirements.




Data Extraction & Manipulation: Write flawless, complex SQL queries to extract, clean, and analyze large volumes of financial and customer data.




Business Intelligence: Translate business requirements into clear, impactful, and actionable dashboards for management and operational teams.




Collaboration & Storytelling: Present data insights in a simplified, compelling manner to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

Profile:

Experience: Senior profile (5-7+ years of experience) with a strong, mandatory background in the banking sector.
Technical Skills: expert-level SQL skills are required.
Deep understanding of data modeling (relational and dimensional / star and snowflake schemas).
Soft Skills: Excellent communication skills. We are looking for an outgoing, dynamic, and expressive professional who thrives in a collaborative environment (not an introvert).
Languages: Fluent in English, combined with either French or Dutch.







If you are interested, please send your updated CV to CvATtalencia.eu

Title: Customer Data Analyst
Description:
As a Data Analyst, you will be part of the Customer Analysis team and play a key role in transforming business needs into data-driven insights. You combine strong analytical capabilities with business understanding and use customer data to deliver actionable recommendations that create business impact.




What will you do?

Translate business needs into hypotheses and analytical research questions
Gather, transform, and analyze raw data from multiple data sources
Identify relevant datasets, variables, and analytical approaches to answer business questions
Deliver clear insights, conclusions, and recommendations based on data analysis
Develop reports and dashboards using Power BI
Perform ad hoc analysis and data queries using SAS EG, SQL, and Python
Create concise and impactful PowerPoint presentations with key findings and recommendations
Collaborate closely with internal stakeholders and team members to support customer-related initiatives
Contribute to improving customer engagement and business decision-making through data insights




What do you bring?

Master’s degree in Economics or another academic field with strong knowledge and experience in data and analytics within a business environment
Minimum of 4 years of relevant experience in Data Analytics
Proven experience with SAS Enterprise Guide (SAS EG), SQL, and Python
Experience with Power BI reporting and data visualization
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to turn data into business value
Good understanding of banking and insurance products and the financial services sector
Experience with GitLab is considered an advantage
Critical thinker with strong attention to detail
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
Proactive, autonomous, and results-oriented mindset
Team player who thrives in a dynamic and multicultural environment
Fluent in Dutch, French, and English (mandatory)




Location: Brussels – Montagne du Parc / Warandeberg

Start Date: 17/08/2026

End Date: 31/12/2026

Hours per week: 40 hours

Title: Young Graduate - Data & AI - as of September 2026
Description:
Are you dynamic, driven and ambitious & graduating in summer 2026? Does the sound of working in a hub of innovation appeal to you? Are you passionate about data & AI? Do you see yourself working in a large international company?

If you answered “yes” (who can blame you?) four times, then you should keep on reading.

Your job

As a Young Graduate within Accenture, your responsibilities are extremely diverse. You have a positive attitude and enjoy working in a team, using your creative and analytical skills to empower our clients by applying new methodologies, processes and technologies. You’ll work within an agile environment and in one of the following areas: Customer Relationship, Risk Management, Finance & Performance Management, Manufacturing and Supply Chain, and across different industries: Financial Services, Life Sciences, Telecommunications, Industrial, Resources and Public Services.



You will have a keen sense of client service, relationship building, innovation and relentless pursuit of high quality. You will be provided with training and support to succeed in your role;

You will be at the forefront of client interaction, understanding your client’s needs, detailing business requirements, shaping and delivering digital solutions that fit best;

You will build and maintain client relationships across IT and business

You will participate in the full lifecycle of a project: from gathering and understanding the end-user needs and preparing the use cases over functional design, architecting the solution, supervising the technical design, to preparing and executing (real-life) test cases before handover.

Depending on your profile you will focus more on the development of AI solutions (Agentic AI, Machine learning, Deep learning, NLP, Computer Vision, Physical AI) or in the strategy definition, functional analysis and project delivery.

You have and will grow a high passion and curiosity for AI but also data centric environments. You are also open to adapting yourself to client reality with projects focusing more on data analysis and less on latest AI innovations.

You will work every day with AI solutions (internal or client ones) to create higher impact and value on your project.

You will be ready to work at client site or Accenture Office, so you’re ready to travel in Belgium and potentially abroad.



Your team

Accenture Industry & Enterprise is a reinvention partner for our clients. We leverage design thinking, industry insights and the latest digital methodologies to help clients innovate, grow and improve their businesses. Our expertise, capabilities and experience mean that our clients (including some of the biggest brands in the world) trust us to find the right solutions for their needs.

Together with our talented international colleagues, we focus on connecting the needs of businesses with the new possibilities that come with technological progress. Because that's where the real challenges are: inventing and testing things that have never been tried before, getting new applications ready for roll-out, and ultimately guiding clients to select and implement the right technologies – including state of the art Security solutions - to transform their businesses.

Your background



Master’s degree in Computer Science, AI, Engineering, Business, or a related field experience

You have an analytical mindset and are at your best in a creative and problem-solving environment;

You thrive in a diverse working environment with fellow employees from all walks of life, cultures and creeds;

For the more technical profiles, you have solid foundation in Python, good understanding of AI / ML concepts , Git and other collaborative platforms and exposure the cloud platforms.

You are able to work with prompts to research content, create deliverables or accelerate digital products creation.

You’re perfectly fluent in English, Dutch/French and can express yourself in French/Dutch;



Our offer

Accenture is an incredible place to work - and keep learning. By joining us, you will become part of a global company with a world-class brand and reputation. Besides the work we do for our clients, we’re really proud of our vibrant, diverse workplace culture: we believe in openness and honesty, fairness and equality, common sense and realism. We want to get to know the real you and help you explore and grow - whatever it is you're great at. So you will always have lots of learning opportunities (formal and informal) to improve your role-specific skills and expertise.

Besides our high-profile, challenging projects and our nurturing work environment, we offer excellent employee benefits, including:



13th month + holiday allowance

20 + 12 holidays

Laptop and smartphone

Hospitalization insurance and extensive group insurance package

Bonus program

Training & learning opportunities

Green mobility program: e-bikes, public transport, bike 2 work allowance,…

Flexrewards: decide on your rewards package with our flexible benefits tool

Discount program: get discounts at your favorite (online) shops

Employee shares purchase plan

Eco-cheques

And many more!



Interested?

Are you ready to join Accenture for a career where you can be yourself and do what you love? Apply now and change the world around you.

Title: Data Analyst (Insurance Sector)
Description:
For an innovative organization active within the insurance and financial services sector, we are looking for a Data Analyst. In this position, you will play a key role in transforming complex data into actionable insights that support strategic and operational decision-making across the business.

You will work in a data-driven environment where analytics, reporting, and continuous improvement are central. The role combines technical expertise with strong stakeholder interaction and offers the opportunity to contribute to both business performance and process optimization.



Your Responsibilities



Data Management & Preparation


Collect and consolidate data from multiple internal and external sources
Clean, validate, and structure datasets to ensure data quality and consistency
Support the maintenance and optimization of reporting datasets and analytical environments


Data Analysis & Insights


Analyze large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and business opportunities
Perform statistical and exploratory data analyses
Translate complex data into clear business insights and recommendations
Support business teams with ad hoc analyses and reporting requests


Reporting & Visualization


Develop dashboards, reports, and visualizations using modern BI tools
Present data in a clear and business-friendly way for both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Monitor KPIs and business performance metrics


Stakeholder Collaboration


Work closely with business, operational, and IT teams to understand reporting and analytical needs
Support decision-making through data-driven recommendations
Participate in initiatives focused on process improvement and data quality enhancement


Continuous Improvement


Stay informed about new technologies, tools, and best practices in analytics and BI
Contribute to the evolution of the organization's data capabilities and reporting standards
Share knowledge and collaborate within the broader data community


Your Profile


Experience in Data Analysis, Reporting, BI, or a similar analytical role
Strong knowledge of SQL for querying and manipulating data
Experience with reporting and visualization tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or similar
Familiarity with Python or R is considered a strong asset
Understanding of data modeling and data warehousing concepts
Strong analytical mindset with excellent problem-solving skills
Able to communicate complex insights in a clear and structured way
Autonomous, detail-oriented, and proactive
Bachelor's or Master's degree in a relevant field such as Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or similar
Professional level of English and fluent in either Dutch or French


What's Offered


The opportunity to join a modern and data-driven organization
A collaborative environment where innovation and analytics are highly valued
Exposure to large-scale data and impactful business projects
Flexibility, autonomy, and opportunities for professional growth
Competitive salary package with additional benefits

Title: Senior data-analist
Description:
Voor Departement Mobiliteit en openbare werken is Vlaanderen connect op zoek naar een Data-Analyse Platform Architect




Jouw toekomstige job

Als data-analyse platform architect binnen het team Data speel je een centrale rol in het uitbouwen en duurzaam beheren van het data‑analyse platform van DMOW. Je zorgt voor een stabiele, performante en toekomstgerichte platformomgeving waarop betrouwbare en toegankelijke dataproducten kunnen worden ontwikkeld ter ondersteuning van beleids- en beheersprocessen.

Je bent verantwoordelijk voor het ontwerpen, realiseren en onderhouden van de platformarchitectuur en bijhorende componenten die BI-, data science- en GIS‑toepassingen mogelijk maken. Daarbij waak je over kwaliteit, betrouwbaarheid, schaalbaarheid en kostenefficiëntie van het platform. Je werkt actief mee aan het doorbreken van datasilo’s en creëert een omgeving waarin data vlot en veilig hergebruikt kan worden voor analyses en AI‑initiatieven

In functie van noden en projecten ondersteun je verschillende teams (DWH, AI, GIS, data governance, …) door hen te ontzorgen op vlak van platformkeuzes, technische inrichting en integraties. Je werkt hierbij nauw samen met data engineers, data scientists, BI‑analisten, GIS‑specialisten en projectleiders en neemt een verbindende rol op tussen platformbeheer en dataproductontwikkeling.




Jouw voornaamste taken en verantwoordelijkheden zijn:

Beheren, optimaliseren en verder uitbouwen van het data-analyse platform

Je bent verantwoordelijk voor het onderhouden en verbeteren van het bestaande data-analyse platform. Daarnaast werk je aan de uitbreiding ervan, zodat het platform robuust, schaalbaar en toekomstbestendig blijft, met blijvende efficiëntie en betrouwbaarheid. Daarbij zorg je ervoor dat de nieuwste AI-technologieën worden geïntegreerd en houd je oog voor het behoud van een kwalitatief, hoogwaardig en stabiel platform waarop de organisatie kan bouwen.
Je past het platform flexibel aan bij nieuwe organisatiedoelen of gebruikersbehoeften en begeleidt integraties met systemen zoals GIS.




Samenwerking met interne en externe stakeholders

Je bent een echte teamspeler en werkt nauw samen met data engineers, BI-analisten, data scientists, GIS-specialisten en andere architecten om gebruikersnoden te vertalen naar technische oplossingen. Je overlegt met andere entiteiten binnen de Vlaamse Overheid om best practices uit te wisselen.

Veiligheid en compliance

Je stemt af met DPO, CISO, enterprise architect en externe IT-partners. Je voert security- en privacymaatregelen door (AVG/GDPR) en zorgt dat de architectuur voldoet aan wet- en regelgeving. Je waarborgt datakwaliteit, veiligheid en compliance binnen systemen.

Integratie en data‑ontsluiting

Je ontwerpt en bouwt efficiënte en veilige dataflows tussen operationele systemen, data lake en data-analyse toepassingen. Hierbij besteed je aandacht aan het integreren en standaardiseren van uiteenlopende databronnen, inclusief (geo)ruimtelijke data. Daarnaast faciliteer je de data‑ontsluiting naar eindgebruikers en beleidsmakers.

Opvolgen van trends en ontwikkelingen

Je hebt een passie voor data en technologie en blijft op de hoogte van de nieuwste trends en ontwikkelingen in AI, data engineering en cloud-technologieën en past deze toe om de systemen te verbeteren en te innoveren.

Documentatie en kennisdeling

Je documenteert architectuur en processen en deelt actief kennis om best practices te verspreiden.




Wat jij van ons mag verwachten

Vlaanderen connect biedt een contract van 38 uur voor onbepaalde duur en een competitief salarispakket met:

Een aantrekkelijke bruto verloning (salaris tussen 3920 EUR en 5300 EUR, afhankelijk van ervaring, competenties en potentieel)
Een bonussysteem op basis van vast te leggen doelstellingen
28 dagen verlof en extra wettelijke feestdagen (11/07, 02/11 en 15/11).
Een mobiliteitsbudget van 810 EUR/maand
Maaltijdcheques van 8 euro/dag (eigen bijdrage 1,09 euro/dag)
Een forfaitaire onkostenvergoeding van 165 EUR/maand
Een hospitalisatieverzekering (je kan gezinsleden aansluiten)
Een groepsverzekering (4% werkgeversbijdrage en 1% werknemersbijdrage)
Een arbeidsongevallenverzekering
Een opleidingsbudget
Flexibele werkuren
Mogelijkheden tot thuiswerk
Tegemoetkoming in de kosten van schoolvakantieopvang




Jouw talenten en vaardigheden

Ervaring en opleiding

Je hebt minstens drie jaar ervaring als platform architect, data engineer, data analist of een andere gelijkwaardige functie. Je beschikt ook over een diploma in een hogere opleiding (Bachelor of Master) in een technische, analytische of bedrijfseconomische richting, of gelijkwaardig door ervaring. Je beheerst het Nederlands op CEFR-niveau C2 en hebt een basiskennis van Engels.

Technische expertise

Je hebt een sterke technische achtergrond in cloud-omgevingen (bij voorkeur Azure) en bent vertrouwd met tools zoals Databricks, Azure ML, container management, Apache Airflow en Microsoft Fabric. Ook heb je ervaring met SQL en Python, en weet je hoe je databronnen kunt structureren voor rapportagedoeleinden, bijvoorbeeld door middel van ster schema’s en views. Bovendien ben je vertrouwd met DevOps-, CI/CD- en MLOps-principes. Het belangrijkste is echter je vermogen om nieuwe platformen en technologieën snel eigen te maken.

Analytisch en probleemoplossend werken

Je beschikt over sterke analytische vaardigheden en hebt oog voor detail en consistentie. Je werkt gestructureerd en zorgvuldig en je kan de risico’s en complexiteit van opdrachten goed inschatten.

Project- & Procesvaardigheden

Je bent vertrouwd met projectmatig werken in een agile data-omgeving. Je signaleert proactief knelpunten en draagt oplossingen aan. Daarnaast denk je mee over standaardisatie en procesverbetering binnen de rapporteringsketen.

Communicatie & Samenwerking

Je communiceert helder en vertaalt technische keuzes naar eenvoudige taal voor niet-technische stakeholders. Je luistert actief, werkt oplossingsgericht en bent klantgericht. Je kunt behoeften van belanghebbenden creatief vertalen naar bruikbare oplossingen.

Persoonsgebonden Competenties

Je werkt nauwgezet en gestructureerd, neemt verantwoordelijkheid en handelt in lijn met de waarden van de organisatie. Je kunt problemen analyseren, hun context duiden en efficiënt aanvullende informatie zoeken. Je bent een betrouwbare partner voor collega’s en belanghebbenden. Verder functioneer je goed in teamverband, maar pak je ook moeiteloos zelfstandig taken op. Je staat open voor constructieve feedback en je bent altijd bereid om bij te leren.




Jouw toekomstig team

Het Departement Mobiliteit en Openbare Werken (DMOW) doet Vlaanderen bewegen: langs de weg, over het water en door de lucht.

Onze experten in beweging zetten zich elke dag in voor een veilige, slimme en duurzame mobiliteit én voor de hedendaagse wegen- en (lucht)haveninfrastructuur. We werken zowel beleidsmatig als in uitvoering mee aan verschillende projecten, in Vlaanderen en ver daarbuiten. Om die brede waaier aan uitdagingen tot een goed einde te brengen, kunnen we rekenen op onze experten met diverse profielen. Samen hebben we één doel: met onze inzet, teamwork en vakmanschap bijdragen tot een betere samenleving. Wij zijn #experteninbeweging.

Het team Data binnen de afdeling Algemene Technische Ondersteuning (ATO) van het Departement Mobiliteit en Openbare Werken (DMOW) ondersteunt beleids- en beheersprocessen met data-oplossingen. We werken aan diverse projecten rond business intelligence, data science, data governance en geografische informatie (GIS).

Het team is gehuisvest in het Marie-Elisabeth Belpairegebouw, vlakbij het Brussel-Noord station.

Ontdek hier meer over DMOW!

Waar ga je werken

Je werkt in Brussel, op wandelafstand van het station Brussel Noord.

Show more
HTML:
Voor Departement Mobiliteit en openbare werken is Vlaanderen connect op zoek naar een Data-Analyse Platform Architect


Jouw toekomstige job

Als data-analyse platform architect binnen het team Data speel je een centrale rol in het uitbouwen en duurzaam beheren van het data‑analyse platform van DMOW. Je zorgt voor een stabiele, performante en toekomstgerichte platformomgeving waarop betrouwbare en toegankelijke dataproducten kunnen worden ontwikkeld ter ondersteuning van beleids- en beheersprocessen.

Je bent verantwoordelijk voor het ontwerpen, realiseren en onderhouden van de platformarchitectuur en bijhorende componenten die BI-, data science- en GIS‑toepassingen mogelijk maken. Daarbij waak je over kwaliteit, betrouwbaarheid, schaalbaarheid en kostenefficiëntie van het platform. Je werkt actief mee aan het doorbreken van datasilo’s en creëert een omgeving waarin data vlot en veilig hergebruikt kan worden voor analyses en AI‑initiatieven

In functie van noden en projecten ondersteun je verschillende teams (DWH, AI, GIS, data governance, …) door hen te ontzorgen op vlak van platformkeuzes, technische inrichting en integraties. Je werkt hierbij nauw samen met data engineers, data scientists, BI‑analisten, GIS‑specialisten en projectleiders en neemt een verbindende rol op tussen platformbeheer en dataproductontwikkeling.


Jouw voornaamste taken en verantwoordelijkheden zijn:

Beheren, optimaliseren en verder uitbouwen van het data-analyse platform

  • Je bent verantwoordelijk voor het onderhouden en verbeteren van het bestaande data-analyse platform. Daarnaast werk je aan de uitbreiding ervan, zodat het platform robuust, schaalbaar en toekomstbestendig blijft, met blijvende efficiëntie en betrouwbaarheid. Daarbij zorg je ervoor dat de nieuwste AI-technologieën worden geïntegreerd en houd je oog voor het behoud van een kwalitatief, hoogwaardig en stabiel platform waarop de organisatie kan bouwen.
  • Je past het platform flexibel aan bij nieuwe organisatiedoelen of gebruikersbehoeften en begeleidt integraties met systemen zoals GIS.


Samenwerking met interne en externe stakeholders

  • Je bent een echte teamspeler en werkt nauw samen met data engineers, BI-analisten, data scientists, GIS-specialisten en andere architecten om gebruikersnoden te vertalen naar technische oplossingen. Je overlegt met andere entiteiten binnen de Vlaamse Overheid om best practices uit te wisselen.

Veiligheid en compliance

  • Je stemt af met DPO, CISO, enterprise architect en externe IT-partners. Je voert security- en privacymaatregelen door (AVG/GDPR) en zorgt dat de architectuur voldoet aan wet- en regelgeving. Je waarborgt datakwaliteit, veiligheid en compliance binnen systemen.

Integratie en data‑ontsluiting

  • Je ontwerpt en bouwt efficiënte en veilige dataflows tussen operationele systemen, data lake en data-analyse toepassingen. Hierbij besteed je aandacht aan het integreren en standaardiseren van uiteenlopende databronnen, inclusief (geo)ruimtelijke data. Daarnaast faciliteer je de data‑ontsluiting naar eindgebruikers en beleidsmakers.

Opvolgen van trends en ontwikkelingen

  • Je hebt een passie voor data en technologie en blijft op de hoogte van de nieuwste trends en ontwikkelingen in AI, data engineering en cloud-technologieën en past deze toe om de systemen te verbeteren en te innoveren.

Documentatie en kennisdeling

  • Je documenteert architectuur en processen en deelt actief kennis om best practices te verspreiden.


Wat jij van ons mag verwachten

Vlaanderen connect biedt een contract van 38 uur voor onbepaalde duur en een competitief salarispakket met:

  • Een aantrekkelijke bruto verloning (salaris tussen 3920 EUR en 5300 EUR, afhankelijk van ervaring, competenties en potentieel)
  • Een bonussysteem op basis van vast te leggen doelstellingen
  • 28 dagen verlof en extra wettelijke feestdagen (11/07, 02/11 en 15/11).
  • Een mobiliteitsbudget van 810 EUR/maand
  • Maaltijdcheques van 8 euro/dag (eigen bijdrage 1,09 euro/dag)
  • Een forfaitaire onkostenvergoeding van 165 EUR/maand
  • Een hospitalisatieverzekering (je kan gezinsleden aansluiten)
  • Een groepsverzekering (4% werkgeversbijdrage en 1% werknemersbijdrage)
  • Een arbeidsongevallenverzekering
  • Een opleidingsbudget
  • Flexibele werkuren
  • Mogelijkheden tot thuiswerk
  • Tegemoetkoming in de kosten van schoolvakantieopvang

Title: Project Manager
Description:
Primary Location

Belgium-Brussels

NATO Body

NATO International Staff (NATO IS)

Schedule

Full-time

Application Deadline

05-Jul-2026, 11:59:00 PM

Salary (Pay Basis)

6,713.06

Grade

NATO Grade G15


SUMMARY The Information, Communication and Technology Management (ICTM) Office provides support and services in the areas of information communication and technology management, and digital information, archives, records and knowledge management to the North Atlantic Council (NAC), its committees and to the IS. Within Business Solutions Management (BSM), the Project Manager is responsible for the implementation of new systems and software packages in support of business requirements as well as the coordination of security accreditation activities. The incumbent manages multiple projects (including the relationship between technology and business at the project level) of varying size and complexity for applications and systems for use in the NATO Headquarters (HQ) environment.
QUALIFICATIONS AND EXPERIENCE The incumbent must:
have a university degree in information technology, computer science or another relevant discipline for this post;
possess a minimum of 3 years of professional experience in project management involving software development and/or system integration in an ICT environment;
have practical knowledge of formal project management frameworks and methodologies (like PRINCE2) and practical experience of managing cross-functional teams;
have experience with software cybersecurity aspects, ideally in environments with high security requirements;
possess the following minimum levels of NATO’s official languages (English/French): V (“Advanced”) in one; II (“Elementary”) in the other.



The following would be considered an advantage:


general knowledge of main ICT infrastructure components including hardware, software (operating systems, applications, databases), networks, datacentres and cloud;
experience in ICT-related public sector procurement processes;
experience of managing projects of the implementation of AI solutions and / or the migration of business applications to the cloud.
MAIN ACCOUNTABILITIES



Project Management. Follow a structured project management methodology for activities (e.g., PRINCE2). Apply knowledge of Organization standards and vendor practices in project management to ensure successful delivery of the project. Estimate size, duration and resource requirements for projects. Monitor and oversee projects. Track financial reports for projects, provide and implement action plans to mitigate risks when applicable. Develop all relevant project documents. Work with the Release Manager to ensure proper operations turnover and Service Level Agreement (SLA) updates when applicable. Lead the post-implementation review evaluating project results, including client satisfaction.

Expertise Development. Utilise a rigorous process of tracking tasks, schedule, and controlling project risks and issues. Help analyse and discuss the potential risks/benefits involved in using the specified technology solution to address the business requirements. Work with procurement, software and hardware vendors to develop Statements of Work (SOW) or Objectives (SOO) and conduct other aspects of the acquisition process. Contribute to continuous improvement.

Information Management. Maintain oversight of proper project documentation in collaboration with the Project Support function. Guide and advice on the Information Management procedures for the Unit and contribute to its further development.

People Management. Ensure the development of team members involved in the project through sound and inclusive management principles. Create a motivating and effective project team.

Perform any other related duty as assigned.


INTERRELATIONSHIPS Reporting Line: The incumbent reports to the Team Leader of the Integration and Coordination Unit. Interactions: The incumbent works in close collaboration with other staff members of ICTM and with staff members of various Divisions from the NATO HQ for assigned projects. The incumbent interfaces with the project board, internal clients, technology teams and suppliers as appropriate. Direct reports: N/a Indirect reports: N/a.
COMPETENCIES The incumbent must demonstrate:
Achievement: Works to meet standards;
Analytical Thinking: Sees multiple relationships;
Clarity and Accuracy: Checks own work;
Conceptual Thinking: Applies learned concepts;
Customer Service Orientation: Takes personal responsibility for correcting problems;
Empathy: Is open to others' perspectives;
Impact and Influence: Takes multiple actions to persuade;
Initiative: Is decisive in a time-sensitive situation;
Teamwork: Solicits inputs and encourages others.
CONTRACT



Contract to be offered to the successful applicant (if non-seconded): Definite duration contract of three years; possibility of renewal for up to three years, during which the incumbent may apply for conversion to an indefinite duration contract.

Contract clause applicable:

In accordance with the contract policy, this is a post in which turnover is desirable for political reasons in order to be able to accommodate the Organisation's need to carry out its tasks as mandated by the Nations in a changing environment, for example by maintaining the flexibility necessary to shape the Organisation's skills profile, and to ensure appropriate international diversity.

The maximum period of service foreseen in this post is 6 years. The successful applicant will be offered a 3-year definite duration contract, which may be renewed for a further period of up to 3 years. However, according to the procedure described in the contract policy the incumbent may apply for conversion to an indefinite contract during the period of renewal and no later than one year before the end of contract.

If the successful applicant is seconded from the national administration of one of NATO’s member States, a 3-year definite duration contract will be offered, which may be renewed for a further period of up to 3 years subject also to the agreement of the national authority concerned. The maximum period of service in the post as a seconded staff member is six years.

Serving staff will be offered a contract in accordance with the NATO Civilian Personnel Régulations.


USEFUL INFORMATION REGARDING APPLICATION AND RECRUITMENT PROCESS



Please note that we can only accept applications from nationals of NATO member countries. Applications must be submitted using e-recruitment system, as applicable:


For NATO civilian staff members only: please apply via the internal recruitment portal ( link );
For all other applications: www.nato.int/recruitment



Before you apply to any position, we encourage you to click here and watch our video providing 6 tips to prepare you for your application and recruitment process.

Do you have questions on the application process in the system and not sure how to proceed? Click here for a video containing the information you need to successfully submit your application on time.

When submitting your application, please ensure that your Taleo Candidate Profile is updated and that your CV is correctly uploaded in the Taleo attachments section.

More information about the recruitment process and conditions of employment, can be found at our website (http://www.nato.int/cps/en/natolive/recruit-hq-e.htm)

Appointment will be subject to receipt of a security clearance (provided by the national Authorities of the selected candidate), approval of the candidate’s medical file by the NATO Medical Adviser, verification of your study(ies) and work experience, and the successful completion of the accreditation and notification process by the relevant authorities.

NATO will not accept any phase of the recruitment and selection prepared, in whole or in part, by means of reference documents without proper quotes (plagiarism), or any tools available on internet, including but not limited to translation facilities, or generative artificial-intelligence (AI) tools. NATO reserves the right to screen applications to identify the use of such tools. All applications prepared, in whole or in part, by means of such tools will be rejected without further consideration, and NATO reserves the right to take further steps in such cases as appropriate.


ADDITIONAL INFORMATION



NATO is committed to diversity and inclusion, and strives to provide equal access to employment, advancement and retention, independent of gender, age, nationality, ethnic origin, religion or belief, cultural background, sexual orientation, and disability. NATO welcomes applications of nationals from all member Nations, and strongly encourages women to apply.

NATO is committed to fostering an inclusive and accessible working environment, where all candidates living with disabilities can fully participate in the recruitment and selection process. If you require reasonable accommodation, please inform us during your selection process.

Candidates will be required to provide documented medical evidence to support their request for accommodation.

Building Integrity is a key element of NATO’s core tasks. As an employer, NATO values commitment to the principles of integrity, transparency and accountability in accordance with international norms and practices established for the defence and related security sector. Selected candidates are expected to be role models of integrity, and to promote good governance through ongoing efforts in their work.

Due to the broad interest in NATO and the large number of potential candidates, telephone or e-mail enquiries cannot be dealt with.

Applicants who are not successful in this competition may be offered an appointment to another post of a similar nature, albeit at the same or a lower grade, provided they meet the necessary requirements.

The nature of this position may require the staff member at times to be called upon to travel for work and/or to work outside normal office hours.

The organization offers several work-life policies including Teleworking and Flexible Working arrangements (Flexitime) subject to business requirements.

Please note that the International Staff at NATO Headquarters in Brussels, Belgium is a non-smoking environment.

For information about the NATO Single Salary Scale (Grading, Allowances, etc.) please visit our website . Detailed data is available under the Salary and Benefits tab.

NATO does not charge any application, processing, training, interviewing, testing or other fee in connection with the application or recruitment process. For more info please click here .

Title: Officer, HR Business Improvement
Description:
Primary Location

Belgium-Brussels

NATO Body

NATO International Staff (NATO IS)

Schedule

Full-time

Application Deadline

12-Jul-2026, 5:59:00 PM

Salary (Pay Basis)

6,713.06

Grade

NATO Grade G15-G17


SUMMARY



Within NATO’s International Staff (IS), the NATO Human Resources (NHR) Office is comprised of five pillars: Talent Management; HR Services; NATO-wide HR Policy and Strategy Coordination; Organisational Design and Development and the HR Digital Services.

The Officer, HR Business Improvement leads the design and delivery of change management initiatives related to HR transformation and process improvement, to ensure closer alignment of HR operations with Organizational objectives. Within this context, they research and recommend the best use of available or future technologies and systems.


QUALIFICATIONS AND EXPERIENCE



The incumbent must:


possess a university degree in Business Management, Information Systems or other relevant discipline;
have at least 5 years’ experience in managing change, ideally in an international organisation or a multicultural environment;
be familiar with ICT systems, office software packages, and their application to enable human resource services through technology; and
possess the following minimum levels of NATO’s official languages (English/French): V (“Advanced”) in one; II (“Elementary”) in the other.



The following would be considered an advantage:


working knowledge of software applications, in particular collaborative tools, tasking tools and solutions, including techniques for retrieving, processing and presenting information;
experience in the NATO operating environment & related knowledge of NATO structure, practices & business processes.
MAIN ACCOUNTABILITIES



Project Management: Initiate, plan and lead end-to-end project execution related to NHR business improvement initiatives enhancing operational efficiency, resilience and business continuity. Contribute to risk assessments and mitigations. Develop and support change management initiatives.

Expertise Development: Keep abreast of the developments in the area of HR tools and technologies, as well as processes, to modernise the NHR ways of working. Provide suggestions on how to leverage the existing tools, processes and procedures to increase NHR operational efficiency. Document existing NHR workflows and processes to identify inefficiencies and risks,

Stakeholder Management: Coordinate and build strong partnerships with relevant stakeholders to successfully drive outcomes. Provide regular updates and feedback to stakeholder to promote efficient information management across NHR sections.

Information Management: Share best practices in terms of updated, clear and easily accessible HR Information and Knowledge Management. Produce and present relevant reports and statistics.

Perform any other related duty as assigned.


INTERRELATIONS



Reporting Line: The Officer, HR Business Improvement reports directly to the Deputy Director, NHR.

Interactions: The incumbent will work closely with NHR, mainly developing partnerships with all subject-matter experts across NHR and Information and Communication Technology Management (ICTM) members.

Direct reports: N/A

Indirect reports: N/A


COMPETENCIES



The incumbent must demonstrate:


Achievement: works to meet standards.
Analytical Thinking: sees multiple relationships.
Clarity and Accuracy: checks own work.
Conceptual Thinking: applies learned concepts.
Customer Service Orientation: takes personal responsibility for correcting problems.
Empathy: is open to others’ perspectives.
Impact and Influence: takes multiple actions to persuade.
Initiative: is decisive in a time-sensitive situation.
Teamwork: solicits inputs and encourages others.
CONTRACT



Contract to be offered to the successful applicant (if non-seconded): Definite duration contract of three years; possibility of renewal for up to 3 years.

Contract clause applicable:

This post is required for a limited period to support a project. As employment in this post is of limited duration, the successful applicant will be offered a definite duration con tract of 3 years’ duration , which may be renewed for a period of up to 3 years if required to support this project.

If the successful applicant is seconded from the national administration of one of NATO’s member States, a 3 years definite duration contract will be offered, which may be renewed for a period of up to 3 years if requir ed to support this project, and subject also to the agreement of the national authority concerned.

Serving staff will be offered a contract in accordance with the NATO Civilian Personnel Regulations.

NOTE: Irrespective of previous qualifications and experience, candidates for twin-graded posts will be appointed at the lower grade. Advancement to the higher grade is not automatic, and will not normally take place during the first three years of service in the post.

Under specific circumstances, serving staff members may be appointed directly to the higher grade, and a period of three years might be reduced by up to twenty four months for external candidates. These circumstances are described in the IS directive on twin-graded posts.


USEFUL INFORMATION REGARDING APPLICATION AND RECRUITMENT PROCESS



Please note that we can only accept applications from nationals of NATO member countries. Applications must be submitted using e-recruitment system, as applicable:


For NATO civilian staff members only: please apply via the internal recruitment portal ( link );
For all other applications: www.nato.int/recruitment



Before you apply to any position, we encourage you to click here and watch our video providing 6 tips to prepare you for your application and recruitment process.

Do you have questions on the application process in the system and not sure how to proceed? Click here for a video containing the information you need to successfully submit your application on time.

When submitting your application, please ensure that your Taleo Candidate Profile is updated and that your CV is correctly uploaded in the Taleo attachments section.

More information about the recruitment process and conditions of employment, can be found at our website (http://www.nato.int/cps/en/natolive/recruit-hq-e.htm)

Appointment will be subject to receipt of a security clearance (provided by the national Authorities of the selected candidate), approval of the candidate’s medical file by the NATO Medical Adviser, verification of your study(ies) and work experience, and the successful completion of the accreditation and notification process by the relevant authorities.

NATO will not accept any phase of the recruitment and selection prepared, in whole or in part, by means of reference documents without proper quotes (plagiarism), or any tools available on internet, including but not limited to translation facilities, or generative artificial-intelligence (AI) tools. NATO reserves the right to screen applications to identify the use of such tools. All applications prepared, in whole or in part, by means of such tools will be rejected without further consideration, and NATO reserves the right to take further steps in such cases as appropriate.


ADDITIONAL INFORMATION



NATO is committed to diversity and inclusion, and strives to provide equal access to employment, advancement and retention, independent of gender, age, nationality, ethnic origin, religion or belief, cultural background, sexual orientation, and disability. NATO welcomes applications of nationals from all member Nations, and strongly encourages women to apply.

NATO is committed to fostering an inclusive and accessible working environment, where all candidates living with disabilities can fully participate in the recruitment and selection process. If you require reasonable accommodation, please inform us during your selection process.

Candidates will be required to provide documented medical evidence to support their request for accommodation.

Building Integrity is a key element of NATO’s core tasks. As an employer, NATO values commitment to the principles of integrity, transparency and accountability in accordance with international norms and practices established for the defence and related security sector. Selected candidates are expected to be role models of integrity, and to promote good governance through ongoing efforts in their work.

Due to the broad interest in NATO and the large number of potential candidates, telephone or e-mail enquiries cannot be dealt with.

Applicants who are not successful in this competition may be offered an appointment to another post of a similar nature, albeit at the same or a lower grade, provided they meet the necessary requirements.

The nature of this position may require the staff member at times to be called upon to travel for work and/or to work outside normal office hours.

The organization offers several work-life policies including Teleworking and Flexible Working arrangements (Flexitime) subject to business requirements.

Please note that the International Staff at NATO Headquarters in Brussels, Belgium is a non-smoking environment.

For information about the NATO Single Salary Scale (Grading, Allowances, etc.) please visit our website . Detailed data is available under the Salary and Benefits tab.

NATO does not charge any application, processing, training, interviewing, testing or other fee in connection with the application or recruitment process. For more info please click here .

Title: Data Engineer (Fabric)
Description:
Our client seeking is seeking a skilled Data Engineer with hands-on experience in Microsoft Fabric and DBT to design, build, and maintain scalable, analytics-ready data platforms. The ideal candidate is a collaborative team player who can also work independently to solve complex business use cases end to end.

Experience in the telecommunications industry, particularly exposure to RAN sharing environments, is a strong advantage.




This is a contract/freelance role for 6 months initially with extensions.




Your Mission:

Design, build, document and maintain end-to-end data pipelines using Microsoft Fabric (Data Engineering, Data Factory, OneLake, Lakehouse, and Warehouses).
Ingest data from a variety of sources including APIs, relational databases, files, SaaS platforms, and telecommunications network systems.
Develop and manage ELT transformations using dbt, including reusable models, tests,
documentation, and data lineage.
Implement medallion architecture (Bronze, Silver, Gold) using Fabric and dbt best practices.
Build robust, analytics-ready data models (facts, dimensions, marts) to support BI, operational reporting, and advanced analytics.
Support telecommunications use cases, including network performance analysis, capacity
tracking, cost allocation, and compliance reporting.
Work with datasets related to RAN sharing, such as multi-operator performance metrics, shared asset utilization, and operator-level data segregation.
Collaborate effectively within cross-functional teams while also owning and independently driving data solutions from problem definition through delivery.
Implement CI/CD pipelines and Git-based version control for data solutions.
Ensure strong data governance, security, and access control, particularly in shared or multi-tenant data environments.
Document architecture, pipelines, and business logic clearly for long-term maintainability.




Your specific expertise:

Strong hands-on experience with Microsoft Fabric, including:

Fabric Data Engineering
Lakehouse & Warehouse
OneLake
Data Pipelines / Data Factory
Proven experience using dbt for analytics engineering and transformation-layer development.
Advanced SQL skills for data transformation, modeling, and optimization.
Experience with Spark (PySpark preferred).
Solid understanding of ELT patterns, dimensional modeling, and data warehouse best practices.
Experience with Git and CI/CD in data projects.




Your profile:

You have an bachelor or master degree in IT/data/computer science (or equivalent experience)
Minimum experience: 3 years in Data engineering in a modern IT environment
Good proficiency of English is a must, knowledge of Dutch or French is a plus
You are Analytical, proactive, and delivery-focused
You are a team player
You have excellent communication skills, and the ability to translate complex technical concepts to end user language.
Comfortable working in agile, fast-paced environment
Experience in a Telecom environment is certainly a big plus
Experience with advanced analytics and/or AI is a plus as well

Title: Data compliance
Description:
ACENSI is looking for Data Compliance a for one of their client based in Bruxelles :




CONFORMITY CRITERIA

• You have a minimum 5 years of experience in GDPR-related projects

• You have proven experience covering at least 4 of the following domains: Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC), Data Governance and Data Compliance roles and bodies, Data Security, Privacy-by-design, Data Retention, Data Compliance frameworks (GDPR/NIS2/EU AI Act).

• You have proven knowledge of GDPR

• You have a DPO certification issued by a recognised certification body (e.g. IAPP, ISACA or equivalent).




EVALUATION CRITERIA:

• You have a degree of experience in coordinating compliance or data governance projects

• You have a depth of experience in data protection awareness and change initiatives e.g. as Project Manager.

• You have experience in analytical activities related to data compliance (e.g. risk assessments, regulatory analysis, reporting); you are able to simplify complex regulatory and technical topics for non-technical audiences.

• You have a level of involvement in GRC and regulatory risk management processes

• You have a level of experience with data compliance tools

• ISO 27k/42K or equivalent certification is a strong plus.




LANGUAGES:

English : professional proficiency

French : professional proficiency

Dutch : professional proficiency

Title: Data Analyst BI
Description:
Who are we?

Fednot is the Federation of Belgian Notaries. Our mission is to support the +1100 notary offices and the ~8.000 notary's employees with legal expertise, management advise, IT solutions, training and information for the general public, etc.

Fednot also develops and delivers software solutions and services to support the digital transformation process of the notary offices. The solutions covers all notary domains (real estate, enterprise, family, etc.) and are based on state-of-the art technologies.

The Data/BI/AI department is responsible to create actionable insights from the data that is managed in the notary offices.




Do you have a passion for what you do, do you go for commitment, think and act in a flexible way, and want to contribute to the success of our company? Then this could be your next step.




We are looking for a




Data Analyst BI (m, f, x)




Full-time, hybrid telework + office in Brussels




Your role

As a Data Analyst BI, you will play a critical role in working with our internal clients to understand their business needs, analyze requirements and translate them into added value reporting or analytics specifications. You will work closely with the data engineers, data governance lead, other data analysts & internal clients to ensure that the solutions meet the expected business goals.

More specifically, you :




You will transform data into actionable insights and reporting that support strategic and operational decision-making across the organization
Gather and understand stakeholders’ requirements and document the ambition and added-value of the request for Fednot, the notaries and or the citizens; you manage stakeholders through setting realistic expectations, maintaining clear feedback and ensuring everyone is aligned on project goals;
Collaborate with stakeholders to gather and document business requirements, ensuring business demand is in alignment with strategic directions and there is a clear understanding on project objectives;
Identify and understand the data models and business processes of our source domains;
Identify, document and evaluate the solutions to be implemented and define acceptance criteria ;
Create clear reports/dashboards that identify consistent trends and propose actionable insights;
Plan and coordinate the delivery of solutions with the various parties involved (Data Engineers, architects, data governance lead, DBA, DPO, Business stakeholders or external dependencies) and keep track of project progress;
Perform in-depth process analysis and propose solutions to enhance operational efficiency;
Support the Product Owners/Managers in the development of a vision and the evolution of data products;




Your profile

You hold a Master degree and have experience as Business Analyst, Functional analyst or as member of a Data/BI/AI Team
You have a background in data analysis or statistics, or a strong affinity with these fields.
At least 1 year of professional experience
You have a basic knowledge of SQL with which you can perform simple analyses in a big data environment
You have experience with visualization tools such as PowerBI, Looker or DataStudio.
You take ownership for your actions
You are insisting on the highest standards and are quality and results driven
You have a strong analytical, conceptual and problem-solving skills, with the ability to analyze complex data sets and derive actionable insights.
A very flexible and proactive approach, combining diplomacy and assertiveness
Fluency in English and French and/or Dutch with strong verbal and written communication skills




We offer

Challenging and interesting projects to work on, using the latest methodologies and technologies, within a growing and economical stable sector.
Room for future development, room to develop creativity and autonomy, fostering an open atmosphere
A familiar working atmosphere as well as independent work in dedicated teams
Flexibility and attention to work-life balance
A competitive salary package with lots of extra-legal benefits




Where are you going to work?

1000 Brussels (40%) & Homeworking (60%), flexible!




Where do you find us?

We’re located right in the center of Brussels, The Central Train Station is just two blocks away.

If you apply with us, your CV and motivation letter, which include identification details, details related to the profession and academic curriculum, will be kept in our database for 3 years for reasons of justified interest (e.g. contacting us for a new job). These data will not be exchanged with third parties, unless there is a legal obligation to do so. You can always exercise all rights included in the GDPR in particular, right to access and information, right to data erasure, right to limitation of processing, right to rectification, right to objection and right to lodge a complaint with the supervising authority, by contacting info@privanot.be. We ensure that all your personal data are treated in accordance with the applicable privacy legislation.

NATO Grade G17-G20


SUMMARY



The Defence Policy and Planning (DPP) Division leads work in the NATO International Staff (IS) on the defence-related aspects of the Alliance’s fundamental security tasks at the political-military level. Within the DPP, the Defence Enablement & Logistics Section develops, facilitates and supports the development of high-quality professional political-military advice to NATO and national authorities on establishing and maintaining the strategic conditions to logistically support and sustain the Alliance's deterrence and defence posture.

The Officer provides political-military advice on the Alliance’s enablement and logistics policies and planning. The incumbent supports NATO and Allied consultations across a number of committees, working in close coordination with NATO and national stakeholders to advance the Alliance’s strategic interests. They will also deputise the Head of Section, as appropriate.


QUALIFICATIONS AND EXPERIENCE



The incumbent must:


possess a university degree in a relevant field for the post;
have at least 5 years of relevant professional experience in a civil-military environment;
have experience in coordinating, developing and analysing defence, policies, plans and concepts, including those related to logistics or related subjects;
demonstrate good understanding of the Alliance’s key defence and security policy issues and of its political and military consultative processes; and
possess the following minimum levels of NATO’s official languages (English/ French): V (“Advanced”) in one; I (“Beginner”) in the other.



The following would be considered an advantage:


experience in executing similar work for a national, military or international organisation;
experience working on logistics and related matters within the commercial sector; and
have strong written and verbal communications skills.
MAIN ACCOUNTABILITIES



Expertise Development: Contribute to the development and ensure the coherence of the Alliance’s political and military approach to key defence and security policy issues related to enablement and logistics. Take into account enablement and logistics lessons identified and learned from the strategic environment in the development and implementation of policies, plans and concepts.

Policy Development: Prepare studies, reports and conceptual and analytical papers addressing enablement and logistics issues, policies, plans and their interaction with defence policy and planning at the strategic level. Facilitate the implementation of high-level political and military guidance in the logistics domain across the NATO enterprise; and manage, coordinate and monitor its implementation and compliance. Track and ensure appropriate follow-up to decisions made by the North Atlantic Council and relevant Committees.

Stakeholder Management: Drive the enablement and logistics agenda forward through the Logistics Committee. Coordinate political-military activities in support of Allied and senior NATO stakeholders. Contribute to the work of other senior NATO committees as appropriate, and ensure that their work is appropriately reflected in enablement and logistics matters. Guide and mentor colleagues and stakeholders, as appropriate.

Perform any other related duty as assigned.


INTERRELATIONSHIPS



Reporting Line: The Officer reports to the Head, Defence Enablement & Logistics Section.

Interactions: The incumbent maintains a close working relationship with other Divisional and Section staff, as well as with other International Staff Divisions, the International Military Staff, NATO’s Strategic Commands, appropriate national logistics staff, and relevant external stakeholders.

Direct reports: N/A

Indirect reports: N/A


COMPETENCIES



The incumbent must demonstrate:


Achievement: creates own measures of excellence and improves performance.
Analytical Thinking: sees multiple relationships.
Change Leadership: expresses vision for change.
Impact and Influence: uses indirect influence.
Initiative: is decisive in a time-sensitive situation.
Organisational Awareness: understands organisational politics.
Teamwork: solicits inputs and encourages others.
CONTRACT



Contract to be offered to the successful applicant (if non-seconded): Definite duration contract of three years; possibility of renewal for up to three years, during which the incumbent may apply for conversion to an indefinite duration contract.

Contract clause applicable:

In accordance with the contract policy, this is a post in which turnover is desirable for political reasons in order to be able to accommodate the Organisation's need to carry out its tasks as mandated by the Nations in a changing environment, for example by maintaining the flexibility necessary to shape the Organisation's skills profile, and to ensure appropriate international diversity.

The maximum period of service foreseen in this post is 6 years. The successful applicant will be offered a 3-year definite duration contract, which may be renewed for a further period of up to 3 years. However, according to the procedure described in the contract policy the incumbent may apply for conversion to an indefinite contract during the period of renewal and no later than one year before the end of contract.

If the successful applicant is seconded from the national administration of one of NATO’s member States, a 3-year definite duration contract will be offered, which may be renewed for a further period of up to 3 years subject also to the agreement of the national authority concerned. The maximum period of service in the post as a seconded staff member is six years.

Serving staff will be offered a contract in accordance with the NATO Civilian Personnel Régulations.

NOTE: Irrespective of previous qualifications and experience, candidates for twin-graded posts will be appointed at the lower grade. Advancement to the higher grade is not automatic, and will not normally take place during the first three years of service in the post.

Under specific circumstances, serving staff members may be appointed directly to the higher grade, and a period of three years might be reduced by up to twenty four months for external candidates. These circumstances are described in the IS directive on twin-graded posts.


USEFUL INFORMATION REGARDING APPLICATION AND RECRUITMENT PROCESS



Please note that we can only accept applications from nationals of NATO member countries. Applications must be submitted using e-recruitment system, as applicable:


For NATO civilian staff members only: please apply via the internal recruitment portal ( link );
For all other applications: www.nato.int/recruitment



Before you apply to any position, we encourage you to click here and watch our video providing 6 tips to prepare you for your application and recruitment process.

Do you have questions on the application process in the system and not sure how to proceed? Click here for a video containing the information you need to successfully submit your application on time.

When submitting your application, please ensure that your Taleo Candidate Profile is updated and that your CV is correctly uploaded in the Taleo attachments section.

More information about the recruitment process and conditions of employment, can be found at our website (http://www.nato.int/cps/en/natolive/recruit-hq-e.htm)

Appointment will be subject to receipt of a security clearance (provided by the national Authorities of the selected candidate), approval of the candidate’s medical file by the NATO Medical Adviser, verification of your study(ies) and work experience, and the successful completion of the accreditation and notification process by the relevant authorities.

NATO will not accept any phase of the recruitment and selection prepared, in whole or in part, by means of reference documents without proper quotes (plagiarism), or any tools available on internet, including but not limited to translation facilities, or generative artificial-intelligence (AI) tools. NATO reserves the right to screen applications to identify the use of such tools. All applications prepared, in whole or in part, by means of such tools will be rejected without further consideration, and NATO reserves the right to take further steps in such cases as appropriate.


ADDITIONAL INFORMATION



NATO is committed to diversity and inclusion, and strives to provide equal access to employment, advancement and retention, independent of gender, age, nationality, ethnic origin, religion or belief, cultural background, sexual orientation, and disability. NATO welcomes applications of nationals from all member Nations, and strongly encourages women to apply.

NATO is committed to fostering an inclusive and accessible working environment, where all candidates living with disabilities can fully participate in the recruitment and selection process. If you require reasonable accommodation, please inform us during your selection process.

Candidates will be required to provide documented medical evidence to support their request for accommodation.

Building Integrity is a key element of NATO’s core tasks. As an employer, NATO values commitment to the principles of integrity, transparency and accountability in accordance with international norms and practices established for the defence and related security sector. Selected candidates are expected to be role models of integrity, and to promote good governance through ongoing efforts in their work.

Due to the broad interest in NATO and the large number of potential candidates, telephone or e-mail enquiries cannot be dealt with.

Applicants who are not successful in this competition may be offered an appointment to another post of a similar nature, albeit at the same or a lower grade, provided they meet the necessary requirements.

The nature of this position may require the staff member at times to be called upon to travel for work and/or to work outside normal office hours.

The organization offers several work-life policies including Teleworking and Flexible Working arrangements (Flexitime) subject to business requirements.

Please note that the International Staff at NATO Headquarters in Brussels, Belgium is a non-smoking environment.

For information about the NATO Single Salary Scale (Grading, Allowances, etc.) please visit our website . Detailed data is available under the Salary and Benefits tab.

NATO does not charge any application, processing, training, interviewing, testing or other fee in connection with the application or recruitment process. For more info please click here .

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Job Title: Data Engineer (3+ Years Experience)




Location: Hybrid (Offices in Aalst & Diegem)




Salary: €4,000/€5,000 per month (gross depending on experience) / + benefits




Overview:




We are looking for a Data Engineer with 3+ years of experience who is not only technically strong but also enjoys mentoring and guiding junior team members. The ideal candidate is a team player with strong communication skills and a collaborative mindset.




Key Responsibilities:




Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines using Azure and Databricks
Implement and manage data architectures following the Bronze, Silver, Gold medallion framework
Support and coach junior data engineers, helping them grow technically and professionally
Collaborate with internal teams while minimizing client-side work
Contribute to continuous improvement of data platforms and best practices




Requirements:




3+ years of experience in data engineering
Strong experience with Azure and Databricks
Solid understanding of medallion architecture (Bronze/Silver/Gold layers)
Fluent in English and Dutch
Strong interpersonal skills and mentoring mindset




What We Offer:




Company car, full insurance package, phone, laptop, and Wi-Fi
Individual performance bonus + company-wide bonus
€10,000 annual training budget
Flexible hybrid working model
Collaborative and growth-driven team culture




Great opportunity to join an exciting start-up company and make a real difference!

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
As a credit insurer, Credendo attaches great importance to the reliability and availability of its data. Within the Data Management Office, we ensure that the data reaches the right person in the right format and at the right time. We have a Enterprise Data Warehouse in SAP BW on HANA that is at the epicentre of our data-driven culture. Using reporting tools such as Power BI and SAP BO, we bring the data to the decision-makers so that they can make well-founded decisions.




As a BI Analyst, you’ll work within the Data Management Office, being part of the Strategy & Innovation department, to participate in various projects for the delivery of new functionalities. You will also provide the necessary support for existing solutions. You will collaborate actively with the data science team concerning new ways to use Credendo’s data on the Azure platform. You will be involved in all phases of the development cycle for the data and analytics platform, i.e. analysis and design, development, testing, go-live, user training and support after production. You will be an ambassador of Credendo’s self-service BI rollout, where you will enable business to build their own reports in a governed environment.




Your responsibilities:




You will take care of the extraction of data from different sources, set up data flows, create transformations and end-user reporting.
You will manage user groups, authorisations, connections, etc.
You will document and design the required functionalities, in close cooperation with the key users.
You will provide specialised knowledge about SAP BW and MS PBI and recommend solutions that meet Credendo’s (agile development) needs.
You will create test scenarios and perform system and integration tests to ensure quality, functionality and performance.
You will help with rolling out self-service BI within Credendo.
You will create/update documentation for end-users and for training courses.
You will create documentation and procedures for implementation, both in technical jargon and in user-friendly language.




Your profile:




You hold a master’s or bachelor’s degree in computer science, information science, engineering or mathematics and you have at least 5 years of relevant experience as a BW and/or BI Analyst.
Experience as a BI Analyst in the financial sector is a plus. Knowledge of the analysis of technical and business-related requirements, business process modelling/mapping, methodology development, data modelling of business requirements and agile development is also an asset.
You have knowledge of development in SAP BW and MS PBI and you have proven ability to provide technical and functional support for both. Knowledge of other BI reporting tools is considered a strong plus.
You can easily convert business requirements into technical BW solutions (or are willing to specialise in this).
You have a good command of the MS PBI platform on a technical level (or you are willing to specialise in this). Experience with rolling out governed self-service BI on a company level is considered a strong plus.
Strong interest in the wider Microsoft BI & Analytics stack is considered a plus.
You quickly pick up on business needs and can translate them into BI solutions.
As a point of contact, you have good listening, interpersonal and communication skills.
You can place information in a broader perspective, quickly grasp complex situations and get to the heart of a problem.
You are team-oriented and like to share knowledge and expertise.
You work in an organised and meticulous way. You know how to deal with deadlines.
You have a good knowledge of English, Dutch and/or French is considered a plus.




We offer:




An interesting permanent position in a stable, multilingual and international environment.
Learning opportunities to continue to develop your skills and expertise.
A salary depending on your profile and experience, supplemented by a hospitalisation and ambulatory care insurance, pension insurance, FlexReward plan and other fringe benefits.
A hybrid working arrangement (three days of teleworking) that offers flexibility and a good work-life balance.
A role in a company where the following values are key: respect, customer intimacy and reliability.







Credendo is a leading European credit insurance company, fuelled by a multinational force of 563 professionals. Active in twelve European countries, we serve local and international clients from all around the world. Even though we are rapidly growing, we strive to preserve the soul of a human-sized company in the way we interact with each other from top to bottom.
