Title: Functional Data Analyst Consultant - Data Modeling & Business Process Optimization | FR/NL and EN
Description:
Functional Data Analyst Consultant at Orange Business

Permanent contract

FR or NL + EN




Ever wondered what it feels like to be at the cutting edge of digital innovation? Our Digital & Data team at Orange Business is a dynamic community of expert consultants revolutionizing Customer Experience, Data, and AI across various sectors, including retail, finance, manufacturing, life sciences, and public services.




We have a significant international presence, with 20% of our activity extending beyond Belgium, and this number continues to grow. In this role, you will act as a key member of the dynamic Functional Data Architecture Consulting team with 12 enthusiastic consultants. You’ll dive into exciting data and functional analysis missions across diverse and impactful projects.




Acting as a bridge between business and data teams, you will analyze business needs, define and validate data models, and ensure data usage and dashboards are aligned with real business processes and KPIs.




We value people who are proactive on every level, deliver exceptional results, work well with others, and make a clear impact on our clients and our business. If you love collaborating, learning, sharing your expertise, and being recognized for your achievements, you’ll fit right in with our team.




Check out some of our ongoing projects:

Rebuilding the Customer Relationship Management (CRM) and Customer Data Platform (CDP) systems in the aviation industry.
Refactoring the Fund Management application in a financial institution by modernizing data storage for improved efficiency and enhancing data visualization.
Data Management projects in retail, transport, and financial sectors.




Your key areas of responsibility as a Functional Data Analyst Consultant:

Cover data modeling, data process improvement, data analysis, and data management within assigned projects.
Translate business requirements into clear functional data specifications.
Define, design, and validate data models in collaboration with technical teams.
Optimize dashboards, KPIs, and data usage from a business perspective.
Ensure data consistency across reports, tools, and domains.
Building documentation and testing implementations of the innovative solutions.
Stakeholder management: Meeting and managing customer needs and expectations.
Act as a trusted consultant, advising clients on data structures, usage, and best practices to meet their business objectives.




What this role is not:

You are not expected to build data pipelines or design technical architectures.
You are not responsible for complex SQL, ETL development, or coding-heavy tasks.
You influence and validate data structures rather than implementing them.




Who you are:

Your interest & soft skills

A good affinity with and interest in technological solutions, with the capability to communicate effectively with different stakeholders.
Analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.
Effectively operate in workshop and interview contexts.
Autonomous and solutions oriented.
Dynamic team player.
Strong communication and presentation skills in French and/or Dutch + English.




Your technical experience

At least 4-5 years hands-on experience in Data & Functional analysis in the context of large-scale IT implementation projects.
Experience in the following domains: Functional analysis, design and testing, data modeling (classical data modeling as well as star schema modeling), data change management.
Experience in one or more of the following operational or analytical business areas: marketing automation & optimization, digital and social Marketing, sales force automation, marketing analysis, sales reporting, dashboarding, benchmarking, customer servicing dashboarding, general reporting & analysis, data analytics & visualization, data quality, data analysis and data science, GDPR compliance.
Experience in working in an Agile environment.
Experience in tool implementation: CRM tools, BI reporting & analysis tools, portal environments and tools, data governance (metadata, MDM, data quality).
Proficient reporting and documentation skills.
Fluency in SQL and other programming languages is highly desirable.




Why you should join us:

Some of the rewards of working at Orange Business

A community of experts - as Digital Natives, we put innovation at the heart of our expertise. Continuously seeking new ideas, we proudly host regular “Guild” workshops and knowledge-sharing sessions through our Data Meetups.
Career growth possibilities.
We may work with tomorrow’s technology, but at our core we are a people-oriented organization.
An open and inclusive culture, that enables us to reach our full potential.
Hybrid ways of working and modern & collaborative office in Brussels.
Compelling advantages including a flexible mobility with possibility for a housing budget, representation and expense allowances…
Read more about the benefits we offer here.




Ready to build with us?

We screen applications on an ongoing basis, with the aim of conducting conversations and interviews as we receive them.




Apply now

We can't wait to meet you!




More about Orange Business:

HTML:
Functional Data Analyst Consultant at Orange Business

Permanent contract

FR or NL + EN


Ever wondered what it feels like to be at the cutting edge of digital innovation? Our Digital & Data team at Orange Business is a dynamic community of expert consultants revolutionizing Customer Experience, Data, and AI across various sectors, including retail, finance, manufacturing, life sciences, and public services.


We have a significant international presence, with 20% of our activity extending beyond Belgium, and this number continues to grow. In this role, you will act as a key member of the dynamic Functional Data Architecture Consulting team with 12 enthusiastic consultants. You’ll dive into exciting data and functional analysis missions across diverse and impactful projects.


Acting as a bridge between business and data teams, you will analyze business needs, define and validate data models, and ensure data usage and dashboards are aligned with real business processes and KPIs.


We value people who are proactive on every level, deliver exceptional results, work well with others, and make a clear impact on our clients and our business. If you love collaborating, learning, sharing your expertise, and being recognized for your achievements, you’ll fit right in with our team.


Check out some of our ongoing projects:

  • Rebuilding the Customer Relationship Management (CRM) and Customer Data Platform (CDP) systems in the aviation industry.
  • Refactoring the Fund Management application in a financial institution by modernizing data storage for improved efficiency and enhancing data visualization.
  • Data Management projects in retail, transport, and financial sectors.


Your key areas of responsibility as a Functional Data Analyst Consultant:

  • Cover data modeling, data process improvement, data analysis, and data management within assigned projects.
  • Translate business requirements into clear functional data specifications.
  • Define, design, and validate data models in collaboration with technical teams.
  • Optimize dashboards, KPIs, and data usage from a business perspective.
  • Ensure data consistency across reports, tools, and domains.
  • Building documentation and testing implementations of the innovative solutions.
  • Stakeholder management: Meeting and managing customer needs and expectations.
  • Act as a trusted consultant, advising clients on data structures, usage, and best practices to meet their business objectives.


What this role is not:

  • You are not expected to build data pipelines or design technical architectures.
  • You are not responsible for complex SQL, ETL development, or coding-heavy tasks.
  • You influence and validate data structures rather than implementing them.


Who you are:

Your interest & soft skills

  • A good affinity with and interest in technological solutions, with the capability to communicate effectively with different stakeholders.
  • Analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.
  • Effectively operate in workshop and interview contexts.
  • Autonomous and solutions oriented.
  • Dynamic team player.
  • Strong communication and presentation skills in French and/or Dutch + English.


Your technical experience

  • At least 4-5 years hands-on experience in Data & Functional analysis in the context of large-scale IT implementation projects.
  • Experience in the following domains: Functional analysis, design and testing, data modeling (classical data modeling as well as star schema modeling), data change management.
  • Experience in one or more of the following operational or analytical business areas: marketing automation & optimization, digital and social Marketing, sales force automation, marketing analysis, sales reporting, dashboarding, benchmarking, customer servicing dashboarding, general reporting & analysis, data analytics & visualization, data quality, data analysis and data science, GDPR compliance.
  • Experience in working in an Agile environment.
  • Experience in tool implementation: CRM tools, BI reporting & analysis tools, portal environments and tools, data governance (metadata, MDM, data quality).
  • Proficient reporting and documentation skills.
  • Fluency in SQL and other programming languages is highly desirable.


Why you should join us:

Some of the rewards of working at Orange Business

  • A community of experts - as Digital Natives, we put innovation at the heart of our expertise. Continuously seeking new ideas, we proudly host regular “Guild” workshops and knowledge-sharing sessions through our Data Meetups.
  • Career growth possibilities.
  • We may work with tomorrow’s technology, but at our core we are a people-oriented organization.
  • An open and inclusive culture, that enables us to reach our full potential.
  • Hybrid ways of working and modern & collaborative office in Brussels.
  • Compelling advantages including a flexible mobility with possibility for a housing budget, representation and expense allowances…
  • Read more about the benefits we offer here.


Ready to build with us?

We screen applications on an ongoing basis, with the aim of conducting conversations and interviews as we receive them.

Title: Junior Performance Management Analyst
Description:
Eurofins is an international network of independent life sciences companies, providing a unique range of analytical testing services to clients across multiple industries, to make life and our environment safer, healthier and more sustainable. From the food you eat, to the water you drink, to the medicines you rely on, Eurofins laboratories work with the biggest companies in the world to ensure the products they supply are safe, their ingredients are authentic, and labelling is accurate.



The Eurofins network is dedicated to delivering testing services that contribute to the health and safety of society and the planet, and to its corporate responsibility to protect the environment and ensure diversity, equity, and inclusion across its companies around the world.





Job Description



Are you passionate about turning data into actionable insights and performance improvement? Do you enjoy projects that combine analytics, process optimisation, and stakeholder collaboration? If so, this could be your next role.



We are looking for a Junior Performance Management Analyst to join the team at our corporate office in Brussels, Belgium.



In this role, you will help define and monitor performance across our corporate offices. Your work will have a direct impact on further improving the services that support Eurofins laboratories worldwide.



Responsibilities



As part of our team, you will work on continuous improvement projects to advance performance excellence across the organisation. You will:



Define performance measurement processes.



Help define and implement KPIs that measure the cost efficiency, quality, and timeliness of corporate offices.



Design reporting solutions that provide stakeholders with clear visibility on performance and business outcomes.



Establish audit processes to strengthen governance and operational effectiveness.



Leverage data and process analysis to identify trends, generate actionable insights, and uncover opportunities for improvement.



Conduct root cause analyses to address performance challenges and support sustainable corrective actions.



Partner closely with IT and digital transformation teams to develop and enhance tools, dashboards, and data-driven solutions.



Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders across the organisation to drive improvements and support business transformation initiatives.



What’s in for you?



A unique opportunity to gain experience within a fast‑growing, international organisation.
Exposure to a wide range of corporate departments, providing a comprehensive understanding of how a global organisation operates.

You will join an international office ideally located near parks and green spaces, bringing together colleagues from over 35 nationalities. Additionally, we offer an attractive compensation package and extra-legal benefits that include covered transportation costs, organic meals, sports activities, and annual office events.



Location: Brussels, Belgium. Curious about where you’ll be working? Tour the office: Meet the office - Brussels, Belgium.





Qualifications



Education: Recently graduated with a master’s degree from a top science, engineering or management university.



Language Proficiency: Fluent in English (oral and written). Additional languages are a plus.



Technical Skills: Strong proficiency in MS Office, especially with Excel. Power BI is desirable. Proficiency in data analysis, data visualisation, and reporting tools.



Personal Skills: Strong problem-solver with “can-do” attitude, strong analytical skills, curious and detail-oriented, conceptual thinking skills.





Additional Information



We support your development! Do you feel like you don’t match 100% of the requirements? Don’t hesitate to apply anyway! Eurofins companies are committed to supporting your career development.



We embrace diversity!  As an Equal Opportunity Employer, the Eurofins network of companies believes in strength and innovation through diversity. We prohibit discrimination against employees or applications based on gender identity and/or expression, race, nationality, age, religion, sexual orientation, disability, and everything else that makes employees of Eurofins companies unique.



Find out more on our Careers page: https://careers.eurofins.com/



Company description: Eurofins Scientific is an international network of independent life sciences companies, providing a unique range of analytical testing services to clients across multiple industries, to make life and our environment safer, healthier and more sustainable. From the food you eat, to the water you drink, to the medicines you rely on, Eurofins laboratories work with the biggest companies in the world to ensure the products they supply are safe, their ingredients are authentic and labelling is accurate.

The Eurofins network of companies believes that it is a global leader in food, environment, pharmaceutical and cosmetic product testing and in discovery pharmacology, forensics, advanced material sciences and agroscience contract research services. It is also one of the market leaders in certain testing and laboratory services for genomics, and in the support of clinical studies, as well as in biopharma contract development and manufacturing. It also has a rapidly developing presence in highly specialised and molecular clinical diagnostic testing and in-vitro diagnostic products.



In under 40 years, Eurofins has grown from one laboratory in Nantes, France to over 65,000 employees across a decentralised and entrepreneurial network of over 950 laboratories in over 1,000 independent companies across 59 countries. Eurofins companies offer a portfolio of over 200,000 analytical methods to evaluate the safety, identity, composition, authenticity, origin, traceability and purity of biological substances and products.



The Eurofins network generated EUR 7.296 billion revenues in FY 2025, and has been one of the fastest growing listed European companies for almost three decades.

Title: Data & Marketing Analyst
Description:
At A-Players, we are building a new AI product in the mental health and mindfulness space - and we are looking for a Data & Marketing Analyst who will own the analytics layer from day one.

We are not building analytics after the fact. We are building it before the first ad goes live. That means clean tracking from day one, attribution that reflects reality, and dashboards the performance team opens every morning - not once a quarter.

In practice, you will set up the full tracking infrastructure from scratch, own the attribution model, connect marketing spend to real revenue, and keep LTV and retention metrics current and trustworthy. You will work side by side with the performance marketing team.

You will be the dedicated analyst on this product. Around you is a mature data team of 10+ people across Data Analytics, Data Engineering, Data Science, BI, and AI, as well as shared infrastructure including BigQuery, dbt, Tableau, and GA4.

This role is a fit if you have navigated the full analytics stack at a fast-moving web subscription product, know what it takes to build trust in data from scratch, and want to do meaningful work on something that is just getting started.

Responsibilities


Build the web tracking foundation for the product. Set up GA4 and GTM, both client-side and server-side, from scratch: UTM conventions, dataLayer schema, event deduplication, and attribution windows.
Implement server-side conversions for paid channels, including Meta CAPI, Google Enhanced Conversions, and TikTok Events API.
Build and maintain a web attribution model that connects marketing spend to actual revenue, and deliver channel-level LTV and CAC analysis.
Design and own Tableau dashboards for acquisition metrics, including CAC, ROAS, and channel mix; funnel metrics, including registration → first purchase → repeat purchase; retention metrics, including cohorts and LTV curves; and financial overview.
Support the performance marketing team with ad-hoc SQL analysis and Python scripts for automation.
Design and analyze A/B tests across the funnel, document results, and help drive action based on findings.
Document tracking conventions and data definitions in the knowledge base.


What We Look For


3+ years of experience as a Data Analyst, Web Analyst, or Marketing Analyst working with web subscription or e-commerce products.
Strong web analytics expertise, including funnels, attribution, tracking logic, and marketing performance measurement.
Experience working with performance marketing data.
Experience working with raw and semi-processed data from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or similar platforms, including data validation and cleanup.
Strong understanding of subscription business metrics, including LTV, cohorts, retention, recurring revenue, CAC, and payback period.
Experience setting up web tracking from scratch at least once, for example for a new product, new funnel, or new market.
Advanced SQL: window functions, CTEs, query optimization, and working with complex datasets.
Proficient Python: pandas, NumPy, analysis scripts, and basic automation.
Experience with BI tools such as Tableau, Looker, Power BI, or similar.
Comfortable working with ambiguity and building structure where it does not yet exist.
Open, clear, and proactive communication style.
High level of ownership and initiative.


Nice to have


Hands-on experience with GA4.
Hands-on experience with GTM, including client-side and server-side implementation.
Experience working with PSP data.
A/B testing experience.
Tableau experience: dashboards, calculated fields, parameter actions.
BigQuery experience: large datasets, cost optimization, and performance optimization.
Experience with dbt.


We Offer


Full ownership and freedom to set up tracking from scratch for a new product, with no legacy constraints.
Real business impact: you will work directly with a performance team running live campaigns, not build dashboards no one reads.
A strong data team around you (10+people), including Data Engineers, Data Scientists, BI specialists, and AI specialists to consult with, learn from, and grow with.
Modern stack: SQL, BigQuery, dbt, GA4, GTM, Tableau, and Python.
Openness to new practices: the team has already implemented GrowthBook, dbt, server-side tracking, and GenAI skills for analysts.
Career path: strong performance opens the door to real career growth.
Flexible schedule and remote-friendly setup.
Competitive compensation with regular performance reviews.
Medical insurance.
Learning and development budget.

You will be the dedicated analyst on this product. Around you is a mature data team of 10+ people across Data Analytics, Data Engineering, Data Science, BI, and AI, as well as shared infrastructure including BigQuery, dbt, Tableau, and GA4.

This role is a fit if you have navigated the full analytics stack at a fast-moving web subscription product, know what it takes to build trust in data from scratch, and want to do meaningful work on something that is just getting started.

Responsibilities

  • Build the web tracking foundation for the product. Set up GA4 and GTM, both client-side and server-side, from scratch: UTM conventions, dataLayer schema, event deduplication, and attribution windows.
  • Implement server-side conversions for paid channels, including Meta CAPI, Google Enhanced Conversions, and TikTok Events API.
  • Build and maintain a web attribution model that connects marketing spend to actual revenue, and deliver channel-level LTV and CAC analysis.
  • Design and own Tableau dashboards for acquisition metrics, including CAC, ROAS, and channel mix; funnel metrics, including registration → first purchase → repeat purchase; retention metrics, including cohorts and LTV curves; and financial overview.
  • Support the performance marketing team with ad-hoc SQL analysis and Python scripts for automation.
  • Design and analyze A/B tests across the funnel, document results, and help drive action based on findings.
  • Document tracking conventions and data definitions in the knowledge base.

What We Look For

  • 3+ years of experience as a Data Analyst, Web Analyst, or Marketing Analyst working with web subscription or e-commerce products.
  • Strong web analytics expertise, including funnels, attribution, tracking logic, and marketing performance measurement.
  • Experience working with performance marketing data.
  • Experience working with raw and semi-processed data from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or similar platforms, including data validation and cleanup.
  • Strong understanding of subscription business metrics, including LTV, cohorts, retention, recurring revenue, CAC, and payback period.
  • Experience setting up web tracking from scratch at least once, for example for a new product, new funnel, or new market.
  • Advanced SQL: window functions, CTEs, query optimization, and working with complex datasets.
  • Proficient Python: pandas, NumPy, analysis scripts, and basic automation.
  • Experience with BI tools such as Tableau, Looker, Power BI, or similar.
  • Comfortable working with ambiguity and building structure where it does not yet exist.
  • Open, clear, and proactive communication style.
  • High level of ownership and initiative.

Nice to have

  • Hands-on experience with GA4.
  • Hands-on experience with GTM, including client-side and server-side implementation.
  • Experience working with PSP data.
  • A/B testing experience.
  • Tableau experience: dashboards, calculated fields, parameter actions.
  • BigQuery experience: large datasets, cost optimization, and performance optimization.
  • Experience with dbt.

We Offer

  • Full ownership and freedom to set up tracking from scratch for a new product, with no legacy constraints.
  • Real business impact: you will work directly with a performance team running live campaigns, not build dashboards no one reads.
  • A strong data team around you (10+people), including Data Engineers, Data Scientists, BI specialists, and AI specialists to consult with, learn from, and grow with.
  • Modern stack: SQL, BigQuery, dbt, GA4, GTM, Tableau, and Python.
  • Openness to new practices: the team has already implemented GrowthBook, dbt, server-side tracking, and GenAI skills for analysts.
  • Career path: strong performance opens the door to real career growth.
  • Flexible schedule and remote-friendly setup.
  • Competitive compensation with regular performance reviews.
  • Medical insurance.
  • Learning and development budget.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
theMiracle is behavioral intelligence and distribution infrastructure for wallets and ecosystems. We match user behavior with tailored campaigns and exclusive rewards inside wallets, helping brands reach more relevant users while wallets increase engagement and retention through native, claimable rewards.




Core Responsibility

Independently scope and run analyses from a business question to a finished, presentation-ready deliverable
On-chain analysis: segmentation, holder snapshots, vertical overlap, behavioral classification
Funnel and attribution analysis, including user-level attribution logic
Own the data-visualization and reporting layer → build and maintain dashboards for internal stakeholders and design polished, client-facing report visuals for campaign performance and holder intelligence.

Must-Have Skills

Advanced SQL (both MySQL and Postgres) and Python for data work
Strong data-visualization and storytelling skills: fluent in at least one BI/dashboarding tool (e.g. Tableau, or Power BI) and a code-based library.
Can turn a dataset into a clear report a non-technical client or exec immediately understands, and knows when a number needs context vs. when it speaks for itself.
Methodology judgment: catches inconsistencies and can defend a number's definition to a skeptical stakeholder.
Comfortable working directly with internal and external stakeholders and translating between their asks and the data.

Nice To Have

Experience with blockchain data

Title: Energy Data Engineer
Description:
Drive the energy transition by providing sustainable technological solutions through our digital platform, facilitating from planning to contract tendering to implementation of renewable energy systems in real estate and industrial companies. We enable our customers to increase the economic efficiency and CO2 balance of their properties while minimizing technical, commercial, and energy risks.

To strengthen our Product Management team, we are looking for an Energy Data Engineer who will contribute to the analysis of energy data, support the development of AI-based price and energy forecasts, and generate actionable insights through the analysis of operational and financial performance. Join this energy revolution and help us transform energy supply in a sustainable and economically viable way!

Tasks


Analyze energy data, including production, consumption, and storage information to identify optimization opportunities
Conduct AI-based market analysis and contribute to the development of price and energy forecasts
Analyze asset data, project progress and operational, environmental as well as financial performance
Perform trend, variance and gap analysis, translating findings into actionable recommendations
Validate data quality and perform plausibility checks to continuously improve analytical outputs
Develop and maintain a central data and reporting infrastructure in close collaboration with IT teams
Create KPI reports, dashboards, and data visualizations for internal and external stakeholders
Collaborate closely with management, operations, sales, and country organizations to support data-driven decision-making


Requirements


Completed degree in Data Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Business Informatics, Physics, Industrial Engineering or a related field.
2–4 years of experience as a Data Analyst, ideally within an energy sector
Proven experience in designing and building purpose-driven analytical environments
Experienced in building data pipelines, including data extraction, validation, transformation and loading
Practical experience working with modern cloud data infrastructures such as BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift, or Databricks
Ideally, experience applying and training AI models and machine learning algorithms
High level of proficiency in SQL and solid experience with Python, R, MATLAB or comparable programming languages


Benefits


An attractive salary package and exciting development opportunities
Further training in renewable energies and project management
A dynamic, innovative environment with short decision-making processes and plenty of creative freedom
The opportunity to play an active role in the energy transition and make a real impact

Title: Data Analyst Supply chain & Service client
HTML:
QoQa c'est avant tout près d'un million de membres de notre magnifique Qommunauté qui découvrent chaque jour des offres dénichées par nos équipes. Notre mission: émerveiller au quotidien.


Notre succès, nous le devons au formidable travail de nos plus de 240 loutres qui s'engagent quotidiennement pour proposer des expériences, des découvertes et un service au top.


Aujourd'hui, nous recherchons la perle rare pour rejoindre l'équipe Data!


Ta mission, si tu l'acceptes:

Ton objectif : transformer la donnée brute en levier stratégique afin de soutenir les départements Opérations (supply chain et service client). Tu garantiras la fiabilité des indicateurs clés pour permettre un pilotage proactif et éclairé de notre organisation.


  • Maîtrise complète de la pipeline : Tu pilotes l'intégralité du cycle de vie de la donnée, de la préparation de l'extraction (en collaboration avec les Data Engineers) depuis le DWH jusqu'à la mise en production de dashboards dans notre outil BI;
  • Partenaire Business : En étroite collaboration avec les équipes métiers, tu traduis leurs enjeux opérationnels en solutions analytiques concrètes;
  • Projets : Tu participeras également à différents projets transverses de l'équipe, selon les priorités du moment.


Parlons de toi et de tes compétences:


Ton expérience - Ta formation

  • Bachelor ou Master en informatique, gestion des données, ingénierie ou domaine équivalent;
  • Minimum 7 ans d'expérience confirmée en Data Analysis / BI, incluant une expertise approfondie des flux Supply Chain (stocks, approvisionnements) et du Service Client (KPIs de satisfaction, suivi de la performance des services de support).


Tes compétences - Tes qualités

  • Excellente maîtrise de SQL et des bases de données relationnelles;
  • Expérience confirmée avec DBT (Data Build Tool) et maîtrise de Github pour le versioning;
  • Bonne maîtrise Tableau Software, pour transformer les données en rapports clairs et percutantsM
  • Une connaissance approfondie de l'ERP Odoo (structure des tables sous-jacentes) est un atout majeur;
  • Approche rigoureuse et orientée résultats;
  • Capacité à collaborer avec les métiers, à comprendre leurs besoins et à concevoir des solutions pertinentes.


Le plus : ton appétence pour l'IA et les outils modernes est sans limite!


Pour que tu restes au taQuet, on t'offre:

  • Un environnement sain, où tu peux être toi même;
  • Une ambiance et un cadre de travail agréables dans notre QG;
  • Un temps de travail flexible, et hybride;
  • Un système de gouvernance en Holacratie;
  • Des avantages sociaux aux petits oignons (5 semaines de vacances, LPP avantageuse, congé maternité et paternité au-delà du minimum légal, etc.);
  • Des rabais et contributions financières: plan de mobilité, abonnement téléphone, repas de midi, sport, etc.);
  • La possibilité de formation continue et de développement.


Les prochaines étapes

Tu as ce qu'il faut pour relever ce défi ?


Pour entrer dans la course, envoie nous au minimum un CV et une lettre ou vidéo de motivation personnalisée. Fais parler ta créativité et raconte-nous pourquoi tu serais la personne idéale pour ce job. Quels sont tes super-pouvoirs: Surprends-nous!


Nous te remercions (et la planète aussi) de ne pas envoyer de candidature papier.


Si ton parcours correspond avec nos attentes pour le poste, nous t'inviterons à découvrir notre tanière et à nous faire découvrir tes talents sur le terrain. Dans le cas contraire, tu recevras un e-mail dans les meilleurs délais.


Alors maintenant, jette-toi à l'eau et tente ta chance!


Entrée en fonction : dès que possible (mais on pourra t'attendre si tu es la bonne personne)

Type de contrat : CDI

Taux: 100%

Lieu : Bussigny

Title: Data Product Analyst
Description:
Who is Sonar?

Sonar is driving the future of agent-centric software development. As the leader in AI code review and verification, we solve a critical problem: ensuring that software generated by AI-assisted developers or autonomous agents is reliable, secure, and maintainable.

Integrating seamlessly with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Gemini, and Devin, we help over 75% of the Fortune 100 build trusted, reliable, compliant software. Customers who use Sonar are 44% less likely to report an outage due to AI-generated code.

We believe code verification is the critical missing link in the Agent-Centric Development Cycle (AC/DC). Industry giants like Nvidia, ServiceNow, Booking.com, Goldman Sachs, AstraZeneca, and Ford Motor Company count on us to provide independent, explainable, consistent review and governance of their AI-generated code via products like:


SonarQube: The world’s leading AI code review and verification platform.
SonarQube Foundation Agent: Currently topping the leaderboards for agentic software repair.
SonarSweep & Sonar Context Augmentation: Providing the enterprise-grade context and constraints agents need to be truly effective.


Our team operates across global hubs in Austin, Bochum, Dubai, Geneva, London, Singapore, Tokyo, and Washington D.C. We move with a mindset we call CODE:


Committed to our customers and community.
Obsessed with quality.
Deliberate in our decisions.
Effective as one team.


With over $400M in revenue and profitable, fast-paced growth, we are building the backbone of the AI software revolution. If you’re hungry to have an impact, want to build at a fast pace, and ready to work at the forefront of AI, we want to hear from you.

Position Description

We want to make the best decisions possible for our products and to do that, we need to make data-driven decisions. To achieve this goal, we are looking for a talented Data Product Analyst (DPA) to join the product data team.

As a Data Product Analyst (DPA) at Sonar, you are the architect of clarity and the bridge between technical infrastructure and business value. We treat Data as a Product, and you own its entire lifecycle—from dbt SQL modeling and event-based schema governance to high-level executive storytelling.

You will thrive in this role if you enjoy a "full-stack" analytical challenge. You are a proactive project leader who owns the data relationship for your domain. Depending on the need, your impact ranges from empowering users through data literacy to leading complex, end-to-end analyses from initial conception to the final executive summary.

What You Will Do

We believe in using the best tools to stay effective and efficient. You will be expected to master and evolve our data ecosystem:

Data project ownership & delivery


Managing a data project from start to finish using agile method, helping stakeholders to refine their questions into structured data projects, advising stakeholders on what to measure, how to model it, and which tool to use and facilitating stakeholder discussions to bridge the gap between high-level business goals and technical execution.
Deliver end-to-end solutions, from ingestion and modeling to executive-ready visualizations and summaries.


Technical stack


Modeling & Architecture: Build and maintain scalable models using dbt (SQL) and provide critical reviews of event schemas for our event-based platform.
Modern Workflow: Leverage Cursor and Git to accelerate development and ensure code quality and version control.
The Analytics Hub: Own the user experience across Mixpanel, Tableau, Metabase, and Atlan, ensuring they serve as the single source of truth.


Data literacy


Onboarding and empowering users to self-serve, effectively reducing the data team as a bottleneck.
Handling ad-hoc requests ranging from querying specific datasets to providing technical guidance on our tooling landscape.
Participating in Level 1 technical support, including monitoring dbt jobs and Tableau extracts to ensure the reliability of our products.
Project Governance: Use Jira for roadmap visibility and dbt/Confluence/Atlan for high-quality documentation that survives handoffs.


Continuous Platform Improvement

You act as a bridge to Data Engineering. You’ll proactively surface data debt, documentation gaps, and tooling friction to ensure the platform gets simpler and stronger over time. You’ll also ensure high standards for PII handling and security policies are built into every delivery.

Experience And Qualifications


Proven Experience: 3–5+ years in a Data Analyst, Data Product Analyst, or Analytics Engineering role, ideally within a fast-paced environment.
Project Leadership: A track record of taking the lead with stakeholders—successfully translating high-level business ambiguity into structured, actionable data projects. You have experience helping and mentoring other team members.
Technical Mastery: hands-on experience with dbt / SQL (modeling, testing, and documentation).
Hands-on experience with data visualization tools (Tableau, Mixpanel, or similar)
The Modern Workflow: Comfort working in a version-controlled environment (Git) and an interest in leveraging AI-assisted development tools (like Cursor) to drive efficiency.
Data Storytelling: The ability to distill complex findings into executive-ready summaries and visualizations that drive decision-making.
You are fluent in English both written and spoken
Familiarity with data governance practices (PII handling and access control).
Experience in Product Analytics or working directly with Product Managers a nice to have
Familiarity with data engineering concepts (Data Lake, Data Warehouse, ETL / ELT pipeline) and tools (AWS, redshift, Lamdas, Databricks) is a plus


We value diversity, equity, and inclusion

At Sonar, we believe that our diversity is our strength. We are a global company that values and respects different backgrounds, perspectives, and cultures. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive work environment where everyone feels valued and empowered to contribute their best. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and welcome all qualified applicants, regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

If you need any accommodation, please reach out to us at [email protected] .

All offers of employment at Sonar are contingent upon the results of a comprehensive background check and reference verification conducted before the start date.

We do not currently support visa candidates in the US.

Applications that are submitted through agencies or third party recruiters will not be considered.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Analyst (F/H/X)
Description:
Amaris recrute un Data Analyst Logistique (H/F) chargé de collecter, exploiter et interpréter les données afin de définir des orientations et optimiser nos performances logistiques. La personne retenue rejoindra nos équipes bbasées à Genève.




🔥 Vos responsabilités :

Contribuer aux projets logistiques en apportant une expertise en analyse de données.
Comprendre les enjeux métier et les traduire en problématiques analytiques, avec de fréquents déplacements sur le terrain.
Cartographier les flux et identifier les données et points de mesure pertinents.
Extraire les données depuis les systèmes sources (SAP, MES, LES, équipements).
Préparer, structurer et fiabiliser les données pour les rendre exploitables.
Réaliser des analyses de données et utiliser des modèles de simulation pour proposer des pistes d’optimisation logistique.
Concevoir des tableaux de bord et indicateurs de performance.
Présenter les résultats de manière claire grâce à des outils de data visualisation.
Assurer une veille sur les outils d’analyse et de simulation.







🎯 Votre profil

Formation d’ingénieur HES, EPF ou équivalent.
Goût prononcé pour le terrain et la collecte du besoin auprès des opérationnels.
Maîtrise des outils d’analyse de données (Power BI ou Tableau Prep, KNIME ou équivalent), de visualisation (Tableau) et des bases de données (SQL).
Excellentes capacités d’organisation et forte autonomie.
Aisance relationnelle, sens de l’écoute, capacité de synthèse et bonne communication.
Dynamisme, flexibilité et esprit d’équipe.







💡Ce que nous offrons

Une communauté internationale réunissant plus de 110 nationalités différentes, avec une forte présence en Suisse et en Europe.
Un environnement où la confiance a une place centrale : 70% de nos leaders clés ont commencé leur carrière au premier niveau de responsabilités.
Un système de formation solide avec notre Académie interne et plus de 250 modules disponibles, adaptés aux enjeux industriels suisses.
Des engagements forts en matière de RSE, notamment par le biais de notre programme WeCare Together.




Amaris Consulting s’engage à promouvoir la diversité au sein de ses effectifs et à créer un environnement de travail inclusif. Nous étudions les candidatures de toutes personnes qualifiées, indépendamment de leur sexe, orientation sexuelle, race, origine ethnique, croyances, âge, état civil, handicap ou toute autre caractéristique. Une attention particulière sera portée aux candidats en situation de handicap .




Postulez dès maintenant et rejoignez notre équipe pour une carrière enrichissante et stimulante !

  • Cartographier les flux et identifier les données et points de mesure pertinents.
  • Extraire les données depuis les systèmes sources (SAP, MES, LES, équipements).
  • Préparer, structurer et fiabiliser les données pour les rendre exploitables.
  • Réaliser des analyses de données et utiliser des modèles de simulation pour proposer des pistes d’optimisation logistique.
  • Concevoir des tableaux de bord et indicateurs de performance.
  • Présenter les résultats de manière claire grâce à des outils de data visualisation.
  • Assurer une veille sur les outils d’analyse et de simulation.



🎯 Votre profil

  • Formation d’ingénieur HES, EPF ou équivalent.
  • Goût prononcé pour le terrain et la collecte du besoin auprès des opérationnels.
  • Maîtrise des outils d’analyse de données (Power BI ou Tableau Prep, KNIME ou équivalent), de visualisation (Tableau) et des bases de données (SQL).
  • Excellentes capacités d’organisation et forte autonomie.
  • Aisance relationnelle, sens de l’écoute, capacité de synthèse et bonne communication.
  • Dynamisme, flexibilité et esprit d’équipe.



💡Ce que nous offrons

  • Une communauté internationale réunissant plus de 110 nationalités différentes, avec une forte présence en Suisse et en Europe.
  • Un environnement où la confiance a une place centrale : 70% de nos leaders clés ont commencé leur carrière au premier niveau de responsabilités.
  • Un système de formation solide avec notre Académie interne et plus de 250 modules disponibles, adaptés aux enjeux industriels suisses.
  • Des engagements forts en matière de RSE, notamment par le biais de notre programme WeCare Together.


Amaris Consulting s’engage à promouvoir la diversité au sein de ses effectifs et à créer un environnement de travail inclusif. Nous étudions les candidatures de toutes personnes qualifiées, indépendamment de leur sexe, orientation sexuelle, race, origine ethnique, croyances, âge, état civil, handicap ou toute autre caractéristique. Une attention particulière sera portée aux candidats en situation de handicap .


Postulez dès maintenant et rejoignez notre équipe pour une carrière enrichissante et stimulante !

Title: Analyste (h/f) 80-100%
Description:
Tu maîtrises les analyses de données et tu souhaites les appliquer à des problématiques concrètes au cœur du métier d’assurance? Postule et fais-toi connaître de nos spécialistes en recrutement.

TA MISSION


En concevant et en faisant évoluer des tableaux de bord dynamiques Power BI, tu contribues au pilotage des Sinistres en temps réel
Grâce à l’élaboration et à la mise en place d’analyses avancées et d’outils d’aide à la décision, tu soutiens les décisions stratégiques et opérationnelles
En élaborant des supports de présentation à forte valeur ajoutée (management, direction), tu valorises les analyses réalisées et facilites la prise de décision à haut niveau
En explorant, structurant et valorisant les données avec Databricks, tu identifies des tendances, détectes des anomalies et génères des insights à forte valeur ajoutée
Parce que tu prends part à des projets analytiques (optimisation des coûts, détection de fraude, automatisation), tu contribues à l’amélioration de la performance et à la maîtrise des coûts
En construisant et en maintenant des sources de données fiables et exploitables, tu soutiens la réalisation d’analyses économiques et techniques


Tes qualités


Avec une approche structurée et rigoureuse, tu produis des reportings et des analyses de haute qualité
Parce que tu évolues dans un environnement technologique varié, tu apportes des réponses pertinentes et adaptées aux besoins
Grâce à ton esprit collaboratif, tu travailles efficacement en équipe et favorises les échanges avec les interlocuteurs métier et IT
Par ton sens de l’organisation et des priorités, tu respectes les délais et assures la livraison de résultats fiables dans des contextes exigeants
Parce que tu utilises régulièrement des LLM, tu accélères les développements et optimises la production d’analyses


Ton expérience


En disposant d’une formation HES, EPF, université – économie, finance, statistique ou équivalente, tu appliques des méthodes avancées
Grâce à ton expérience avec Power BI, SQL et Python, tu participes à la mise en œuvre d’outils analytiques


Pour nous rejoindre


Partage-nous ton vécu avec ton CV

Pourquoi rejoindre notre équipe?

Si toi aussi tu aimes l’entraide, la bienveillance et la proactivité, tu es à la bonne place !

Ici, au Groupe Mutuel, nous t’offrons la possibilité d’exercer une activité variée tout en favorisant un environnement de travail stimulant qui concilie ta vie privée ainsi que professionnelle. Tu te retrouveras dans une équipe où travail, rigueur mais surtout plaisir sont les mots d’ordre.

Title: Analytics Engineer (w/m/d)
Description:
Wir bei pfister verfolgen eine Vision: Wir möchten mit unseren Produkten in jedes Schweizer Zuhause einziehen. Dafür suchen wir tatkräftige Unterstützung. Hilf uns, die rund 100'000 Produkte unseres Sortiments ins Zuhause unserer Kundinnen und Kunden zu bringen. Ob in einer der 18 Filialen oder in der Zentrale in Suhr: Dich erwartet ein einmaliges Umfeld, die Arbeit mit ansprechenden Produkten und nicht zuletzt Wertschätzung und Respekt für deinen Einsatz. Bei pfister arbeiten heute schon rund 1'400 Personen - kommst du bald dazu?

Was Wir Bieten


Ein kleines Team mit kurzen Entscheidungswegen und direkter Anbindung an die Geschäftsleitung
Deine Arbeit fliesst direkt in Management-Entscheidungen und operative Prozesse ein
Wir setzen AI im Arbeitsalltag produktiv ein (z.B. für Analysen und Automatisierung) und bauen das weiter aus
Breites Themenspektrum: Infrastruktur, CRM und Kampagnen, NPS, Pricing — und vieles mehr
Ein moderner Data-Stack auf Azure, den wir aktiv aufbauen — inklusive laufender Migrationsprojekte; du kannst Architektur und Tooling mitprägen


Deine neuen Aufgaben


Aufbau und kontinuierliche Weiterentwicklung einer zukunftsfähigen Datenbasis als Grundlage für datenbasierte und kundenorientierte Entscheidungen im gesamten Unternehmen
Konzeption, Entwicklung und Betrieb moderner Datenpipelines sowie ETL-/ELT-Prozesse und aktive Mitgestaltung der Datenarchitektur
Entwicklung datengetriebener Modelle, Analysen und Handlungsempfehlungen mit direktem Mehrwert für strategische und operative Entscheidungen
Fachliche Leitung von Erweiterungs- und Einführungsprojekten sowie Mitwirkung an der Umsetzung innovativer technischer und analytischer Lösungen
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit internen Stakeholdern und Data-Science-Expertinnen und -Experten als kompetente Ansprechperson und Sparringspartner


Das bringst du mit


Erfahrung im Data Engineering, insbesondere im Aufbau von Datenpipelines sowie im Umgang mit Cloud Data Warehouse
Sehr gute SQL-Kenntnisse und fundierte Erfahrung mit relationalen Datenbanken
Gute Python-Kenntnisse für die Entwicklung und Automatisierung datenbezogener Prozesse
Analytische, strukturierte und selbständige Arbeitsweise mit hoher Datenaffinität
Abgeschlossenes Studium in (Wirtschafts-)Informatik, Statistik, Mathematik oder vergleichbare Ausbildung
Erfahrung mit dbt
Erfahrung im Bereich Data Science / statistische Modellierung
Erfahrung im Projektmanagement

Title: MSAT Data Manager (f/m/d) - 80% -100%
Description:
Switzerland, Visp

The actual location of this job is in Visp, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

As our operations continue to expand, we are looking for motivated MSAT Data Managers (m/f/d) to strengthen our Biologics organization and support our customer across multiple projects—including a dedicated manufacturing plant for a conjugated vaccine.

In this role, you will be at the center of data-driven decision-making, ensuring accurate data extraction, transformation, and reporting to enable technical excellence, regulatory compliance, and operational efficiency.

You will also play a key role in safeguarding data integrity, evaluating process performance, and driving continuous improvement initiatives across our manufacturing operations. Your work will directly contribute to robust processes and successful product delivery.

If you are passionate about data, enjoy working in a dynamic, fast-growing environment, and want to make a tangible impact in biologics manufacturing, this is your opportunity. Apply now and be part of our success story in Visp.

This is a fully site‑based role. Working together in person supports close, real‑time collaboration and the technical precision needed to manufacture medicines to the highest quality and safety standards.

What You Will Get


An agile career and a dynamic work culture
An inclusive and ethical workplace
Compensation programs that recognize high performance
The full list of our global benefits can be found here: https://www.lonza.com/careers/benefits


What You Will Do


Extract, structure, and curate manufacturing and batch record data (paper and electronic) to support data-driven decisions
Prepare and analyze process data for investigations, deviations, CAPAs, and continuous improvement initiatives
Develop and maintain dashboards, trackers, and reporting tools (Excel, Power BI) for process monitoring
Support Continuous Process Verification (CPV) and generate control charts and product quality reports
Perform statistical analysis (e.g., trending, capability studies) to evaluate process performance and identify improvement opportunities
Collaborate cross-functionally with MSAT, QA, Operations, and Automation to ensure data accuracy and availability
Contribute to SOPs, technical reports, and regulatory documentation while ensuring GMP/GDP compliance


What We Are Looking For


Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Life Sciences, Biotechnology, Data Science, Engineering, or related field
Initial experience (1–3 years) in biopharma manufacturing, data analytics, or a regulated environment
Strong data handling and analysis skills, including advanced Excel capabilities; Power BI or VBA is a plus
Knowledge of statistical methods and tools (e.g., Minitab, JMP, Python, R, SeeQ, or similar)
Experience working with manufacturing data sources such as batch records, DeltaV, or electronic systems is advantageous
Strong organizational and collaboration skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities
Excellent communication skills in English; German is a plus


About Lonza

At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Title: Data Scientist, Marketing Analytics
Description:
Who is Sonar?

Sonar is driving the future of agent-centric software development. As the leader in AI code verification and governance, we solve a critical problem: ensuring that software generated by AI-assisted developers or autonomous agents is reliable, secure, and maintainable.

Integrating seamlessly with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Gemini, and Devin, we help over 75% of the Fortune 100 build trusted, reliable, compliant software. Customers who use Sonar are 44% less likely to report an outage due to AI-generated code.

We believe code verification is the critical missing link in the Agent-Centric Development Cycle (AC/DC). Industry giants like Nvidia, ServiceNow, Booking.com, Goldman Sachs, AstraZeneca, and Ford Motor Company count on us to provide independent, explainable, consistent review and governance of their AI-generated code via products like:


SonarQube: The world’s leading AI code review and verification platform.
SonarQube Foundation Agent: Currently topping the leaderboards for agentic software repair.
SonarSweep & Sonar Context Augmentation: Providing the enterprise-grade context and constraints agents need to be truly effective.


Our team operates across global hubs in Austin, Bochum, Dubai, Geneva, London, Singapore, Tokyo, and Washington D.C. We move with a mindset we call CODE:


Committed to our customers and community.
Obsessed with quality.
Deliberate in our decisions.
Effective as one team.


With over $400M in revenue and profitable, fast-paced growth, we are building the backbone of the AI software revolution. If you’re hungry to have an impact, want to build at a fast pace, and ready to work at the forefront of AI, we want to hear from you.

Position Description

This role is the data team's dedicated analytical partner for Marketing. Your core job is to turn marketing data into decisions: diagnose what is working and what is not, explain why, and recommend where to spend the next dollar. You will own marketing attribution, ROI, and conversion analysis end to end, and you will be proactive about it, surfacing findings and opportunities before anyone asks.

You will explore data, form hypotheses, and partner with our Data and Analytics Engineers to shape the models and pipelines you need. You will help define those models, but your primary measure of success is the quality and impact of your analysis.

This role sits in the Data & Insights team and partners closely with Marketing, IT/Marketing Ops, Data Engineers, Analytics Engineers, and data scientists covering other domains.

What You Will Do


Drive marketing decisions with analysis. Own attribution, ROI, conversion, and funnel analysis. Diagnose drops and anomalies, quantify what drives them, and translate findings into clear recommendations for marketing leaders.
Optimize marketing spend. Connect spend to outcomes across channels and campaigns, and advise on where to invest, cut, or test next.
Be proactive. Explore and correlate data to surface insights nobody asked for. Anticipate the next question. Bring opportunities to the marketing team rather than waiting for requests.
Run experiments. Design and read out A/B tests and other experiments, applying sound statistical methods and being honest about significance and causality.
Partner on the data foundation. Work hand in hand with Data Engineers and Analytics Engineers to get marketing data into the warehouse and modeled. Define requirements, explore raw data, and contribute to data models that connect marketing data with sales and product data.
Tell the story. Communicate insights so non-technical stakeholders can act, and document context, caveats, and decisions so the work survives handoffs.


Experience And Qualifications


Strong analytical track record: someone who has measurably influenced business or marketing decisions through analysis, not just produced reports.
Comfort with the latest AI tools, and a habit of using them to work faster and sharper: exploring data, writing and debugging code, drafting analysis, and accelerating insight. You stay current as the tooling evolves and bring new approaches to the team.
Eagerness to develop: you actively grow your skills, seek feedback, and treat new tools and methods as opportunities rather than threats.
Solid SQL. You can independently query, join, and explore data without waiting for someone to prepare it for you.
Proficiency in Python for analysis, modeling, and automation.
Statistical foundation: experimentation, significance testing, regression, segmentation, forecasting, and the judgment to know which applies.
Working knowledge of marketing and GTM data: channels, campaigns, attribution models, funnel and conversion metrics, and the realities of joining marketing data to CRM/sales and product usage data.
Willingness to get hands-on with data modeling. You don't need to be a dbt expert, but you must be comfortable exploring messy data and partnering on (or building) the models you need rather than waiting for clean tables.
Strong communication and stakeholder skills: you can challenge weak measurement respectfully and make a recommendation, not just present options.
Proactivity and autonomy: you raise your hand early, plan your own work, and look for impact without being asked.


In-office culture

We're intentional about this. We believe the best teams are built in the room together. Three anchor days — Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays — create the collaboration rhythm that makes a hub office worth having.

Candidates need to be genuinely based in the location the role is posted — if that's not where you are today, we're happy to support relocation for the right person.

We value diversity, equity, and inclusion

At Sonar, we believe that our diversity is our strength. We are a global company that values and respects different backgrounds, perspectives, and cultures. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive work environment where everyone feels valued and empowered to contribute their best. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and welcome all qualified applicants, regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

If you need any accommodation, please reach out to us at [email protected] .

All offers of employment at Sonar are contingent upon the results of a comprehensive background check and reference verification conducted before the start date.

Applications that are submitted through agencies or third party recruiters will not be considered.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Experience And Qualifications

  • Strong analytical track record: someone who has measurably influenced business or marketing decisions through analysis, not just produced reports.
  • Comfort with the latest AI tools, and a habit of using them to work faster and sharper: exploring data, writing and debugging code, drafting analysis, and accelerating insight. You stay current as the tooling evolves and bring new approaches to the team.
  • Eagerness to develop: you actively grow your skills, seek feedback, and treat new tools and methods as opportunities rather than threats.
  • Solid SQL. You can independently query, join, and explore data without waiting for someone to prepare it for you.
  • Proficiency in Python for analysis, modeling, and automation.
  • Statistical foundation: experimentation, significance testing, regression, segmentation, forecasting, and the judgment to know which applies.
  • Working knowledge of marketing and GTM data: channels, campaigns, attribution models, funnel and conversion metrics, and the realities of joining marketing data to CRM/sales and product usage data.
  • Willingness to get hands-on with data modeling. You don't need to be a dbt expert, but you must be comfortable exploring messy data and partnering on (or building) the models you need rather than waiting for clean tables.
  • Strong communication and stakeholder skills: you can challenge weak measurement respectfully and make a recommendation, not just present options.
  • Proactivity and autonomy: you raise your hand early, plan your own work, and look for impact without being asked.

In-office culture

We're intentional about this. We believe the best teams are built in the room together. Three anchor days — Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays — create the collaboration rhythm that makes a hub office worth having.

Candidates need to be genuinely based in the location the role is posted — if that's not where you are today, we're happy to support relocation for the right person.

We value diversity, equity, and inclusion

At Sonar, we believe that our diversity is our strength. We are a global company that values and respects different backgrounds, perspectives, and cultures. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive work environment where everyone feels valued and empowered to contribute their best. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and welcome all qualified applicants, regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

If you need any accommodation, please reach out to us at [email protected] .

All offers of employment at Sonar are contingent upon the results of a comprehensive background check and reference verification conducted before the start date.

Applications that are submitted through agencies or third party recruiters will not be considered.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Responsible Sourcing Data Analyst
Description:
YOUR MISSION

Located in Richemont offices in Bellevue/Plan-les-Ouates, she/he will report to the Responsible SourcingData&Tools Project Manager. The Responsible Sourcing analyst will be responsible for collecting, analyzing, and interpreting large data sets (ESG data, Suppliers data….) to extract valuable and actionable insights for Responsible Sourcing strategy, decision-making, objectives setting, reporting and disclosure. This role involves translating complex data into understandable information, identifying trends and providing recommendations that support strategic business decisions in line with Responsible Sourcing strategy.

As Responsible Sourcing Data Analyst, you will design reports and data visualizations and develop tools for internal - Responsible Sourcing experts, Group procurement - and external stakeholders - mainly Regions/Maisons procurement and sustainability function - including but not limited to the preparation of reports and materials to communicate responsible sourcing key metrics.

Ultimately, in a fast evolving Sustainability regulations context, this role will also contribute to the development of new sourcing key performance indicators, building a resilient Responsible Sourcing management system for the Group

The ideal candidate will possess strong analytical skills, a keen eye for detail, and the ability to communicate findings effectively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders

HOW WILL YOU MAKE AN IMPACT?

Data collection & Extraction


Lead efforts to integrate data from various sources into the department applications, leveraging automation tools to streamline data processes
Gather data from various sources, including databases, data warehouses, APIs, and external datasets.
Develop and maintain automated data collection processes where applicable.


Data Analysis & Modelling


Perform exploratory data analysis to identify patterns, trends, and anomalies.
Apply statistical methods and data modelling techniques to answer business questions.
Develop and execute queries to extract and manipulate data for analysis.


Key metrics, tools & application development


Design, development and maintenance of the department in-house applications, dashboards and key performance indicators
Monitor their respective performance, identify areas for optimization, and implement enhancements to improve usability, performance, and scalability
Document and record all developments
Present complex data findings in a clear, concise, and compelling manner to various audiences.


Data governance & Quality assurance


Support the Responsible Sourcing Data & Tools project manager to establish and enforce data governance policies and procedures
Clean, transform and validate data to ensure accuracy, completeness, and consistency of data used in Responsible Sourcing dashboards and reports.
Identify & Resolve data quality issues.


Support, Collaboration and Communication


Collaborate closely with Responsible Sourcing experts to understand business requirements, gather insights, and update dashboards, reports
Provide technical support and training to end-users of Responsible sourcing dashboards, tools and applications


Others Transversal Related Tasks


Support the Responsible Sourcing team in the development of supply chains ESG key metrics to align with sustainability frameworks and regulations – e.g GRI standards, CSRD, CSDDDD…


HOW WILL YOU EXPERIENCE SUCCESS WITH US?


Education: Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, Business Analytics, or a related discipline.
Experience:
2-5 years of experience in a data analysis role.
Proven experience with data manipulation, analysis, and reporting.
Technical Skills:
SQL Proficiency: Strong ability to write complex SQL queries for data extraction, manipulation, and analysis.
Data Visualization Tools: Hands-on experience with at least Looker
Spreadsheet Software: Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel (pivot tables, VLOOKUP, advanced formulas, data modelling).
Programming (Optional but highly valued): Familiarity with Python or R for data analysis, statistical modelling, and automation.
Analytical & Problem-Solving Skills:
Strong analytical and critical thinking abilities to interpret data and draw meaningful conclusions.
Ability to identify root causes of problems and propose data-driven solutions.
Excellent attention to detail and accuracy.
Communication Skills:
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present complex information clearly and concisely.
Ability to effectively communicate with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Business Acumen:
Understanding of business processes and the ability to connect data insights to business objectives.
Curiosity and a proactive approach to understanding business challenges.
Teamwork & Collaboration:
Ability to work independently and as part of a cross-functional team.
Strong interpersonal skills.

Preferred Skills


Master's degree in a quantitative field.
Experience with cloud data platforms (e.g., Google Cloud Platform).
Knowledge of data warehousing concepts and ETL processes.
Understanding of machine learning concepts and their application in business.

Title: Data Management Specialist
Description:
Agap2 est un groupe européen d’ingénierie et de conseil opérationnel fondé en 2005 et spécialisé dans l’industrie. Nous travaillons dans les secteurs de la pharmacie, de la biotechnologie, des dispositifs médicaux, de l’horlogerie et de la mécanique de précision.




En tant que consultant, vous serez amené à accompagner nos clients dans la réalisation de leurs projets sur site en apportant votre expertise et vos compétences. Vous êtes agile et désireux de vous adapter à différents environnements de travail qui alimenteront votre curiosité de voir ce qui se cache derrière les portes fermées des plus grands projets du portefeuille de nos clients.




Vos missions

Collecter, analyser et valoriser les données de production afin de soutenir les activités opérationnelles.
Développer et maintenir des tableaux de bord et des reportings à partir de bases de données existantes.
Suivre et analyser les indicateurs de performance (KPIs) et les tendances des procédés.
Participer à l'automatisation des analyses et des rapports grâce à des solutions innovantes et des outils d'intelligence artificielle.
Contribuer aux projets de digitalisation et d'amélioration continue dans un environnement réglementé.




Profil recherché

Formation supérieure en Data Science, Informatique, Bioinformatique, Biotechnologies ou domaine similaire.
Première expérience ou forte appétence pour l'analyse de données et la programmation (Python, SQL, R).
Maîtrise d'un outil de visualisation de données (Power BI, Tableau, Spotfire ou équivalent).
Esprit analytique, curiosité, autonomie et capacité à travailler en équipe.
Anglais professionnel requis ; une expérience en environnement pharmaceutique ou biotechnologique est un plus.

Title: Finance Analytics Engineer
Description:
CONTEXT

We are looking for a Finance Analytics Engineer to translate business needs into data requirements, validating outputs and enabling our FP&A organization to work effectively in the new data environment.

This is not a pure engineering role. The ideal candidate combines analytical engineering capability, comfortable with SQL, BigQuery and Looker, with strong Finance domain fluency and the communication skills to bridge the technical and business worlds. You will be embedded in a small, high-impact team and will work closely with the Group Data Office, Finance Process & Data, and FP&A leadership.

HOW WILL YOU MAKE AN IMPACT?


Own the FP&A requirements for the GCP/Looker migration, defining what data FP&A needs, in what form, and to what quality standard
Act as the primary liaison between Group Business Analytics and the Finance Data domain lead, ensuring business priorities are represented and delivered
Build, validate and maintain Looker dashboards and data models that serve the reporting and insight needs of the team
Drive adoption of the new data platform within the Central Group Finance organization, providing training, documentation and ongoing support to non- technical stakeholders - Identify and close data gaps: working with upstream teams to ensure the right data assets exist, are accurate and are accessible
Enable the Business Analytics team to operate effectively in the new stack, upskilling colleagues and embedding good data practices
Contribute to the design and continuous improvement of the team's reporting and insight infrastructure


HOW WILL YOU EXPERIENCE SUCCESS WITH US?

Education, Experience And Core Competencies


Master’s degree in Data Analytics, Data Science, Engineering or a related field
5+ years of experience in an analytics, data or BI role, ideally within a Finance context
Hands-on proficiency in SQL and BigQuery; experience with Looker (LookML) is strongly preferred
Proven experience translating business requirements into data models, dashboards or analytical solutions
Solid understanding of Finance processes, reporting cycles and KPIs; you can speak the language of FP&A
Experience working alongside a data migration or platform transformation program
Fluency in English is a must; French is considered a plus


Personal And Methodological Skills


A bridge-builder mindset: able to translate between technical and business stakeholders with ease
Proactive and ownership-oriented, you identify gaps and act on them without being asked
Strong attention to data quality and detail, balanced with the ability to deliver pragmatically under pressure
Comfortable with ambiguity in a transformation context; able to help define the path forward
Structured thinker who can manage multiple workstreams and prioritize effectively


Social Skills


Confident collaborator across technical and non-technical teams, including senior leadership
Clear communicator who can explain complex data concepts in plain business language
Empathetic enabler, you get satisfaction from helping others become more capable and independent

Title: Ingénieur de données/Ingénieure de données
Description:
CLEEVEN est un cabinet de conseil européen fondé autour de deux convictions fondamentales :

L'excellence des ingénieurs européens doit être un moteur de la compétitivité mondiale
Notre mission humaine : accompagner nos consultants dans leur développement personnel et professionnel

Présent en Suisse, nous accompagnons nos clients dans leurs projets de transformation digitale et de valorisation de la donnée.




Contexte du projet :

Nous recherchons un Data Analyst / BI Engineer pour accompagner le développement et l'évolution de solutions de reporting et d'analyse décisionnelle.

Vous interviendrez au croisement des enjeux métiers et techniques afin de transformer des données complexes en indicateurs de pilotage et en outils d'aide à la décision. Vous participerez à l'ensemble de la chaîne de valeur de la donnée : compréhension des besoins, modélisation, construction de tableaux de bord et amélioration continue des solutions analytiques.




Objectifs du projet :

Recueillir et analyser les besoins de reporting et d'analyse des équipes métiers.
Concevoir, développer et maintenir des tableaux de bord et solutions de reporting.
Extraire, transformer et analyser des données provenant de multiples sources.
Construire et faire évoluer des modèles de données et des couches sémantiques.
Définir et suivre des KPIs permettant de piloter l'activité.
Garantir la qualité, la cohérence et la fiabilité des données.
Accompagner les utilisateurs dans l'exploitation des outils décisionnels.
Participer à l'amélioration continue de l'écosystème BI et Data de l'entreprise.




Compétences clés :

Solides compétences en SQL et en bases de données relationnelles.
Expérience sur des outils de Business Intelligence et de visualisation de données.
Bonne maîtrise des concepts de modélisation de données.
Capacité à traduire des besoins métiers en solutions de reporting concrètes.
Excellentes capacités d'analyse et de résolution de problèmes.
Aisance dans les échanges avec des interlocuteurs métiers et techniques.
Bon niveau d'anglais professionnel.

Nice To Have

Expérience avec Looker et/ou LookML.
Expérience avec Power BI, Tableau ou des outils similaires.
Connaissances des concepts ETL/ELT et des pipelines de données.
Expérience sur des plateformes cloud (GCP, Azure ou AWS).
Expérience dans un environnement industriel ou manufacturier.
Connaissances de Python pour l'analyse de données ou l'automatisation.




Profil recherché :

Formation supérieure en informatique, data, systèmes d'information, mathématiques, statistiques ou ingénierie.
Expérience en tant que Data Analyst, BI Engineer ou sur un poste similaire.
Forte capacité d'analyse et sens du détail.
Capacité à comprendre les enjeux métiers et à transformer les données en informations exploitables.




À très vite !
