  • Grosszügige Vergünstigungen von Produkten und Dienstleistungen bei 400+ Firmen
  • Attraktives Dienstjubiläumsreglement

Dein Verantwortungsbereich

  • Du unterstützt das Sales Team bei der Erstellung von Budgets und Kalkulationen
  • Du bereitest Daten und Auswertungen für Sales verständlich auf
  • Du arbeitest dich in Kostenstrukturen und betriebliche Zusammenhänge ein
  • Aufbereitung von Markt und Wettbewerbsinformationen zur Unterstützung von Akquisitionsprojekten
  • Als CRM Super User, verantwortest du die Datenqualität, User Onboarding und KVP
  • Erstellung von Reports, Forecasts und Dashboards zur Unterstützung datenbasierter Entscheidungen im Sales
  • Du arbeitest eng mit den Teams aus Operations, Culinary, Procurement, Controlling und Sales zusammen

Dein Profil

  • Abgeschlossenes Studium oder erste Berufserfahrung im Bereich Betriebswirtschaft, Controlling oder operative Erfahrung
  • Gastronomie Erfahrung von Vorteil
  • Praktische Erfahrung im Umgang mit Zahlen, Kalkulationen oder Auswertungen
  • Solide Excel-Kenntnisse und Motivation, dich in diesem Bereich weiterzuentwickeln
  • Starkes Interesse an Zusammenhängen zwischen Betrieb, Kosten und Sales
  • Strukturierte und sorgfältige Arbeitsweise sowie Freude am Lernen
  • Teamorientierte Persönlichkeit mit einer offenen, proaktive und pragmatischen Art
  • Sehr gute Deutsch- & Englischkenntnisse, Französisch von Vorteil

Du arbeitest in einem engagierten und herzlichen Team im schönen und modernen Service Center in Kloten oder Remote im Home Office.

Title: Data Scientist (m/w/d) 60%
Description:
Unternehmensbeschreibung

Wüest Partner ist ein innovatives und unabhängiges Software- und Dienstleistungsunternehmen der Immobilienwirtschaft. Seit 1985 schaffen wir fundierte Entscheidungsgrundlagen durch die Verbindung von Immobilien-Fachexpertise, Daten und digitalen Lösungen. Als Branchenführer gestalten wir gemeinsam mit unseren Kundinnen und Kunden die Zukunft der Immobilienbranche – mit umfassenden Leistungen in den Bereichen Bewertung, Beratung, Data, Analytics, KI, Software, Nachhaltigkeit und Bildung.

Die Wüest-Partner-Gruppe beschäftigt rund 500 Mitarbeitende an 15 Standorten in Europa mit Hauptsitz in der Schweiz. Zur Gruppe gehören die Schweizer Tochtergesellschaften Durable, Datahouse, Qualicasa und Signa-Terre.

Stellenbeschreibung

Dein Team und deine Mission

In der Division Data, Analytics & Technology bündeln wir Expertise in Datenanalyse, Modellierung, Softwareentwicklung und Immobilienmarktresearch.

Am Standort Zürich wirst du Teil des 9-köpfigen Data Teams Schweiz und arbeitest in offener und kooperativer Atmosphäre mit unseren erfahrenen Experten der Bereiche Data Science und Data Engineering zusammen. Unsere Mission ist die Entwicklung qualitativ hochwertiger Datensets, darauf aufbauenden Analysen und datengetriebenen Modellen, um die Produkte von Wüest Partner auf das nächste Level zu heben.

Deine Aufgaben


Aufbau und Entwicklung räumlicher und gebäudespezifischer Datenbestände und Modelle zum Immobilienmarkt, sowohl bezogen auf den Standort Schweiz, als auch im internationalen Kontext
Konzeption und kontinuierliche Optimierung von Data Pipelines in unserem internationalen Data Warehouse
Ausführen quantitativer und qualitativer Datenanalysen mit räumlichem Bezug
Länder- und teamübergreifende Zusammenarbeit zur Koordination und Lösungsfindung daten- und modellbezogener Fragestellungen
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit unserer Division der Real Estate Experts zur Gewährleistung hoher Datenstandards im Bewertungskontext
Rolle als Expert/in und zentrale Ansprechperson für datenbezogene Fragestellungen
Monitoring und Dokumentation von Prozessen zur Sicherstellung der Datenqualität


Qualifikationen

Dein Profil


Hochschulabschluss mit analytischem oder technischem Schwerpunkt, Natur- oder Ingenieurwissenschaften
Sehr gute Programmierkenntnisse mit Python und R für die Schwerpunkte Statistik, Data Science und Data Processing
Nachweislich Erfahrung mit Databricks / Spark (PySpark, sparklyr, SparkR) - Zertifizierung wünschenswert
Solide Datenbank- und DWH Kenntnisse (inkl. SQL)
Erfahrung mit Bitbucket, Gitea und Versionierungstools, z.B. Git
Interesse an immobilienbezogenen Themen, Data Analytics, Machine Learning und AI
Eigeninitiative, selbständige und gut strukturierte Arbeitsweise mit ausgeprägten analytischen und konzeptionellen Fähigkeiten
Teamorientierte Persönlichkeit mit hoher Sozial- und Kommunikationskompetenz
Verhandlungssichere Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse


Zusätzliche Informationen

Wir bieten dir


Flexibles Work Smart-Konzept mit Home Office-Möglichkeit
Erfrischende, professionelle und kollegiale Unternehmenskultur mit tollen Events (Unternehmensfeiern, Gipfeli-Pausen etc.)
Hoher Teamspirit, flache Hierarchien und abwechslungsreiche Aufgaben
Schöner Arbeitsplatz im Zentrum von Zürich mit moderner Infrastruktur
Grosszügiges Weiterbildungsbudget inkl. Zeit dafür sowie weitere attraktive Fringe Benefits


Kontakt

Bei offenen Fragen steht dir Federica Arcella, Lead Talent Acquisition, gerne unter federica.arcella@wuestpartner.com zur Verfügung.

Title: EU Collectibles Inventory Insights Lead
Description:
At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts.

Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet.

Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all.

EU Collectibles Inventory Insights Manager

Location: Bern, Switzerland

Hybrid

At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts.

Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet.

Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all.

About The Team

eBay is the #1 Collectibles marketplace in the world, selling to millions of customers globally. Our mission is to be the most loved destination for collectors of trading cards, memorabilia, toys, comics, coins, and more. To accomplish this, we put our collectors first and innovate tirelessly to optimize experiences across our platform.

eBay Collectibles is looking for an EU Inventory Insights Manager to drive deep understanding of where to play and how to win within the broad and diverse collectibles market. This analytics pro will leverage data to advise where we focus incentives, seller acquisition efforts, buyer programming, retention/loyalty, and where we should execute our strategy across the entire EU collectibles portfolio. This role’s remit encompasses critical analytics work that will inform strategy and operational day-to-day decision making within one of eBay's most passionate and rapidly expanding categories. The ideal candidate will possess a complete analytical toolkit including how to synthesize data into actionable strategic and operational insights.

eBay would not be what it is today without our amazing employees -– from Inventory Insights leaders to Customer Support and everyone in between. We take great pride in hiring and developing top talent who can add to our culture and believe in our mission. eBay is an exciting opportunity to harness a growth mindset and engage with a passionate community of enthusiasts, fostering a vibrant and dynamic marketplace.

About The Role

This is a critical analytics role for the European Collectibles team, whose charter is to grow the category by continuing to make eBay a trusted and top-of-mind destination for Collectors. We are looking for someone to enable strategy execution. You will support initiatives ranging from analyzing data to understanding what is eBay’s next focus area to optimizing existing operations. You will also partner with the category managers within EU Collectibles, the wider global Collectibles teams, the Product and Technology teams, and other business teams on different initiatives. This role requires someone who has the technical skills to extract and analyze data as well as develop visualizations to communicate insights in an easily digestible way.

Responsibilities


Extract data and derive data-driven insights to inform decision making
Support development of A/B tests related to buyer and seller trust, behavior, and inventory velocity
Build reporting dashboards and scorecards
Support data needs for operating and strategic discussions with the Collectibles leadership team
Support the development of executive-ready presentations on strategy and operational updates
Work cross-functionally with peer analytics partners to develop comprehensive tools and business recommendations



Qualifications

Experience:

5 -7 years in analytics

Technical Requirements


Advanced SQL coding (+ demonstrated expertise working with and pulling data from Data Warehouse)
Advanced MS Excel
Demonstrated expertise developing and updating scorecards / dashboards in Tableau and MS Excel
Proficient in MS Powerpoint and Google Presentations



Other Requirements/differentiators


Able to thoughtfully apply detailed logic and methods to solve problems and enable decision making
Able to build constructive and effective relationships with a broad and diverse group of business partners
Track record of presenting ideas and directions to influence others into taking desired actions
Able to evaluate financial and business indicators and translate data into concrete information to get results
Strong communications, including the ability to tell a compelling story backed by data
Desire to engage with customers directly and passionate about helping eBay win!



Additional Details

eBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com. We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities.

We use cookies to enhance your experience and may use AI tools for administrative tasks in the hiring process. To learn how we handle your personal data and use AI responsibly, please visit our Talent Privacy Notice, Privacy Center, and AI Hiring Guidelines.

Title: Senior Business/Data Modelling Analyst
Description:
The client is building a new Data Warehouse / Data Intelligence Hub for their Asset Management division. Data has already been migrated and the technical foundation is in place.

The mandate has arisen due to internal resource reallocation during budget delays, and an external expert is now required to drive data modelling activities. This position requires a senior profile with strong business analysis, data analysis, and data modelling capabilities.

The successful candidate will help shape the data warehouse, connect existing datasets, and coach internal team members.




Key Responsibilities

* Lead the creation and optimisation of data models for the Data Warehouse / Data Intelligence Hub.

* Conduct business analysis to gather requirements from Asset Management stakeholders.

* Perform data analysis to validate data quality, relationships, and modelling decisions.

* Build conceptual, logical, and physical data models.

* Create and validate Power BI reports to demonstrate data availability and correctness.

* Collaborate with data engineers, business teams, and technical stakeholders.

* Guide and coach the internal team in data modelling best practices.

* Ensure the data warehouse structure is scalable and strategically aligned.

* Connect already migrated datasets to make them usable for analytics and reporting.




Desired Skills and Experience

* Minimum 5+ years in Business Analysis and Data Modelling.

* Strong technical background; experience in data engineering preferred.

* Strong understanding of data warehousing and modelling processes.

* Experience with Power BI (building reports, validating data structures).

* Ability to shape the data warehouse and work independently.

* Asset Management experience beneficial but not required.

* Certifications not required (but a plus).







Experience within Insurance/Financial Services is mandatory

Title: Commercial Analyst Real Estate (m/w/d) 100 %
Description:
Bei Andermatt Swiss Alps gestalten wir die Zukunft des alpinen Lebens. Zur Verstärkung unseres Real-Estate-Teams suchen wir eine analytisch geprägte und unternehmerisch denkende Persönlichkeit, die Preisgestaltung, Vertriebsstrategie und kommerzielle Entscheidungen datenbasiert mitgestaltet. In enger Zusammenarbeit mit dem CEO Real Estate und dem Führungsteam übersetzt du Marktanalysen, Kundenkenntnisse und Geschäftsdaten in konkrete Handlungsempfehlungen und schaffst damit die Grundlage für nachhaltiges Wachstum, höhere Wertschöpfung und langfristigen Unternehmenserfolg.

Die Position bietet die Möglichkeit, zentrale strategische Entscheidungen in einer der führenden alpinen Immobilien- und Tourismusdestinationen Europas aktiv zu prägen.

Commercial Analyst Real Estate (m/w/d) 100 %

Das kannst du bewegen


Entwicklung und Weiterentwicklung von Analyse- und Prognosemodellen für Preisgestaltung, Nachfrageentwicklung und Bestandssteuerung zur Optimierung der kommerziellen Performance unseres Immobilienportfolios
Laufende Analyse von Markttrends, Preisentwicklungen und Wettbewerbsaktivitäten zur Ableitung fundierter Handlungsempfehlungen und kontinuierlichen Weiterentwicklung der Preisstrategie
Analyse von Verkaufszahlen, Pipeline-Performance und Konversionsraten zur Identifikation von Käuferverhalten, Nachfragemustern und Potenzialen für gezielte Vertriebsinitiativen
Unterstützung strategischer Entscheidungen zu Produktpositionierung, Wohnungs- und Nutzungsmix sowie Preisgestaltung in enger Zusammenarbeit mit den Bereichen Development und Sales, mit dem Ziel, den Projekterfolg nachhaltig zu maximieren
Erstellung von Forecasts und Business Cases sowie laufendes Monitoring der Geschäftsentwicklung im Vergleich zu den Planwerten; Identifikation von Abweichungen und Ableitung geeigneter Massnahmen
Aufbereitung komplexer Analysen und Daten in klaren, adressatengerechten Entscheidungsgrundlagen, Berichten und Präsentationen für die Geschäftsleitung und weitere zentrale Stakeholder
Förderung einer datenbasierten Entscheidungs- und Arbeitskultur innerhalb der Organisation sowie Sicherstellung hoher Standards in den Bereichen Datenqualität, Datenintegrität und Data Governance



Das bringst du mit


Abgeschlossenes Studium in Betriebswirtschaft, Volkswirtschaft, Finance oder einem vergleichbaren Fachgebiet
Erfahrung in einem analytisch geprägten und leistungsorientierten Umfeld mit Berührungspunkten zu strategischen, kommerziellen oder investitionsbezogenen Fragestellungen
Sehr gute Kenntnisse in Excel und PowerPoint sowie nachweisliche Erfahrung in der Entwicklung strukturierter Finanz-, Geschäfts- und Entscheidungsmodelle
Ausgeprägtes kommerzielles Verständnis sowie die Fähigkeit, komplexe Analysen in klare, praxisorientierte Handlungsempfehlungen zu übersetzen
Hohe Problemlösungskompetenz und die Fähigkeit, auch in einem dynamischen Umfeld mit unvollständigen Informationen strukturierte Entscheidungen vorzubereiten und Ergebnisse voranzutreiben
Starke Kommunikations- und Stakeholder-Management-Fähigkeiten sowie die Fähigkeit, auf allen Ebenen der Organisation überzeugend und adressatengerecht zu agieren
Sehr gute Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift (mindestens Niveau C1); Deutschkenntnisse sind ein klarer Vorteil



Arbeitsort: Andermatt & Homeoffice

Vertrag: unbefristet

Stellenantritt: per sofort oder nach Verfügbarkeit

Noch Fragen? Unser HR Recruiter Daniel Suter beantwortet sie gerne telefonisch unter +41 41 888 15 65.

  • Unterstützung strategischer Entscheidungen zu Produktpositionierung, Wohnungs- und Nutzungsmix sowie Preisgestaltung in enger Zusammenarbeit mit den Bereichen Development und Sales, mit dem Ziel, den Projekterfolg nachhaltig zu maximieren
  • Erstellung von Forecasts und Business Cases sowie laufendes Monitoring der Geschäftsentwicklung im Vergleich zu den Planwerten; Identifikation von Abweichungen und Ableitung geeigneter Massnahmen
  • Aufbereitung komplexer Analysen und Daten in klaren, adressatengerechten Entscheidungsgrundlagen, Berichten und Präsentationen für die Geschäftsleitung und weitere zentrale Stakeholder
  • Förderung einer datenbasierten Entscheidungs- und Arbeitskultur innerhalb der Organisation sowie Sicherstellung hoher Standards in den Bereichen Datenqualität, Datenintegrität und Data Governance


Das bringst du mit

  • Abgeschlossenes Studium in Betriebswirtschaft, Volkswirtschaft, Finance oder einem vergleichbaren Fachgebiet
  • Erfahrung in einem analytisch geprägten und leistungsorientierten Umfeld mit Berührungspunkten zu strategischen, kommerziellen oder investitionsbezogenen Fragestellungen
  • Sehr gute Kenntnisse in Excel und PowerPoint sowie nachweisliche Erfahrung in der Entwicklung strukturierter Finanz-, Geschäfts- und Entscheidungsmodelle
  • Ausgeprägtes kommerzielles Verständnis sowie die Fähigkeit, komplexe Analysen in klare, praxisorientierte Handlungsempfehlungen zu übersetzen
  • Hohe Problemlösungskompetenz und die Fähigkeit, auch in einem dynamischen Umfeld mit unvollständigen Informationen strukturierte Entscheidungen vorzubereiten und Ergebnisse voranzutreiben
  • Starke Kommunikations- und Stakeholder-Management-Fähigkeiten sowie die Fähigkeit, auf allen Ebenen der Organisation überzeugend und adressatengerecht zu agieren
  • Sehr gute Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift (mindestens Niveau C1); Deutschkenntnisse sind ein klarer Vorteil


Arbeitsort: Andermatt & Homeoffice

Vertrag: unbefristet

Stellenantritt: per sofort oder nach Verfügbarkeit

Noch Fragen? Unser HR Recruiter Daniel Suter beantwortet sie gerne telefonisch unter +41 41 888 15 65.

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
About VAT Group

At VAT, we change the world with vacuum solutions. As the world’s leading supplier of high-performance vacuum valves, we have been driving innovation for more than 60 years. With over 3,200 employees worldwide, we operate from our headquarters in Haag, Switzerland, with manufacturing sites in Switzerland, Malaysia and Romania, as well as sales and service hubs around the world. We guided by our passions integrity, teamwork, customer centricity, and innovation – always working together as #oneVAT.

Joining us means becoming part of a passionate, international team where your voice is heard, your ideas matter, and your career growth is supported.

Business Analyst

As a Senior Business Analyst, you will play a central role in shaping data-driven decision-making at VAT by translating business needs into clear, actionable technical specifications. You will work closely with stakeholders across business and IT to ensure that analytics solutions are aligned with strategic goals and deliver measurable value.

You’ll be part of a global IT team spanning Malaysia, Switzerland, and Romania, collaborating across time zones and cultures. Your work will help ensure that every decision-maker at VAT has access to the right data and insights — at the right time and in the right format — to drive operational excellence and customer value.

Responsibilities


Lead and mentor Business Analysts and continuously challenge and improve internal business analysis processes to ensure operational excellence and maximized value delivery for VAT
Create and maintain Solution Design Documents (SDDs) to capture, refine, and translate business requirements into clear technical specifications for data and analytics solutions
Consult with business stakeholders across domains (e.g. procurement, manufacturing, supply chain, finance, sales) to understand their needs, challenge initial requests, and propose fit-for-purpose solutions
Collaborate with IT teams, including analytics engineers, data engineers, architects and functional consultants to ensure alignment between business needs and technical implementation
Support the development process by providing initial data exports, performing manual test cases, and conducting first-level validation of analytics solutions before business handover
Educate and support key users and end users on the design, value, and intended use of analytics solutions, including clarifying limitations and preventing misinterpretation of data
Contribute to agile delivery by participating in sprint planning, reviews, retrospectives, and daily stand-ups as part of a cross-functional team


Required Qualifications


Minimum 5 years of experience as a Business Analyst, Data Analyst, or in a similar role within IT, data & analytics or business intelligence
Master’s degree in Business Analytics, Information Systems, Economics, Computer Science, or a related field — or equivalent professional experience
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with sharp attention to detail
Proven experience in gathering, refining, and documenting business requirements and translating them into clear technical specifications
Excellent communication and collaboration skills to work effectively with both business and technical stakeholders
Comfortable working in cross-functional, multicultural teams
Ability to work independently, manage multiple priorities, and adapt in a dynamic environment
Proficiency in working with data, whether through tools like Excel, scripting or programming languages, or other analytical platforms
Solid understanding of core business processes (e.g. finance, supply chain, sales, manufacturing) and how they are supported by enterprise IT systems (e.g. ERP, CRM, analytics platforms)
Fluent in English, both written and spoken


Preferred Qualifications


Proficiency in SQL and Power BI, including experience with semantic models and report building
Familiarity with Microsoft Dynamics 365 (D365) or other ERP systems and their impact on reporting and analytics
Experience working with Microsoft Fabric or similar modern data platforms
Exposure to visual communication standards such as IBCS
Experience in conducting user trainings and supporting solution adoption including change management
Hands-on experience working in an agile environment, applying Scrum methodology
Microsoft Certification: Power BI Data Analyst Associate (PL-300) or similar
Ability to take ownership, even when the decision requires navigating ambiguity and uncertainty
Experience leading and mentoring others through periods of change
Strong understanding of project lifecycle management, including planning, execution, release, and post-delivery support

Title: SAP Data & Analytics Specialist
Description:
In Accenture, potrai portare la tua visione innovativa al cuore del cambiamento, lavorando con le aziende più importanti a livello mondiale per trasformare interi settori di mercato. Modella il futuro del business e della tecnologia sviluppando soluzioni all'avanguardia in grado di generare nuovo valore.

Il tuo ruolo e le tue prospettive

Entra a far parte del nostro network internazionale di talenti Technology in ambito SAP. Lavorerai con tecnologie innovative su progetti entusiasmanti, sviluppando per le aziende più importanti percorsi personalizzati verso le nuove soluzioni SAP, dalle roadmap digitali fino alle trasformazioni funzionali e di settore. In particolare, potrai:



Analizzare i requisiti di business e tradurli in modelli dati e soluzioni analitiche

Progettare e implementare modelli dati e processi di trasformazione

Sviluppare dashboard e report avanzati per supportare le decisioni di business

Collaborare con team multidisciplinari per definire soluzioni Data & Analytics

Supportare la definizione e l’evoluzione della roadmap Data & Analytics dei clienti, contribuendo a iniziative di innovazione



Se condividi la nostra passione per le soluzioni SAP e hai le seguenti caratteristiche, ti stiamo aspettando:



Esperienza di 2–5 anni in progetti Data & Analytics in ambito SAP.

Esperienza pratica in SAP BTP, Datasphere, SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) e SAP BW/4HANA, con conoscenza dei processi ETL complessi.

Competenza nello sviluppo di dashboard e report in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) con visualizzazioni avanzate.

Capacità di analisi dei dati e problem solving, con attenzione alla qualità e coerenza dei dati.

Orientamento al lavoro di squadra e capacità di collaborare con team multidisciplinari su progetti complessi.



Rappresentano un plus:



Passione per l’AI e la Generative AI, con interesse nell’applicarne le potenzialità a soluzioni innovative in ambito Data & Analytics

Conoscenza di SAP S/4HANA e SAP ECC per comprendere i processi aziendali

Esperienza con linguaggi di scripting o programmazione (SQL, Python o ABAP) e strumenti di data governance e qualità dei dati.



Scopri di più su di noi

Accenture è un’azienda globale di servizi professionali con capacità avanzate in campo digitale, cloud e security. Combinando un’esperienza unica e competenze specialistiche in più di 40 settori industriali, fornisce servizi in ambito Strategy & Consulting, Technology, Industry X, Operations e Accenture Song, sostenuta dalla più ampia rete di Advanced Technology e Intelligent Operations centers a livello mondiale. I nostri 801.000 talenti combinano ogni giorno tecnologia e ingegno umano, servendo clienti in oltre 120 Paesi.

Se desideri prepararti al meglio al nostro processo di selezione ti invitiamo a consultare la nostra sezione FAQ: https://www.accenture.com/it-it/careers/explore-careers/area-of-interest/journey-to-accenture

Policy

Qualora il tuo percorso di selezione andasse a buon fine, avrai la possibilità di aderire all’Accordo di smart working, ove compatibile con l’attività svolta, e lavorare da remoto secondo quanto previsto dalle policy aziendali. Per informazioni sulla nostra protezione della privacy art13 L.679/2016 (GDPR) vai su https://www.accenture.com/it-it/careers/privacy-policy

Accenture garantisce le pari opportunità.

Title: Data & Business Analyst
Description:
Proxima è un gruppo di aziende con skill funzionali e tecnologiche innovative, che mettono a fattor comune la propria esperienza in un’offerta congiunta di sviluppo di soluzioni all’interno della nostra Software Factory. Offriamo consulenza e servizi di Application Management e Quality Assurance.

Chi cerchiamo?

Data & Business Analyst per supportare e accelerare lo sviluppo di data foundation scalabili e di prodotti analitici.

Dove e come?

Italia, full-remote

Di cosa ti occuperai?


Lavorerai trasversalmente su più domini di business, contribuendo ad attività di data discovery, analisi dei processi aziendali, data harmonization e sviluppo di asset dati riutilizzabili.
Agirai come punto di raccordo tra business stakeholder, product team e data engineering team, trasformando fonti dati frammentate in basi dati affidabili, coerenti e scalabili a supporto di prodotti analytics.
Analizzerai e documenterai fonti dati, processi e soluzioni di reporting nei mercati europei, identificando disponibilità dati, gap, inconsistenze e requisiti di business.
Supporterai la valutazione e l’onboarding di nuove fonti dati, traducendo i processi di business e le esigenze degli stakeholder in requisiti funzionali, business rule, KPI e requisiti dati.
Contribuirai alla definizione e manutenzione di data asset riutilizzabili, business glossary, KPI definition, documentazione dati e concetti di business armonizzati.
Collaborerai con Product Manager, Data Engineer, Power BI Developer e team Regional Data Foundations per supportare attività di product discovery, data quality, standardizzazione e coerenza delle soluzioni analytics su più mercati e domini di business.


Hai queste competenze?


Almeno 3 anni di esperienza come Business Analyst, Functional Analyst, Data Analyst o ruoli simili;
esperienza nella gestione di dataset su larga scala e multi-market all’interno del settore FMCG/CPG, con capacità di trasformare dati commerciali in actionable business insights;
Esperienza in ambito Data & Analytics, Business Intelligence, Data Management o Digital Transformation;
Ottima conoscenza della lingua inglese;
Forte capacità di comprendere processi di business;
Esperienza con team cross-funzionali in contesti Agile;


Costituiscono Un Plus


Esperienza di data modeling;
Esperienza nel supporto a soluzioni di reporting e analytics;
Conoscenza di processi commerciali, sales, marketing, pricing o revenue management;
Esperienza con SQL;
Familiarità con Azure, Power BI e Databricks.


Sei la persona ideale se...

Sei la persona ideale se hai pensiero critico, attenzione al dettaglio e ottime capacità comunicative.

Cosa offriamo?

CCNL Commercio, range RAL 30/36.000€

BUONI PASTO (anche in modalità full remote)

ASSICURAZIONE SANITARIA CON COPERTURA INTEGRATIVA

I nostri benefit

PERCORSI DI CARRIERA

Abbiamo un articolato sistema di valutazione delle competenze e misurazione delle performance che consente al dipendente di crescere insieme a noi.

FORMAZIONE PROFESSIONALIZZANTE

Offriamo percorsi di formazione e di aggiornamento professionale con taglio tecnico/pratico su tematiche in ambito ICT.

SCONTI E PROMOZIONI RISERVATE

Abbiamo stabilito degli accordi con delle piattaforme per riservare ai nostri dipendenti scontistica e promozioni esclusive.

Il presente annuncio è rivolto a entrambi i sessi, ai sensi delle leggi 903/77 e 125/91, e a persone di tutte le età e tutte le nazionalità, ai sensi dei decreti legislativi 215/03 e 216/03.

Title: MTS - Data Analysis and Automated Reports Development
Description:
Are you ready to shape the future of capital markets? We are looking for a Data Analysis and Automated Reports Development specialist to join the MTS team. The position will be based in Rome, and start as soon as possible. You will report to the Market Surveillance Manager.

Starting annual gross salary - 35.600 euro

You Will Primarily Be Responsible For


Writing technical documentation for internal and external use
Maintaining web front-end pages for clients and internal management
Checking and analysing market data and generating periodic reports for management, sales and customers
Collaborating with development teams and product managers to implement software solutions
Developing new reports for market participants, issuers, universities and internal management
Ensuring data accuracy, integrity and security in all reporting activities


Your Profile


2 to 3 years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence or IT roles, ideally within financial markets or market infrastructure
Degree in engineering, computer science or a related field
Strong knowledge of Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Access, Power BI)
Basic knowledge of Microsoft Office VBA and .NET programming (Visual Studio)
Familiarity with Windows Server OS, Windows 11 client OS and SQL/SQL Server
Understanding of web server OS, JavaScript, PHP and FTP/SFTP server management
Excellent analytical mindset, attention to detail and strong problem-solving skills
Ability to write clear technical documentation and communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Team-oriented, proactive and able to manage multiple tasks simultaneously
Fluent in Italian and English


Join a Company Where Market Infrastructure Is Built On Innovation - And Where Every Innovation Starts With a Bold Mind Asking, “What’s Next?”. At Euronext, We Believe Change Is a Starting Point, Not An Obstacle. We Look For People Who See Growth Opportunities, Think Long Term And Want To Help Shape Capital Markets For Future Generations. Working With Us Means


Being part of a European, international and diverse team – Collaborate across borders with talented colleagues connected by a shared ambition and purpose.
Learning as you grow – Develop your skills through continuous learning, real responsibilities and flexible career paths that evolve with you.
Having an impact – Contribute to projects that matter, in an agile and dynamic environment where your ideas help drive progress.
Driving sustainability – Play an active role in building transparent, responsible and sustainable capital markets for the long term. Together, we do not just support markets - we help shape their future.


Apply by submitting a CV in English.

MTS (part of the Euronext Group) is a leading operator of regulated electronic platforms and market data services for the European fixed income market, supporting daily transaction volumes of more than €265 billion (2025). With a rich history spanning over three decades, MTS has established itself as a cornerstone of the fixed income trading community, known for its innovation, reliability and comprehensive service offerings.

Join Euronext – Shape capital markets for future generations Euronext is the leading European capital market covering the entire capital markets value chain, from listing, trading, clearing, settlement and custody, to solutions for issuers and investors. We operate seven national stock exchanges, handling 25% of European lit equity trading. Our products include equities, FX, ETFs, bonds, derivatives, commodities and indices. Euronext also provides clearing and settlement services through Euronext Clearing and our network of Euronext Securities CSDs. In addition, Euronext runs MTS, one of Europe’s leading electronic fixed income trading markets, and Nord Pool, the European power market. The company has a diverse domestic and international client base.

Find out more about us at: Euronext.com

We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate against individuals on the basis of race, gender, age, citizenship, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability, or any other legally protected factor. We value the unique talents of all our people, who come from diverse backgrounds with different personal experiences and points of view and we are committed to providing an environment of mutual respect.

Additional Information

This job description is only describing the main activities within a certain role and is not exhaustive. It does not prevent to add more tasks, projects.

  • Degree in engineering, computer science or a related field
  • Strong knowledge of Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Access, Power BI)
  • Basic knowledge of Microsoft Office VBA and .NET programming (Visual Studio)
  • Familiarity with Windows Server OS, Windows 11 client OS and SQL/SQL Server
  • Understanding of web server OS, JavaScript, PHP and FTP/SFTP server management
  • Excellent analytical mindset, attention to detail and strong problem-solving skills
  • Ability to write clear technical documentation and communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders
  • Team-oriented, proactive and able to manage multiple tasks simultaneously
  • Fluent in Italian and English

Join a Company Where Market Infrastructure Is Built On Innovation - And Where Every Innovation Starts With a Bold Mind Asking, “What’s Next?”. At Euronext, We Believe Change Is a Starting Point, Not An Obstacle. We Look For People Who See Growth Opportunities, Think Long Term And Want To Help Shape Capital Markets For Future Generations. Working With Us Means

  • Being part of a European, international and diverse team – Collaborate across borders with talented colleagues connected by a shared ambition and purpose.
  • Learning as you grow – Develop your skills through continuous learning, real responsibilities and flexible career paths that evolve with you.
  • Having an impact – Contribute to projects that matter, in an agile and dynamic environment where your ideas help drive progress.
  • Driving sustainability – Play an active role in building transparent, responsible and sustainable capital markets for the long term. Together, we do not just support markets - we help shape their future.

Apply by submitting a CV in English.

MTS (part of the Euronext Group) is a leading operator of regulated electronic platforms and market data services for the European fixed income market, supporting daily transaction volumes of more than €265 billion (2025). With a rich history spanning over three decades, MTS has established itself as a cornerstone of the fixed income trading community, known for its innovation, reliability and comprehensive service offerings.

Join Euronext – Shape capital markets for future generations Euronext is the leading European capital market covering the entire capital markets value chain, from listing, trading, clearing, settlement and custody, to solutions for issuers and investors. We operate seven national stock exchanges, handling 25% of European lit equity trading. Our products include equities, FX, ETFs, bonds, derivatives, commodities and indices. Euronext also provides clearing and settlement services through Euronext Clearing and our network of Euronext Securities CSDs. In addition, Euronext runs MTS, one of Europe’s leading electronic fixed income trading markets, and Nord Pool, the European power market. The company has a diverse domestic and international client base.

Find out more about us at: Euronext.com

Title: Capacity and Data Analyst

As part of the Thermo Fisher Scientific team, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life every single day to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our global teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while helping to take science a step beyond by developing solutions for some of the world’s toughest challenges, like protecting the environment, making sure our food is safe or helping find cures for cancer.

DESCRIPTION:

At Thermo Fisher Scientific, you'll support our mission to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer as a Data Analyst III. In this role, you'll transform complex data into actionable insights that drive strategic decision-making across the organization. You'll collaborate with cross-functional teams to develop and maintain scalable analytics solutions, create intuitive visualizations, and deliver critical reporting that enhances business performance. Using your expertise in data analysis and visualization tools, you'll identify trends, opportunities, and areas for optimization while ensuring data accuracy and integrity. You'll provide analytics guidance, proactively recommending solutions and improvements based on your findings. This role combines technical expertise with business acumen to deliver impactful insights that support our company's continued growth and success.

REQUIREMENTS:


Advanced Degree plus 3 years of experience, or Bachelor's Degree plus 5 years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or related role
Preferred Fields of Study: Data Analytics, Computer Science, Information Systems, Business Analytics, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics or related quantitative field
Relevant professional certifications (e.g., Power BI, SQL, data analytics) are a plus
Advanced proficiency in SQL, Power BI, and advanced Excel
Strong experience with data visualization, dashboard development, and report automation
Experience with ETL processes and data pipeline development
Knowledge of statistical analysis and data modeling techniques
Demonstrated ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights
Experience working with large datasets and multiple data sources
Strong project management skills and ability to manage multiple priorities
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Ability to collaborate effectively across functions and levels
Detail-oriented with strong problem-solving skills
Experience with Python, R, or other programming languages preferred
Knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure) and data warehouse concepts preferred
Understanding of data governance and quality management principles
Ability to work independently and as part of a team
Strong business acumen and customer focus
Experience in manufacturing, supply chain, or life sciences industry preferred
